basmala: In the name of God, the All-Merciful, the Compassionate.
intro: Al-Anʿām — “The Cattle” — is one of the great Meccan sūras, said to have come down all at once. It opens with praise of the Creator of the heavens, the earth, the darknesses and the light, and makes tawḥīd its thread throughout: a patient refutation of polytheism, an unfolding of the signs (āyāt) across the whole of creation, from split grains to the stars that guide through the night. At its heart stands the itinerary of Ibrāhīm (vv.74-79), who, in the setting of the star, the moon and the sun, learns to love none but the One who does not vanish. Here too are the keys of the unseen (mafātiḥ al-ghayb, v.59), which none possesses but Him, and the affirmation that no soul shall bear the burden of another. Qushayrī turns the opening basmala into light: by His Name hearts were illumined and stood upright; by His Name distresses dissolved and vanished. And from the “created of clay” (v.2) he draws this pearl: nobility lies in the joining, not in the origin; the origin is dust, the joining is nearness.
themes: tawḥīd and the refutation of associationism
Ibrāhīm's itinerary toward the One God
the keys of the unseen (mafātiḥ al-ghayb)
the individual responsibility of the soul
the light of guidance against the darknesses

versets.ar: الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ الَّذِي خَلَقَ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضَ وَجَعَلَ الظُّلُمَاتِ وَالنُّورَ ۖ ثُمَّ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا بِرَبِّهِمْ يَعْدِلُونَ
versets.tr: al-ḥamdu li-llāhi lladhī khalaqa s-samāwāti wa-l-arḍa wa-jaʿala ẓ-ẓulumāti wa-n-nūr thumma lladhīna kafarū bi-rabbihim yaʿdilūn
versets.trad: Praise be to God, who created the heavens and the earth and made the darknesses and the light! Yet those who disbelieve in their Lord set up equals to Him.
versets.commentaire: God begins by praising Himself, celebrating His own glory with an eternal eulogy. Qushayrī: “The One who” (alladhī) is allusion, “created the heavens and the earth” is expression; the secrets stood upright at the mere hearing of “the One who,” for they are realized through His existence and endure through His contemplation. He created the darkness of night and the brightness of day, but also the desolation of denial and the light of knowing; to some the darknesses of disobedience as trial, to others the light of gnosis as garden.

mots.def: ḥamd: praise, gratitude rendered to God.

mots.sens: He created the heavens and the earth
mots.def: khalaqa: to create out of nothing.

mots.racine: ظ ل م / ن و ر

mots.sens: they set up equals to Him
mots.def: ʿadala bi-: to place on the same rank, to equate.

versets.ar: هُوَ الَّذِي خَلَقَكُم مِّن طِينٍ ثُمَّ قَضَىٰ أَجَلًا ۖ وَأَجَلٌ مُّسَمًّى عِندَهُ ۖ ثُمَّ أَنتُمْ تَمْتَرُونَ
versets.tr: huwa lladhī khalaqakum min ṭīnin thumma qaḍā ajalan wa-ajalun musamman ʿindahu thumma antum tamtarūn
versets.trad: It is He who created you from clay, then decreed a term. And a term is named with Him. Yet you doubt.
versets.commentaire: He established the origin from clay and deposited in it the wonders of the journey, manifesting in it what He has shown to no other. Qushayrī: nobility lies in the joining, not in the origin; the joining is nearness, the origin is dust. Of the two terms: He set a term for trial and a term for grace; the term of trial is in this world, the term of grace in the next.

mots.racine: خ ل ق / ط ي ن
ق ض ي / أ ج ل

versets.ar: وَهُوَ اللَّهُ فِي السَّمَاوَاتِ وَفِي الْأَرْضِ ۖ يَعْلَمُ سِرَّكُمْ وَجَهْرَكُمْ وَيَعْلَمُ مَا تَكْسِبُونَ
versets.tr: wa-huwa llāhu fī s-samāwāti wa-fī l-arḍi yaʿlamu sirrakum wa-jahrakum wa-yaʿlamu mā taksibūn
versets.trad: He is God in the heavens and on the earth. He knows what you keep secret and what you make known, and He knows what you earn.
versets.commentaire: He is the Worshipped of all in heaven, the Sought of all on earth, the Existent before every sky and space, before every darkness and light, before every sun and moon, before every trace and vestige. His knowledge enfolds the secret of the heart as much as the spoken word: nothing of man escapes Him, neither the inner murmur nor the open deed.

mots.racine: س م و / أ ر ض
mots.sens: in the heavens and on the earth

mots.racine: س ر ر / ج ه ر
mots.sens: your secret and your open speech
mots.def: sirr: secret; jahr: what is uttered openly.

versets.ar: وَمَا تَأْتِيهِم مِّنْ آيَةٍ مِّنْ آيَاتِ رَبِّهِمْ إِلَّا كَانُوا عَنْهَا مُعْرِضِينَ
versets.tr: wa-mā taʾtīhim min āyatin min āyāti rabbihim illā kānū ʿanhā muʿriḍīn
versets.trad: Not a single sign reaches them among the signs of their Lord without their turning away from it.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: He grants them no unveiling and no gentleness but they meet it with denial and rejection; no approach but they receive it with aversion, no expansion but they greet with contraction. The sign is offered to them like an outstretched hand; they push it away.

mots.def: the manifest tokens of His Lordship.
aʿraḍa: to turn away, to disregard.

versets.ar: فَقَدْ كَذَّبُوا بِالْحَقِّ لَمَّا جَاءَهُمْ ۖ فَسَوْفَ يَأْتِيهِمْ أَنبَاءُ مَا كَانُوا بِهِ يَسْتَهْزِئُونَ
versets.tr: fa-qad kadhdhabū bi-l-ḥaqqi lammā jāʾahum fa-sawfa yaʾtīhim anbāʾu mā kānū bihi yastahziʾūn
versets.trad: They have denied the Truth when it came to them, but tidings shall come to them of that which they used to mock.
versets.commentaire: They persisted in opposition, swollen with pride; soon they shall taste the bitter recoil of their conduct and savor the fruits of their denial. Today's mockery will return to them tomorrow as an awaited tiding, but in the form of chastisement.

mots.racine: ك ذ ب / ح ق ق
mots.def: kadhdhaba: to treat as a lie.
nabaʾ (pl. anbāʾ): an announcement of weight.

versets.ar: أَلَمْ يَرَوْا كَمْ أَهْلَكْنَا مِن قَبْلِهِم مِّن قَرْنٍ مَّكَّنَّاهُمْ فِي الْأَرْضِ مَا لَمْ نُمَكِّن لَّكُمْ وَأَرْسَلْنَا السَّمَاءَ عَلَيْهِم مِّدْرَارًا وَجَعَلْنَا الْأَنْهَارَ تَجْرِي مِن تَحْتِهِمْ فَأَهْلَكْنَاهُم بِذُنُوبِهِمْ وَأَنشَأْنَا مِن بَعْدِهِمْ قَرْنًا آخَرِينَ
versets.tr: a-lam yaraw kam ahlaknā min qablihim min qarnin makkannāhum fī l-arḍi mā lam numakkin lakum wa-arsalnā s-samāʾa ʿalayhim midrāran wa-jaʿalnā l-anhāra tajrī min taḥtihim fa-ahlaknāhum bi-dhunūbihim wa-anshaʾnā min baʿdihim qarnan ākharīn
versets.trad: Do they not see how many generations We destroyed before them, whom We had established on the earth more firmly than We have established you? We sent the sky upon them in abundant rain, and We made rivers flow at their feet. Yet We destroyed them for their sins, and We raised up after them other generations.
themes: the abundance that did not save
versets.commentaire: Their predecessors enjoyed a firmer footing and a wider share of visible goods: We smoothed for them the paths of livelihood, opened wide the gates of prosperity. But when they had fixed their hearts on the mirages of hope and attained the object of their desire, We opened upon them the hidden folds of the Decree. Such is Our custom of requital upon Our enemies, as grace is Our custom toward Our friends.

mots.racine: ه ل ك / ق ر ن

mots.def: makkana: to make firm, to give power and standing.

versets.ar: وَلَوْ نَزَّلْنَا عَلَيْكَ كِتَابًا فِي قِرْطَاسٍ فَلَمَسُوهُ بِأَيْدِيهِمْ لَقَالَ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا إِنْ هَٰذَا إِلَّا سِحْرٌ مُّبِينٌ
versets.tr: wa-law nazzalnā ʿalayka kitāban fī qirṭāsin fa-lamasūhu bi-aydīhim la-qāla lladhīna kafarū in hādhā illā siḥrun mubīn
versets.trad: Had We sent down upon you a Book written on parchment, and had they touched it with their hands, those who disbelieve would still have said: “This is nothing but plain magic!”
versets.commentaire: God proclaims the perfection of His power in all that He wishes to manifest; but upon those for whom straying is decreed, even were You to show them every proof and open for them every way, they would only sink deeper into error and aversion. Of what use is the lamp to one who has lost his sight? So too the arguments are vain for one deprived of the eternal solicitude.

mots.racine: ك ت ب / ق ر ط س

mots.sens: they touched it with their hands

mots.racine: س ح ر / ب ي ن

versets.ar: وَقَالُوا لَوْلَا أُنزِلَ عَلَيْهِ مَلَكٌ ۖ وَلَوْ أَنزَلْنَا مَلَكًا لَّقُضِيَ الْأَمْرُ ثُمَّ لَا يُنظَرُونَ
versets.tr: wa-qālū law-lā unzila ʿalayhi malakun wa-law anzalnā malakan la-quḍiya l-amru thumma lā yunẓarūn
versets.trad: They say: “Why has an angel not been sent down upon him?” Yet had We sent down an angel, the matter would have been decreed, and they would be granted no respite.
versets.commentaire: He shows that what counts is the eternal apportionment, not the weight of proofs: of what use is the lamp to one who has lost his sight? likewise, of what use are arguments to one deprived of the eternal care? Sending an angel would only have hastened the verdict, with no further delay allowed.

mots.racine: ن ز ل / م ل ك
mots.sens: why an angel was not sent down upon him

mots.racine: ق ض ي / أ م ر

versets.ar: وَلَوْ جَعَلْنَاهُ مَلَكًا لَّجَعَلْنَاهُ رَجُلًا وَلَلَبَسْنَا عَلَيْهِم مَّا يَلْبِسُونَ
versets.tr: wa-law jaʿalnāhu malakan la-jaʿalnāhu rajulan wa-la-labasnā ʿalayhim mā yalbisūn
versets.trad: And had We made him an angel, We would have given him human form, and We would only have increased their confusion.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī, in a single stroke: whoever has not sanctified his secret finds his affair obscured. The very ambiguity these men demand by asking for an angel would fall back upon them: the angel itself, clothed in human form, would redouble their bewilderment. The veil comes not from the sign, but from the heart that has not purified itself.

mots.sens: We would have made him an angel
mots.def: jaʿala: to make, to set up.

mots.sens: We would have made him a man
We would have confounded what they confound
mots.def: labasa: to confuse, to render ambiguous.

versets.ar: وَلَقَدِ اسْتُهْزِئَ بِرُسُلٍ مِّن قَبْلِكَ فَحَاقَ بِالَّذِينَ سَخِرُوا مِنْهُم مَّا كَانُوا بِهِ يَسْتَهْزِئُونَ
versets.tr: wa-la-qadi stuhziʾa bi-rusulin min qablika fa-ḥāqa bi-lladhīna sakhirū minhum mā kānū bihi yastahziʾūn
versets.trad: Messengers before you were indeed mocked, but those who scoffed were encompassed by the very thing they mocked.
versets.commentaire: O Muḥammad, one who denied as you are denied came before you; so Our help was their due, and We took vengeance on those who defied them, until the evil consequence of their scheme returned to their own faces. Mockery closes upon the mocker like a trap sprung by his own hands.

mots.racine: ه ز أ / ر س ل
ح ي ق / س خ ر
mots.def: ḥāqa: to encircle, to fall back upon; sakhira: to mock.

versets.ar: قُلْ سِيرُوا فِي الْأَرْضِ ثُمَّ انظُرُوا كَيْفَ كَانَ عَاقِبَةُ الْمُكَذِّبِينَ
versets.tr: qul sīrū fī l-arḍi thumma nẓurū kayfa kāna ʿāqibatu l-mukadhdhibīn
versets.trad: Say: “Travel through the earth, then see what was the end of the deniers.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: Say: traverse the earth in its length and breadth, then look — has anyone ever escaped Our verdict? has any refuge been found outside Our command? The outward journey is but a sign of the inner journey: none eludes the Decree that enfolds every land.

mots.racine: ع ق ب / ك ذ ب
mots.def: ʿāqiba: outcome, end; mukadhdhib: one who denies.

versets.ar: قُل لِّمَن مَّا فِي السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضِ ۖ قُل لِّلَّهِ ۚ كَتَبَ عَلَىٰ نَفْسِهِ الرَّحْمَةَ ۚ لَيَجْمَعَنَّكُمْ إِلَىٰ يَوْمِ الْقِيَامَةِ لَا رَيْبَ فِيهِ ۚ الَّذِينَ خَسِرُوا أَنفُسَهُمْ فَهُمْ لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ
versets.tr: qul li-man mā fī s-samāwāti wa-l-arḍi qul li-llāhi kataba ʿalā nafsihi r-raḥmata la-yajmaʿannakum ilā yawmi l-qiyāmati lā rayba fīhi lladhīna khasirū anfusahum fa-hum lā yuʾminūn
versets.trad: Say: “To whom belongs what is in the heavens and on the earth?” Say: “To God!” He has prescribed mercy upon Himself. He will surely gather you to the Day of Resurrection, of which there is no doubt. Those who have lost their own souls — they do not believe!
versets.commentaire: Ask them: is there in the dwelling any dweller? has the cosmos, in truth, any weight before the Real? If they remain without answer, say: God suffices in His Lordship. And on “He has prescribed mercy upon Himself,” Qushayrī: He knew, decreed and willed according to His knowledge; whoever's salvation became attached to His knowing, His decree preceded him with the degrees He reserves for him.

mots.sens: to whom belongs what is in the heavens

mots.racine: ك ت ب / ر ح م
mots.def: kataba ʿalā nafsih: to impose upon Himself.

mots.racine: خ س ر / ن ف س

versets.ar: وَلَهُ مَا سَكَنَ فِي اللَّيْلِ وَالنَّهَارِ ۚ وَهُوَ السَّمِيعُ الْعَلِيمُ
versets.tr: wa-lahu mā sakana fī l-layli wa-n-nahāri wa-huwa s-samīʿu l-ʿalīm
versets.trad: To Him belongs all that reposes in the night and the day. He is the All-Hearing, the All-Knowing.
versets.commentaire: The originated beings belong to God in ownership, are manifest through Him, begin from Him and return to Him. Qushayrī: He is the All-Hearing of the moaning of those who yearn, the All-Knowing of the sighing of the ecstatics who have found Him. His hearing is not that of a distant judge, but the attentiveness of a Near One to the loving plaints of hearts.

mots.def: sakana: to dwell, to be at rest.

mots.racine: ل ي ل / ن ه ر
س م ع / ع ل م

versets.ar: قُلْ أَغَيْرَ اللَّهِ أَتَّخِذُ وَلِيًّا فَاطِرِ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضِ وَهُوَ يُطْعِمُ وَلَا يُطْعَمُ ۗ قُلْ إِنِّي أُمِرْتُ أَنْ أَكُونَ أَوَّلَ مَنْ أَسْلَمَ ۖ وَلَا تَكُونَنَّ مِنَ الْمُشْرِكِينَ
versets.tr: qul a-ghayra llāhi attakhidhu waliyyan fāṭiri s-samāwāti wa-l-arḍi wa-huwa yuṭʿimu wa-lā yuṭʿamu qul innī umirtu an akūna awwala man aslama wa-lā takūnanna mina l-mushrikīn
versets.trad: Say: “Shall I take as protector any other than God, the Creator of the heavens and the earth, who feeds and is not fed?” Say: “Yes, I have been commanded to be the first to submit; and be not among those who associate.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī makes the Prophet's heart speak: after He has honored me with His fair protection, shall I take another than Him as friend? after the light of His care has fallen upon me, shall I look toward anyone in the two worlds? That is impossible in conjecture and in thought. And on “He feeds and is not fed”: He has the attribute of generosity, so He feeds; He has the right of Priority, so He is not fed.

mots.racine: غ ي ر / و ل ي
mots.sens: shall I take a protector other than God
He feeds and is not fed

mots.def: aslama: to submit, to make islām.

versets.ar: قُلْ إِنِّي أَخَافُ إِنْ عَصَيْتُ رَبِّي عَذَابَ يَوْمٍ عَظِيمٍ
versets.tr: qul innī akhāfu in ʿaṣaytu rabbī ʿadhāba yawmin ʿaẓīm
versets.trad: Say: “Truly I fear, if I disobey my Lord, the chastisement of a tremendous Day.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: I am made certain of my own powerlessness, fearful of my Lord's chastisement, and shaped by obedience to His command. The prophet's fear is no servile dread: it is the lucidity of one who knows himself to be nothing before the majesty, and who draws from it perfect docility.

mots.racine: ع ذ ب / ع ظ م
mots.sens: the chastisement of a tremendous Day

versets.ar: مَّن يُصْرَفْ عَنْهُ يَوْمَئِذٍ فَقَدْ رَحِمَهُ ۚ وَذَٰلِكَ الْفَوْزُ الْمُبِينُ
versets.tr: man yuṣraf ʿanhu yawmaʾidhin fa-qad raḥimahu wa-dhālika l-fawzu l-mubīn
versets.trad: Whoever is spared on that Day, He has shown him mercy. That is the manifest triumph.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī, terse: whoever is reached by the eternal solicitude has the coming punishment turned away from him. To be spared on the supreme Day is not the fruit of a merit wrenched out, but the overflow of a mercy that preceded every work.

mots.def: ṣarafa: to turn away, to avert.

mots.racine: ف و ز / ب ي ن

versets.ar: وَإِن يَمْسَسْكَ اللَّهُ بِضُرٍّ فَلَا كَاشِفَ لَهُ إِلَّا هُوَ ۖ وَإِن يَمْسَسْكَ بِخَيْرٍ فَهُوَ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ
versets.tr: wa-in yamsaska llāhu bi-ḍurrin fa-lā kāshifa lahu illā huwa wa-in yamsaska bi-khayrin fa-huwa ʿalā kulli shayʾin qadīr
versets.trad: If God touches you with harm, none can remove it but He; and if He touches you with good, He is powerful over all things.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: it is He who saves you from trial, and He who casts you into affliction. Since One alone holds the power to bring into being, all others are but His acts: none but He has the dignity of creating. To see a second hand behind evil or good is still a form of hidden association.

mots.racine: م س س / ض ر ر
mots.def: massa: to touch, to afflict; ḍurr: harm.

versets.ar: وَهُوَ الْقَاهِرُ فَوْقَ عِبَادِهِ ۚ وَهُوَ الْحَكِيمُ الْخَبِيرُ
versets.tr: wa-huwa l-qāhiru fawqa ʿibādihi wa-huwa l-ḥakīmu l-khabīr
versets.trad: He is the Irresistible, high above His servants, and He is the Wise, the All-Aware.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the rank of Oneness rises above the attribute of humanity: this One has never ceased to be, that one was not and was brought into being. And when the dominion of tawḥīd blazes forth, what subsistence remains to contingency? The created fades before the splendor of the One as the star pales at sunrise.

mots.racine: ف و ق / ع ب د
ح ك م / خ ب ر
mots.def: ḥakīm: wise; khabīr: who knows the depth of things.

versets.ar: قُلْ أَيُّ شَيْءٍ أَكْبَرُ شَهَادَةً ۖ قُلِ اللَّهُ ۖ شَهِيدٌ بَيْنِي وَبَيْنَكُمْ ۚ وَأُوحِيَ إِلَيَّ هَٰذَا الْقُرْآنُ لِأُنذِرَكُم بِهِ وَمَن بَلَغَ ۚ أَئِنَّكُمْ لَتَشْهَدُونَ أَنَّ مَعَ اللَّهِ آلِهَةً أُخْرَىٰ ۚ قُل لَّا أَشْهَدُ ۚ قُلْ إِنَّمَا هُوَ إِلَٰهٌ وَاحِدٌ وَإِنَّنِي بَرِيءٌ مِّمَّا تُشْرِكُونَ
versets.tr: qul ayyu shayʾin akbaru shahādatan quli llāhu shahīdun baynī wa-baynakum wa-ūḥiya ilayya hādhā l-qurʾānu li-undhirakum bihi wa-man balagha a-innakum la-tashhadūna anna maʿa llāhi ālihatan ukhrā qul lā ashhadu qul innamā huwa ilāhun wāḥidun wa-innanī barīʾun mimmā tushrikūn
versets.trad: Say: “What thing is greatest in testimony?” Say: “God is witness between me and you. This Qurʾān has been revealed to me that I may warn you by it, and whomever it reaches. Do you indeed testify that there are other gods alongside God?” Say: “I do not testify.” Say: “He is but one God, and I am quit of what you associate.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the testimony of the Real outweighs every testimony; when they themselves claim to bear witness, their sciences do not encompass the reality of things, whereas nothing escapes the Real. Then God informs His Prophet that he is sent to all mankind and to all who will arise until the Day of Resurrection.

mots.racine: ك ب ر / ش ه د
ب ر أ / ش ر ك
mots.def: barīʾ: disavowed, clear of; ashraka: to associate.

versets.ar: الَّذِينَ آتَيْنَاهُمُ الْكِتَابَ يَعْرِفُونَهُ كَمَا يَعْرِفُونَ أَبْنَاءَهُمُ ۘ الَّذِينَ خَسِرُوا أَنفُسَهُمْ فَهُمْ لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ
versets.tr: alladhīna ātaynāhumu l-kitāba yaʿrifūnahu kamā yaʿrifūna abnāʾahumu lladhīna khasirū anfusahum fa-hum lā yuʾminūn
versets.trad: Those to whom We gave the Book recognize it as they recognize their own children. But those who have lost their own souls — they do not believe.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: their knowledge enfolded the truthfulness of the Chosen One in his prophethood; but the eternal wretchedness overtook them and bound their tongues from acknowledging it: they denied it openly while knowing its truth in the secret of their hearts. The worst blindness is not ignorance, but the denial of what one knows.

versets.ar: وَمَنْ أَظْلَمُ مِمَّنِ افْتَرَىٰ عَلَى اللَّهِ كَذِبًا أَوْ كَذَّبَ بِآيَاتِهِ ۗ إِنَّهُ لَا يُفْلِحُ الظَّالِمُونَ
versets.tr: wa-man aẓlamu mimmani ftarā ʿalā llāhi kadhiban aw kadhdhaba bi-āyātihi innahu lā yufliḥu ẓ-ẓālimūn
versets.trad: Who is more unjust than one who forges a lie against God or denies His signs? Truly, the unjust shall not prosper.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the misfortune of abandonment drove them, by its sting, to persist in lying against God; then they had no shame before His gaze and no fear of His chastisement. To invent against God and deny His signs is the height of injustice — not against Him, who remains untouched, but against the soul that ruins itself.

mots.racine: ف ر ي / ك ذ ب

versets.ar: وَيَوْمَ نَحْشُرُهُمْ جَمِيعًا ثُمَّ نَقُولُ لِلَّذِينَ أَشْرَكُوا أَيْنَ شُرَكَاؤُكُمُ الَّذِينَ كُنتُمْ تَزْعُمُونَ
versets.tr: wa-yawma naḥshuruhum jamīʿan thumma naqūlu li-lladhīna ashrakū ayna shurakāʾukumu lladhīna kuntum tazʿumūn
versets.trad: On the Day We gather them all, then say to those who associated: “Where are those you claimed to be God's associates?”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: He gathers them for the day of mustering and resurrection, but He separates them in the verdict and the decree: the raising brings them together, the judgment divides them. All reappear together; each, however, rejoins his own destiny, and the invoked associates vanish.

versets.ar: ثُمَّ لَمْ تَكُن فِتْنَتُهُمْ إِلَّا أَن قَالُوا وَاللَّهِ رَبِّنَا مَا كُنَّا مُشْرِكِينَ
versets.tr: thumma lam takun fitnatuhum illā an qālū wa-llāhi rabbinā mā kunnā mushrikīn
versets.trad: Then their only plea will be to say: “By God, our Lord, we were not associators!”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: here is the height of their obstinacy: they deny before God the very thing they had been guilty of and sworn upon. Had they any knowledge of God, they would have known that He knows their secret and their whisper, that nothing of their beginning or their end escapes Him; but the ignorance that masters them makes them say what disgraces them.

mots.def: mushrik: one who associates with God.

versets.ar: انظُرْ كَيْفَ كَذَبُوا عَلَىٰ أَنفُسِهِمْ ۚ وَضَلَّ عَنْهُم مَّا كَانُوا يَفْتَرُونَ
versets.tr: unẓur kayfa kadhabū ʿalā anfusihim wa-ḍalla ʿanhum mā kānū yaftarūn
versets.trad: See how they lied against themselves, and how what they invented forsook them!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: this is a word of astonishment: their story holds what would confound the likes of you. The lie they had forged abandons them at the moment they would have needed it; it vanishes like a shadow, and they find themselves lying against their own selves.

mots.def: ḍalla: to be lost, to vanish.

versets.ar: وَمِنْهُم مَّن يَسْتَمِعُ إِلَيْكَ ۖ وَجَعَلْنَا عَلَىٰ قُلُوبِهِمْ أَكِنَّةً أَن يَفْقَهُوهُ وَفِي آذَانِهِمْ وَقْرًا ۚ وَإِن يَرَوْا كُلَّ آيَةٍ لَّا يُؤْمِنُوا بِهَا ۚ حَتَّىٰ إِذَا جَاءُوكَ يُجَادِلُونَكَ يَقُولُ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا إِنْ هَٰذَا إِلَّا أَسَاطِيرُ الْأَوَّلِينَ
versets.tr: wa-minhum man yastamiʿu ilayka wa-jaʿalnā ʿalā qulūbihim akinnatan an yafqahūhu wa-fī ādhānihim waqran wa-in yaraw kulla āyatin lā yuʾminū bihā ḥattā idhā jāʾūka yujādilūnaka yaqūlu lladhīna kafarū in hādhā illā asāṭīru l-awwalīn
versets.trad: Among them are some who listen to you, but We have placed veils over their hearts so they understand it not, and in their ears a deafness. Were they to see every sign, they would not believe in it; so that when they come to dispute with you, those who disbelieve say: “These are nothing but tales of the ancients!”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: true listening is the listening of acceptance, and it issues from the eye of certainty; as for outward listening, it is of no account. Whomever the Real has tried with a sealed heart and a veil of confusion over his insight, this only adds aversion to his aversion. Whoever is set at a distance by the eternal apportionment, no stratagem brings near.

mots.racine: س ط ر / أ و ل

versets.ar: وَهُمْ يَنْهَوْنَ عَنْهُ وَيَنْأَوْنَ عَنْهُ ۖ وَإِن يُهْلِكُونَ إِلَّا أَنفُسَهُمْ وَمَا يَشْعُرُونَ
versets.tr: wa-hum yanhawna ʿanhu wa-yanʾawna ʿanhu wa-in yuhlikūna illā anfusahum wa-mā yashʿurūn
versets.trad: They forbid others from it and themselves withdraw from it; yet they destroy none but themselves, without realizing.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: in this verse there is a fearsome sign for one who summons to the Real aloud, yet does not apply it to the secret of himself. Their states contradicted their words; they cast the rope upon their own necks, and whomever the apportionment has set far off, his own act does not bring near. To turn others away and to turn away oneself: a double ruin they do not perceive.

mots.def: naʾā: to draw away, to keep far.

versets.ar: وَلَوْ تَرَىٰ إِذْ وُقِفُوا عَلَى النَّارِ فَقَالُوا يَا لَيْتَنَا نُرَدُّ وَلَا نُكَذِّبَ بِآيَاتِ رَبِّنَا وَنَكُونَ مِنَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ
versets.tr: wa-law tarā idh wuqifū ʿalā n-nāri fa-qālū yā laytanā nuraddu wa-lā nukadhdhiba bi-āyāti rabbinā wa-nakūna mina l-muʾminīn
versets.trad: If only you could see when they are made to stand before the Fire! They will say: “Ah, would that we might be sent back! Then we would not deny the signs of our Lord, and we would be among the believers!”
versets.commentaire: When they are set at the brink of the Fire, the veil falls and regret tears at them. But this wish to return is itself a lie, as the next verse will say: the corruption of their depths would remain. Repentance at the threshold of chastisement is no longer repentance but terror — and terror does not convert the heart.

mots.racine: و ق ف / ن و ر
mots.sens: they are made to stand before the Fire
mots.def: wuqifa: to be set, brought to a halt.

mots.sens: ah, would that we were sent back
mots.def: layta: an unattainable wish; radda: to send back.

versets.ar: بَلْ بَدَا لَهُم مَّا كَانُوا يُخْفُونَ مِن قَبْلُ ۖ وَلَوْ رُدُّوا لَعَادُوا لِمَا نُهُوا عَنْهُ وَإِنَّهُمْ لَكَاذِبُونَ
versets.tr: bal badā lahum mā kānū yukhfūna min qablu wa-law ruddū la-ʿādū li-mā nuhū ʿanhu wa-innahum la-kādhibūn
versets.trad: Nay, what they used to conceal before will appear plainly to them. But even were they sent back, they would return to what they were forbidden. They are indeed liars!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī unfolds here his meditation on the veils: tomorrow, the day the veils are torn and the secrets appear — how many a man draped in the cloak of piety, whose intimates swore that he was detached from the world and enamored of his Master, and whose case will be revealed quite otherwise; and how many a man with his veil torn in people's eyes, taken for dissolute, whose hidden jewel the clear-sighted will then see appear. And were they sent back, they would return to their denial: for the depth, not the occasion, decides.

mots.racine: ع و د / ن ه ي
mots.sens: they would return to the forbidden

versets.ar: وَقَالُوا إِنْ هِيَ إِلَّا حَيَاتُنَا الدُّنْيَا وَمَا نَحْنُ بِمَبْعُوثِينَ
versets.tr: wa-qālū in hiya illā ḥayātunā d-dunyā wa-mā naḥnu bi-mabʿūthīn
versets.trad: And they say: “There is nothing but our life in this world, and we shall not be raised!”
versets.commentaire: They enclose existence within the horizon of the immediate world and deny the raising. For Qushayrī this is the blindness of one who perceives only the apparent: the veil of the sensible masks the horizon of the unseen, and they take the passage for the dwelling, the shadow for the light.

mots.racine: ح ي ي / د ن و
mots.sens: there is nothing but our life in this world

mots.def: baʿatha: to raise, to bring forth.

versets.ar: وَلَوْ تَرَىٰ إِذْ وُقِفُوا عَلَىٰ رَبِّهِمْ ۚ قَالَ أَلَيْسَ هَٰذَا بِالْحَقِّ ۚ قَالُوا بَلَىٰ وَرَبِّنَا ۚ قَالَ فَذُوقُوا الْعَذَابَ بِمَا كُنتُمْ تَكْفُرُونَ
versets.tr: wa-law tarā idh wuqifū ʿalā rabbihim qāla a-laysa hādhā bi-l-ḥaqqi qālū balā wa-rabbinā qāla fa-dhūqū l-ʿadhāba bi-mā kuntum takfurūn
versets.trad: If only you could see when they are made to stand before their Lord. He will say: “Is this not the Truth?” They will say: “Yes indeed, by our Lord!” He will say: “Then taste the chastisement for what you used to deny!”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: O regret upon them, at the station of confusion, at the place where one undergoes trembling and remembers the shortfall of works! They stand on the feet of remorse, gnashing the teeth of repentance, when no repentance avails them, no complaint is heard, no mercy descends. Summoned: “Is this not the Truth?”, they confess it against their will.

mots.sens: they are made to stand before their Lord

mots.racine: ذ و ق / ع ذ ب

versets.ar: قَدْ خَسِرَ الَّذِينَ كَذَّبُوا بِلِقَاءِ اللَّهِ ۖ حَتَّىٰ إِذَا جَاءَتْهُمُ السَّاعَةُ بَغْتَةً قَالُوا يَا حَسْرَتَنَا عَلَىٰ مَا فَرَّطْنَا فِيهَا وَهُمْ يَحْمِلُونَ أَوْزَارَهُمْ عَلَىٰ ظُهُورِهِمْ ۚ أَلَا سَاءَ مَا يَزِرُونَ
versets.tr: qad khasira lladhīna kadhdhabū bi-liqāʾi llāhi ḥattā idhā jāʾat'humu s-sāʿatu baghtatan qālū yā ḥasratanā ʿalā mā farraṭnā fīhā wa-hum yaḥmilūna awzārahum ʿalā ẓuhūrihim a-lā sāʾa mā yazirūn
versets.trad: Lost indeed are those who deny the meeting with God, until, when the Hour comes upon them suddenly, they cry: “Alas for us, for how we neglected it!” They bear their burdens on their backs. How evil is what they bear!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: what a loss, and what a loss! They lost not wealth, nor rank, nor state, but — as it has been said — the separation from the One in whose remembrance one has spent one's life. To deny the liqāʾ, the meeting with God, is to forfeit the one gain; and whoever has not known the majesty of that meeting, how should he grieve at having missed it?

mots.racine: خ س ر / ل ق ي
mots.sens: lost by denying the meeting with God

mots.racine: س و ع / ب غ ت

versets.ar: وَمَا الْحَيَاةُ الدُّنْيَا إِلَّا لَعِبٌ وَلَهْوٌ ۖ وَلَلدَّارُ الْآخِرَةُ خَيْرٌ لِّلَّذِينَ يَتَّقُونَ ۗ أَفَلَا تَعْقِلُونَ
versets.tr: wa-mā l-ḥayātu d-dunyā illā laʿibun wa-lahwun wa-la-d-dāru l-ākhiratu khayrun li-lladhīna yattaqūna a-fa-lā taʿqilūn
versets.trad: The life of this world is nothing but play and diversion. But the abode of the hereafter is better for those who are godfearing. Will you not reason?
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī cuts to the heart: everything from which the ego draws its share today belongs to the world; and what belongs to the world inevitably distances you from your Master. What distracts you from the Real — its nearness is not blessed. Play (laʿib) and diversion (lahw) do not mean visible frivolity, but all that holds the heart far from God.

mots.racine: ل ع ب / ل ه و
mots.def: laʿib: play; lahw: distraction that diverts.

mots.racine: د و ر / أ خ ر

versets.ar: قَدْ نَعْلَمُ إِنَّهُ لَيَحْزُنُكَ الَّذِي يَقُولُونَ ۖ فَإِنَّهُمْ لَا يُكَذِّبُونَكَ وَلَٰكِنَّ الظَّالِمِينَ بِآيَاتِ اللَّهِ يَجْحَدُونَ
versets.tr: qad naʿlamu innahu la-yaḥzunuka lladhī yaqūlūna fa-innahum lā yukadhdhibūnaka wa-lākinna ẓ-ẓālimīna bi-āyāti llāhi yajḥadūn
versets.trad: We know that what they say grieves you. In truth it is not you they deny, but it is the signs of God that the unjust reject.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī reads here a consolation for the Messenger: We know what they say of you; and they say it only because of Us and for Us. You were great in dignity among them — they called you Muḥammad the Trustworthy — before We set upon you this mark; if what reaches you reaches you, it is for Our word, and that is not lost with Us. The offense done to the messenger goes back to the One who sends him.

mots.sens: it is not you they deny
they reject the signs of God

versets.ar: وَلَقَدْ كُذِّبَتْ رُسُلٌ مِّن قَبْلِكَ فَصَبَرُوا عَلَىٰ مَا كُذِّبُوا وَأُوذُوا حَتَّىٰ أَتَاهُمْ نَصْرُنَا ۚ وَلَا مُبَدِّلَ لِكَلِمَاتِ اللَّهِ ۚ وَلَقَدْ جَاءَكَ مِن نَّبَإِ الْمُرْسَلِينَ
versets.tr: wa-la-qad kudhdhibat rusulun min qablika fa-ṣabarū ʿalā mā kudhdhibū wa-ūdhū ḥattā atāhum naṣrunā wa-lā mubaddila li-kalimāti llāhi wa-la-qad jāʾaka min nabaʾi l-mursalīn
versets.trad: Messengers before you were already denied; yet they bore patiently the denial and the harm until Our help came to them. There is no changing the words of God. And there has already come to you some account of the messengers.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: whoever takes Our path bears patiently what reaches him for Our sake; his trade with Us is never lost, his state never hidden from Us. Those who faced Our decree, having known Us, faced it only with their very lives, and bore what they met for Us only upon the pupils of their eyes. The patience (ṣabr) of the prophets is the seal of their love.

mots.racine: ك ذ ب / ر س ل
ب د ل / ك ل م
mots.sens: none can change the words of God
mots.def: baddala: to alter; kalimāt: decrees, words.

versets.ar: وَإِن كَانَ كَبُرَ عَلَيْكَ إِعْرَاضُهُمْ فَإِنِ اسْتَطَعْتَ أَن تَبْتَغِيَ نَفَقًا فِي الْأَرْضِ أَوْ سُلَّمًا فِي السَّمَاءِ فَتَأْتِيَهُم بِآيَةٍ ۚ وَلَوْ شَاءَ اللَّهُ لَجَمَعَهُمْ عَلَى الْهُدَىٰ ۚ فَلَا تَكُونَنَّ مِنَ الْجَاهِلِينَ
versets.tr: wa-in kāna kabura ʿalayka iʿrāḍuhum fa-ini staṭaʿta an tabtaghiya nafaqan fī l-arḍi aw sullaman fī s-samāʾi fa-taʾtiyahum bi-āyatin wa-law shāʾa llāhu la-jamaʿahum ʿalā l-hudā fa-lā takūnanna mina l-jāhilīn
versets.trad: If their aversion is hard upon you, then, if you can seek out a tunnel into the earth or a ladder into the sky to bring them a sign, do so. Had God willed, He would have gathered them all upon guidance. So be not among the ignorant!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: through the excess of his tenderness, he exhausted himself in seeking God's mercy for them, and bore upon his noble heart, knowing the wretchedness of their state, every kind of sorrow. So God taught him that they were kept far from nearness, struck by the ancient apportionment. Had He willed to guide them, they would have found a resting place in their breasts; but whoever is overthrown by Omnipotence, no stratagem raises him.

mots.racine: ك ب ر / ع ر ض
mots.sens: their aversion is hard upon you
mots.def: kabura: to become heavy; iʿrāḍ: turning away.

mots.racine: ج م ع / ه د ي
mots.sens: He would gather them upon guidance

versets.ar: إِنَّمَا يَسْتَجِيبُ الَّذِينَ يَسْمَعُونَ ۘ وَالْمَوْتَىٰ يَبْعَثُهُمُ اللَّهُ ثُمَّ إِلَيْهِ يُرْجَعُونَ
versets.tr: innamā yastajību lladhīna yasmaʿūna wa-l-mawtā yabʿathuhumu llāhu thumma ilayhi yurjaʿūn
versets.trad: Only those who hear can respond. As for the dead, God will raise them, then to Him they shall return.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: whoever has lost hearing in his secret depths is denied, in his outward self, the gift of following; and the eternal choice inscribed in His knowledge prevails. The “dead” are first the extinguished hearts, deaf to the call; God alone can revive them, as He will raise the bodies.

mots.racine: م و ت / ب ع ث

versets.ar: وَقَالُوا لَوْلَا نُزِّلَ عَلَيْهِ آيَةٌ مِّن رَّبِّهِ ۚ قُلْ إِنَّ اللَّهَ قَادِرٌ عَلَىٰ أَن يُنَزِّلَ آيَةً وَلَٰكِنَّ أَكْثَرَهُمْ لَا يَعْلَمُونَ
versets.tr: wa-qālū law-lā nuzzila ʿalayhi āyatun min rabbihi qul inna llāha qādirun ʿalā an yunazzila āyatan wa-lākinna aktharahum lā yaʿlamūn
versets.trad: And they say: “Why has a sign not been sent down upon him from his Lord?” Say: “God is able to send down a sign, but most of them do not know.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: they asked for more in the way of marvels, while there had come of that what would slaughter every pretext; and they did not know that it is God who held them back. Were it not for what veiled their insights, they would not have imagined that they lacked proofs. The sign is abundant; it is the inner eye that is lacking.

mots.sens: why a sign was not sent down upon him

mots.racine: ك ث ر / ع ل م
mots.sens: most of them do not know

versets.ar: وَمَا مِن دَابَّةٍ فِي الْأَرْضِ وَلَا طَائِرٍ يَطِيرُ بِجَنَاحَيْهِ إِلَّا أُمَمٌ أَمْثَالُكُم ۚ مَّا فَرَّطْنَا فِي الْكِتَابِ مِن شَيْءٍ ۚ ثُمَّ إِلَىٰ رَبِّهِمْ يُحْشَرُونَ
versets.tr: wa-mā min dābbatin fī l-arḍi wa-lā ṭāʾirin yaṭīru bi-janāḥayhi illā umamun amthālukum mā farraṭnā fī l-kitābi min shayʾin thumma ilā rabbihim yuḥsharūn
versets.trad: There is no creature on the earth, no bird flying on its two wings, but they are communities like you. We have neglected nothing in the Book. Then to their Lord they shall be gathered.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: creatures are equal, the works alike in their need of the One who produces them, in the instant of their surging forth as in their subsistence; no thing, no trace, no vestige, but it is a witness to His oneness and a manifest proof of its own created condition. The least living thing bears the signature of the One.

mots.racine: ط ي ر / ج ن ح
mots.sens: a bird flying on its two wings

mots.racine: أ م م / م ث ل

mots.sens: We have neglected nothing in the Book

versets.ar: وَالَّذِينَ كَذَّبُوا بِآيَاتِنَا صُمٌّ وَبُكْمٌ فِي الظُّلُمَاتِ ۗ مَن يَشَإِ اللَّهُ يُضْلِلْهُ وَمَن يَشَأْ يَجْعَلْهُ عَلَىٰ صِرَاطٍ مُّسْتَقِيمٍ
versets.tr: wa-lladhīna kadhdhabū bi-āyātinā ṣummun wa-bukmun fī ẓ-ẓulumāti man yashaʾi llāhu yuḍlilhu wa-man yashaʾ yajʿalhu ʿalā ṣirāṭin mustaqīm
versets.trad: Those who deny Our signs are deaf and dumb, in the darknesses. God leads astray whom He wills and sets whom He wills upon a straight path.
themes: the will that guides or leads astray
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: those whom the eternal solicitude has missed — deprivation has stopped their ears and abandonment has covered their gaze. The will is not contradicted, the decree is not disputed, and the Real in every state remains sovereign. Deafness and blindness are not infirmities of the senses, but the veil cast over the heart.

mots.racine: ص م م / ب ك م
ض ل ل / ص ر ط
mots.sens: leads astray / sets on the path

versets.ar: قُلْ أَرَأَيْتَكُمْ إِنْ أَتَاكُمْ عَذَابُ اللَّهِ أَوْ أَتَتْكُمُ السَّاعَةُ أَغَيْرَ اللَّهِ تَدْعُونَ إِن كُنتُمْ صَادِقِينَ
versets.tr: qul a-raʾaytakum in atākum ʿadhābu llāhi aw atatkumu s-sāʿatu a-ghayra llāhi tadʿūna in kuntum ṣādiqīn
versets.trad: Say: “What think you? If God's chastisement comes to you, or the Hour comes to you, will you call upon any other than God? Be then truthful!”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: when harm touches you and an affair overwhelms you, from whom do you hope for deliverance? whose gentleness do you call upon? a creature of the East or of the West? an angel of the sky or a servant of the earth? In extreme distress the mask falls and the heart invokes none but the One.

mots.racine: أ ت ي / ع ذ ب

mots.sens: will you call upon other than God

versets.ar: بَلْ إِيَّاهُ تَدْعُونَ فَيَكْشِفُ مَا تَدْعُونَ إِلَيْهِ إِن شَاءَ وَتَنسَوْنَ مَا تُشْرِكُونَ
versets.tr: bal iyyāhu tadʿūna fa-yakshifu mā tadʿūna ilayhi in shāʾa wa-tansawna mā tushrikūn
versets.trad: Nay, it is upon Him that you call, and He will remove, if He wills, that for which you called upon Him, and you will forget what you associated.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: if you abase yourselves through your souls or ponder long in your hearts, you will find none but Him, no refuge outside His decree; you will return to Him for the removal of harm and the gentle gift of good. When you have tried everything, tasted the sweet and the bitter, affliction will lead you to His door; and when you return, broken, marked with humility and need, He does what He wills.

mots.sens: nay, it is upon Him you call

mots.def: kashafa: to lift, to take away.

mots.racine: ن س ي / ش ر ك

versets.ar: وَلَقَدْ أَرْسَلْنَا إِلَىٰ أُمَمٍ مِّن قَبْلِكَ فَأَخَذْنَاهُم بِالْبَأْسَاءِ وَالضَّرَّاءِ لَعَلَّهُمْ يَتَضَرَّعُونَ
versets.tr: wa-la-qad arsalnā ilā umamin min qablika fa-akhadhnāhum bi-l-baʾsāʾi wa-ḍ-ḍarrāʾi laʿallahum yataḍarraʿūn
versets.trad: We had already sent messengers to communities before you, then We seized them with distress and adversity, that they might humble themselves.
themes: the trial that calls to humility
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: He informs of His ancient custom in the governance of nations: what He bestowed of graces and bounties upon those who obeyed Him, and what He brought down of pains and scourges upon those who opposed. Distress (baʾsāʾ) and adversity (ḍarrāʾ) are a pedagogy: they break pride so that imploring (taḍarruʿ) may spring forth.

mots.racine: ر س ل / أ م م
ب أ س / ض ر ر

mots.def: taḍarraʿa: to grow humble, to beseech.

versets.ar: فَلَوْلَا إِذْ جَاءَهُم بَأْسُنَا تَضَرَّعُوا وَلَٰكِن قَسَتْ قُلُوبُهُمْ وَزَيَّنَ لَهُمُ الشَّيْطَانُ مَا كَانُوا يَعْمَلُونَ
versets.tr: fa-law-lā idh jāʾahum baʾsunā taḍarraʿū wa-lākin qasat qulūbuhum wa-zayyana lahumu sh-shayṭānu mā kānū yaʿmalūn
versets.trad: Why then, when Our might came upon them, did they not humble themselves? Rather their hearts hardened, and Satan made fair to them what they were doing.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: when the trial cast its shadow over them, had they returned with fair imploring and sweet supplication, We would have lifted from them the trials and poured upon them Our favors; but abandonment turned them from the hereafter, and they persisted in their revolt: their hearts hardened and the causes of their wretchedness multiplied.

mots.sens: why did they not humble themselves

mots.def: zayyana: to adorn, to make attractive.

versets.ar: فَلَمَّا نَسُوا مَا ذُكِّرُوا بِهِ فَتَحْنَا عَلَيْهِمْ أَبْوَابَ كُلِّ شَيْءٍ حَتَّىٰ إِذَا فَرِحُوا بِمَا أُوتُوا أَخَذْنَاهُم بَغْتَةً فَإِذَا هُم مُّبْلِسُونَ
versets.tr: fa-lammā nasū mā dhukkirū bihi fataḥnā ʿalayhim abwāba kulli shayʾin ḥattā idhā fariḥū bi-mā ūtū akhadhnāhum baghtatan fa-idhā hum mublisūn
versets.trad: Then when they forgot what they had been reminded of, We opened upon them the gates of all things, until, just as they rejoiced in what they were given, We seized them suddenly, and they were plunged into despair.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī here uncovers the hidden ruse (istidrāj): when their absence from the Presence had grown long and Our admonitions no longer reached them, We made easy for them the paths of ease, poured upon them the downpours of blessings, opened the gates of opulence; and when hope had settled well in their hearts, We seized them suddenly. Abundance granted to the heedless is no favor, but a trap that springs shut.

mots.racine: ن س ي / ذ ك ر
mots.sens: they forgot what they had been reminded

mots.racine: ف ت ح / ب و ب
mots.sens: We opened the gates of all things

mots.def: ablasa: to be struck dumb with despair.

versets.ar: فَقُطِعَ دَابِرُ الْقَوْمِ الَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا ۚ وَالْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ رَبِّ الْعَالَمِينَ
versets.tr: fa-quṭiʿa dābiru l-qawmi lladhīna ẓalamū wa-l-ḥamdu li-llāhi rabbi l-ʿālamīn
versets.trad: So the last remnant of the people who did wrong was cut off. Praise be to God, Lord of the worlds!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: they tarried but a little, until no trace nor vestige remained of them, no tale nor tiding. And God — by the attribute of glory and the right of majesty — feels no loneliness at their vanishing, nor any comfort at their existence. Praise closes the account of chastisement: for justice too is a face of the divine perfection.

mots.racine: ق ط ع / د ب ر
mots.sens: the last remnant was cut off
mots.def: dābir: what follows, the rear; qaṭaʿa: to cut.

mots.racine: ر ب ب / ع ل م

versets.ar: قُلْ أَرَأَيْتُمْ إِنْ أَخَذَ اللَّهُ سَمْعَكُمْ وَأَبْصَارَكُمْ وَخَتَمَ عَلَىٰ قُلُوبِكُم مَّنْ إِلَٰهٌ غَيْرُ اللَّهِ يَأْتِيكُم بِهِ ۗ انظُرْ كَيْفَ نُصَرِّفُ الْآيَاتِ ثُمَّ هُمْ يَصْدِفُونَ
versets.tr: qul a-raʾaytum in akhadha llāhu samʿakum wa-abṣārakum wa-khatama ʿalā qulūbikum man ilāhun ghayru llāhi yaʾtīkum bihi nẓur kayfa nuṣarrifu l-āyāti thumma hum yaṣdifūn
versets.trad: Say: “What think you? If God took away your hearing and your sight and set a seal upon your hearts, what god other than God could bring them back to you?” See how We expound the signs, and yet they turn away!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: He made known to them the place of their powerlessness and the truth of their need for the eternal power, through the permanence of their indigence. Did He not maintain hearing, sight and health at every instant, who would restore what He had taken, who would re-establish what He had withheld? None but God. Perception itself is a renewed loan of His grace.

mots.racine: أ خ ذ / س م ع / ب ص ر
mots.sens: He takes your hearing and your sight

mots.def: ṣadafa: to turn away, to swerve.

versets.ar: قُلْ أَرَأَيْتَكُمْ إِنْ أَتَاكُمْ عَذَابُ اللَّهِ بَغْتَةً أَوْ جَهْرَةً هَلْ يُهْلَكُ إِلَّا الْقَوْمُ الظَّالِمُونَ
versets.tr: qul a-raʾaytakum in atākum ʿadhābu llāhi baghtatan aw jahratan hal yuhlaku illā l-qawmu ẓ-ẓālimūn
versets.trad: Say: “What think you? If God's chastisement comes to you suddenly or openly, who will be destroyed but the unjust people?”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: If He hastens the promised chastisement, do you suppose the one who does not deserve it would be afflicted, or that the deserving would find flight and refuge outside Him? That is impossible in thought. Divine justice never strays from its target: it reaches only the one who has condemned himself.

mots.racine: ب غ ت / ج ه ر
mots.def: baghta: suddenness; jahra: in broad day.

versets.ar: وَمَا نُرْسِلُ الْمُرْسَلِينَ إِلَّا مُبَشِّرِينَ وَمُنذِرِينَ ۖ فَمَنْ آمَنَ وَأَصْلَحَ فَلَا خَوْفٌ عَلَيْهِمْ وَلَا هُمْ يَحْزَنُونَ
versets.tr: wa-mā nursilu l-mursalīna illā mubashshirīna wa-mundhirīna fa-man āmana wa-aṣlaḥa fa-lā khawfun ʿalayhim wa-lā hum yaḥzanūn
versets.trad: We send the messengers only as bearers of glad tidings and as warners. Those who believe and amend themselves shall have no fear, nor shall they grieve.
themes: the twofold mission of the messengers
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: Our command is nothing but to make them embrace what saves them, through the fair promise, and to make them quit what destroys them; whoever believes and confirms, We fulfill the promise to him. The messenger brings two twin lamps: glad tidings (bishāra) to awaken longing, warning (indhār) to rouse fear.

mots.racine: أ م ن / ص ل ح
mots.def: aṣlaḥa: to reform oneself, to act well.

versets.ar: وَالَّذِينَ كَذَّبُوا بِآيَاتِنَا يَمَسُّهُمُ الْعَذَابُ بِمَا كَانُوا يَفْسُقُونَ
versets.tr: wa-lladhīna kadhdhabū bi-āyātinā yamassuhumu l-ʿadhābu bi-mā kānū yafsuqūn
versets.trad: But those who deny Our signs — the chastisement shall touch them for their perversity.
versets.commentaire: The contrast extends the previous verse: to belief that amends corresponds serenity; to the denial of the signs corresponds chastisement, the consequence of perversion (fisq). Qushayrī recalls that the coming trial arises from opposition to the Real, not from arbitrariness: each reaps the fruit of his own choice.

mots.def: fasaqa: to go out of obedience.

versets.ar: قُل لَّا أَقُولُ لَكُمْ عِندِي خَزَائِنُ اللَّهِ وَلَا أَعْلَمُ الْغَيْبَ وَلَا أَقُولُ لَكُمْ إِنِّي مَلَكٌ ۖ إِنْ أَتَّبِعُ إِلَّا مَا يُوحَىٰ إِلَيَّ ۚ قُلْ هَلْ يَسْتَوِي الْأَعْمَىٰ وَالْبَصِيرُ ۚ أَفَلَا تَتَفَكَّرُونَ
versets.tr: qul lā aqūlu lakum ʿindī khazāʾinu llāhi wa-lā aʿlamu l-ghayba wa-lā aqūlu lakum innī malakun in attabiʿu illā mā yūḥā ilayya qul hal yastawī l-aʿmā wa-l-baṣīru a-fa-lā tatafakkarūn
versets.trad: Say to them: “I do not say to you that I possess the treasuries of God. I do not know the unseen. And I do not say to you that I am an angel. I only follow what is revealed to me.” Say: “Are the blind and the seeing alike? Will you not reflect?”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī makes pure servanthood speak: say to them: I do not overstep my step, I do not exceed my limit, I establish nothing of my own depths; it is only said to me: have you conveyed? and I answer: yes, I have delivered. Then, on the blind and the seeing: do light and darkness equal one another? do denial and tawḥīd resemble each other? No, never.

mots.racine: ع ل م / غ ي ب
mots.sens: I do not know the unseen

mots.racine: ع م ي / ب ص ر

versets.ar: وَأَنذِرْ بِهِ الَّذِينَ يَخَافُونَ أَن يُحْشَرُوا إِلَىٰ رَبِّهِمْ ۙ لَيْسَ لَهُم مِّن دُونِهِ وَلِيٌّ وَلَا شَفِيعٌ لَّعَلَّهُمْ يَتَّقُونَ
versets.tr: wa-andhir bihi lladhīna yakhāfūna an yuḥsharū ilā rabbihim laysa lahum min dūnihi waliyyun wa-lā shafīʿun laʿallahum yattaqūn
versets.trad: And warn by it those who fear being gathered to their Lord — they have, besides Him, no protector and no intercessor — that they may become godfearing.
themes: the warning to those who fear
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: warning is an informing of the places of fear; and He reserved the warning for the fearful as He reserved guidance for the godfearing — “a guidance for the godfearing” — for benefit and following belong to reverential fear. Here, fear is knowledge: only one who knows is afraid; as for hearts beneath the veil of ignorance, no tremor of fear visits them.

mots.racine: و ل ي / ش ف ع

versets.ar: وَلَا تَطْرُدِ الَّذِينَ يَدْعُونَ رَبَّهُم بِالْغَدَاةِ وَالْعَشِيِّ يُرِيدُونَ وَجْهَهُ ۖ مَا عَلَيْكَ مِنْ حِسَابِهِم مِّن شَيْءٍ وَمَا مِنْ حِسَابِكَ عَلَيْهِم مِّن شَيْءٍ فَتَطْرُدَهُمْ فَتَكُونَ مِنَ الظَّالِمِينَ
versets.tr: wa-lā taṭrudi lladhīna yadʿūna rabbahum bi-l-ghadāti wa-l-ʿashiyyi yurīdūna wajhahu mā ʿalayka min ḥisābihim min shayʾin wa-mā min ḥisābika ʿalayhim min shayʾin fa-taṭrudahum fa-takūna mina ẓ-ẓālimīn
versets.trad: And do not drive away those who call upon their Lord morning and evening, seeking His Face. You are in no way accountable for them, nor are they accountable for you, that you should drive them away and be among the unjust.
themes: the poor who seek His Face
versets.commentaire: The verse of the “people of the bench” (ahl al-ṣuffa), those poor ones scorned by the notables. Qushayrī: look not, O Muḥammad, at the rags upon their outward, but at the burning ardor in their secrets. And on “they seek His Face”: their invocation is bound to morning and evening, for outward works are temporary; but their desire has swallowed all their instants, for the states of the within are without term. They rose with no request of their world nor their hereafter, with no care but the story of their Master.

mots.racine: غ د و / ع ش و
ر و د / و ج ه
mots.def: wajh: Face, presence; here: pure desire for God.

versets.ar: وَكَذَٰلِكَ فَتَنَّا بَعْضَهُم بِبَعْضٍ لِّيَقُولُوا أَهَٰؤُلَاءِ مَنَّ اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِم مِّن بَيْنِنَا ۗ أَلَيْسَ اللَّهُ بِأَعْلَمَ بِالشَّاكِرِينَ
versets.tr: wa-kadhālika fatannā baʿḍahum bi-baʿḍin li-yaqūlū a-hāʾulāʾi manna llāhu ʿalayhim min bayninā a-laysa llāhu bi-aʿlama bi-sh-shākirīn
versets.trad: Thus did We try some of them by others, that they might say: “Are these the ones whom God has favored among us?” Does God not know best those who are thankful?
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī, in two movements: as for the favored, let him be thankful; as for the less endowed, let him be patient. And higher still: on the tongue of love, the path of the less endowed is also thankfulness — and his thankfulness yields in nothing to that of the favored. The inequality of gifts is a trial that reveals the grateful hearts.

mots.def: fatana: to put to the test.

versets.ar: وَإِذَا جَاءَكَ الَّذِينَ يُؤْمِنُونَ بِآيَاتِنَا فَقُلْ سَلَامٌ عَلَيْكُمْ ۖ كَتَبَ رَبُّكُمْ عَلَىٰ نَفْسِهِ الرَّحْمَةَ ۖ أَنَّهُ مَنْ عَمِلَ مِنكُمْ سُوءًا بِجَهَالَةٍ ثُمَّ تَابَ مِن بَعْدِهِ وَأَصْلَحَ فَأَنَّهُ غَفُورٌ رَّحِيمٌ
versets.tr: wa-idhā jāʾaka lladhīna yuʾminūna bi-āyātinā fa-qul salāmun ʿalaykum kataba rabbukum ʿalā nafsihi r-raḥmata annahu man ʿamila minkum sūʾan bi-jahālatin thumma tāba min baʿdihi wa-aṣlaḥa fa-annahu ghafūrun raḥīm
versets.trad: When those who believe in Our signs come to you, say: “Peace be upon you! Your Lord has prescribed mercy upon Himself: whoever of you commits an evil in ignorance, then repents thereafter and amends — He is forgiving, compassionate.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: He set them at the rank of the great and the lords: for greeting is usually the act of the one who comes; but before the great, the visitor falls silent in reverence until the master begins — here it is God who commands the Prophet to say to them “Peace be upon you.” And on prescribed mercy: His inscription in your favor is eternal, His inscription against you is momentary; and the momentary does not abolish the eternal.

mots.racine: س و أ / ج ه ل
ت و ب / ص ل ح

versets.ar: وَكَذَٰلِكَ نُفَصِّلُ الْآيَاتِ وَلِتَسْتَبِينَ سَبِيلُ الْمُجْرِمِينَ
versets.tr: wa-kadhālika nufaṣṣilu l-āyāti wa-li-tastabīna sabīlu l-mujrimīn
versets.trad: Thus do We expound the signs, so that the path of the criminals may be made clear.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: We lift the obscurity, We make clear the path of proof, We make the suns of tawḥīd rise and support their people with the finest help; and We mark the hearts of the enemies with the brand of abandonment — that to none may remain an excuse, and on the path may remain no obscurity. To expound the signs is to set apart the way of light from the way of darkness.

mots.def: faṣṣala: to set forth in detail.

mots.racine: س ب ل / ج ر م

versets.ar: قُلْ إِنِّي نُهِيتُ أَنْ أَعْبُدَ الَّذِينَ تَدْعُونَ مِن دُونِ اللَّهِ ۚ قُل لَّا أَتَّبِعُ أَهْوَاءَكُمْ ۙ قَدْ ضَلَلْتُ إِذًا وَمَا أَنَا مِنَ الْمُهْتَدِينَ
versets.tr: qul innī nuhītu an aʿbuda lladhīna tadʿūna min dūni llāhi qul lā attabiʿu ahwāʾakum qad ḍalaltu idhan wa-mā anā mina l-muhtadīn
versets.trad: Say: “I am forbidden to worship those you call upon besides God.” Say: “I will not follow your passions, for then I would go astray and be no longer among the rightly guided.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: proclaim openly the acknowledgment of the gift with which We have favored you — infallibility and grace —, and make known to them that you have been returned within the precinct of protection and handled in the hand of safekeeping: passion has no power over you, and you have from the abode of Reality no remoteness, no absence from the Presence.

mots.racine: ن ه ي / ع ب د
ت ب ع / ه و ي
mots.sens: I do not follow your passions

versets.ar: قُلْ إِنِّي عَلَىٰ بَيِّنَةٍ مِّن رَّبِّي وَكَذَّبْتُم بِهِ ۚ مَا عِندِي مَا تَسْتَعْجِلُونَ بِهِ ۚ إِنِ الْحُكْمُ إِلَّا لِلَّهِ ۖ يَقُصُّ الْحَقَّ ۖ وَهُوَ خَيْرُ الْفَاصِلِينَ
versets.tr: qul innī ʿalā bayyinatin min rabbī wa-kadhdhabtum bihi mā ʿindī mā tastaʿjilūna bihi ini l-ḥukmu illā li-llāhi yaquṣṣu l-ḥaqqa wa-huwa khayru l-fāṣilīn
versets.trad: Say: “I stand upon a clear proof from my Lord, though you have denied it. I do not possess what you would hasten. The judgment belongs to none but God. He recounts the Truth, and He is the best of those who decide.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: Say: God has not left me in the wilderness of the quest and the maze of perplexity; He has spared me the labor of toilsome reasoning and refreshed me with the suns of Reality. If you remain in the darkness of confusion, I have no power to lift what afflicts you. The bayyina, the clear evidence, is not an argument wrenched out but a light received.

mots.def: istaʿjala: to press, to wish to hasten.

versets.ar: قُل لَّوْ أَنَّ عِندِي مَا تَسْتَعْجِلُونَ بِهِ لَقُضِيَ الْأَمْرُ بَيْنِي وَبَيْنَكُمْ ۗ وَاللَّهُ أَعْلَمُ بِالظَّالِمِينَ
versets.tr: qul law anna ʿindī mā tastaʿjilūna bihi la-quḍiya l-amru baynī wa-baynakum wa-llāhu aʿlamu bi-ẓ-ẓālimīn
versets.trad: Say: “If I possessed what you would hasten, the matter would already be settled between me and you. And God knows best the unjust.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: if I had power to produce the proofs you demand, I would grant you all you propose, out of compassion for you; but He who alone holds the judgment is contradicted in nothing of what He wills. The withheld chastisement is no weakness: it is the wisdom that measures its hour.

mots.racine: ع ل م / ظ ل م

versets.ar: وَعِندَهُ مَفَاتِحُ الْغَيْبِ لَا يَعْلَمُهَا إِلَّا هُوَ ۚ وَيَعْلَمُ مَا فِي الْبَرِّ وَالْبَحْرِ ۚ وَمَا تَسْقُطُ مِن وَرَقَةٍ إِلَّا يَعْلَمُهَا وَلَا حَبَّةٍ فِي ظُلُمَاتِ الْأَرْضِ وَلَا رَطْبٍ وَلَا يَابِسٍ إِلَّا فِي كِتَابٍ مُّبِينٍ
versets.tr: wa-ʿindahu mafātiḥu l-ghaybi lā yaʿlamuhā illā huwa wa-yaʿlamu mā fī l-barri wa-l-baḥri wa-mā tasquṭu min waraqatin illā yaʿlamuhā wa-lā ḥabbatin fī ẓulumāti l-arḍi wa-lā raṭbin wa-lā yābisin illā fī kitābin mubīn
versets.trad: With Him are the keys of the unseen; none knows them but He. He knows what is on the land and in the sea. Not a leaf falls but He knows it; there is no grain in the darknesses of the earth, nothing moist or dry, but it is in a clear Book.
themes: the keys of the unseen (mafātiḥ al-ghayb)
versets.commentaire: The verse of the mafātiḥ al-ghayb. Qushayrī: the key is that by which the lock is opened and the goal of each is attained; it is the power and the knowledge of the Real, through which alone beings come to be. And this pearl: you possess keys of your own unseen, and He possesses the keys of the unseen: if you believe in His unseen, He extends the sun over yours. The falling leaf, the grain buried in the night of the earth — nothing escapes the Register of His knowledge.

mots.racine: ف ت ح / غ ي ب
س ق ط / و ر ق

mots.def: the dark depths of the soil.

mots.racine: ك ت ب / ب ي ن
mots.def: the clear Register of divine knowledge.

versets.ar: وَهُوَ الَّذِي يَتَوَفَّاكُم بِاللَّيْلِ وَيَعْلَمُ مَا جَرَحْتُم بِالنَّهَارِ ثُمَّ يَبْعَثُكُمْ فِيهِ لِيُقْضَىٰ أَجَلٌ مُّسَمًّى ۖ ثُمَّ إِلَيْهِ مَرْجِعُكُمْ ثُمَّ يُنَبِّئُكُم بِمَا كُنتُمْ تَعْمَلُونَ
versets.tr: wa-huwa lladhī yatawaffākum bi-l-layli wa-yaʿlamu mā jaraḥtum bi-n-nahāri thumma yabʿathukum fīhi li-yuqḍā ajalun musamman thumma ilayhi marjiʿukum thumma yunabbiʾukum bi-mā kuntum taʿmalūn
versets.trad: It is He who takes your souls by night and knows what you have committed by day; then He raises you up in it, that an appointed term may be fulfilled. Then to Him is your return, and He will inform you of what you used to do.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: He gathers the souls in sleep as in death; and just as He does not chastise you by night for what you committed by day, despite His knowledge of your acts, all the more will He not chastise you tomorrow, when He has gathered you, for the evil He knew of your states. Each night is a death, each dawn a resurrection: a gentle pedagogy of the great passage.

mots.def: jaraḥa: to commit, to acquire (of deeds).

mots.sens: He raises you up in it (the day)

versets.ar: وَهُوَ الْقَاهِرُ فَوْقَ عِبَادِهِ ۖ وَيُرْسِلُ عَلَيْكُمْ حَفَظَةً حَتَّىٰ إِذَا جَاءَ أَحَدَكُمُ الْمَوْتُ تَوَفَّتْهُ رُسُلُنَا وَهُمْ لَا يُفَرِّطُونَ
versets.tr: wa-huwa l-qāhiru fawqa ʿibādihi wa-yursilu ʿalaykum ḥafaẓatan ḥattā idhā jāʾa aḥadakumu l-mawtu tawaffat'hu rusulunā wa-hum lā yufarriṭūn
versets.trad: He is the Irresistible, high above His servants, and He sends guardians over you, until, when death comes to one of you, Our envoys take his soul, and they are not remiss.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: He is above His servants by supremacy and elevation, above them by the power to chastise them from on high. The guardians (ḥafaẓa) watch over man until the final instant, when the angels of death take the soul with perfect exactness, without any negligence.

mots.racine: و ف ي / ر س ل

versets.ar: ثُمَّ رُدُّوا إِلَى اللَّهِ مَوْلَاهُمُ الْحَقِّ ۚ أَلَا لَهُ الْحُكْمُ وَهُوَ أَسْرَعُ الْحَاسِبِينَ
versets.tr: thumma ruddū ilā llāhi mawlāhumu l-ḥaqqi a-lā lahu l-ḥukmu wa-huwa asraʿu l-ḥāsibīn
versets.trad: Then they are returned to God, their true Master. Is the judgment not His? And He is the swiftest of reckoners.
themes: the return to the true Master
versets.commentaire: With striking concision, Qushayrī: He returned them to Himself — and they had never left His grasp. The “return” to God is no journey, but the unveiling of a presence that never ceased: the creature has not departed for a single instant from the palm of His sovereignty.

mots.racine: و ل ي / ح ق ق
mots.def: mawlā: master, protector; al-ḥaqq: the Real.

mots.racine: س ر ع / ح س ب

versets.ar: قُلْ مَن يُنَجِّيكُم مِّن ظُلُمَاتِ الْبَرِّ وَالْبَحْرِ تَدْعُونَهُ تَضَرُّعًا وَخُفْيَةً لَّئِنْ أَنجَانَا مِنْ هَٰذِهِ لَنَكُونَنَّ مِنَ الشَّاكِرِينَ
versets.tr: qul man yunajjīkum min ẓulumāti l-barri wa-l-baḥri tadʿūnahu taḍarruʿan wa-khufyatan la-in anjānā min hādhihi la-nakūnanna mina sh-shākirīn
versets.trad: Say: “Who delivers you from the darknesses of the land and the sea, when you call upon Him humbly and in secret: If He delivers us from this, we shall surely be among the thankful?”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the recalling of the blessing makes love grow: for when one acknowledges a generous favor bestowed, love settles in the heart. In the darknesses of danger, man implores in secret (khufya) and in humility (taḍarruʿ); this naked imploring is the involuntary confession that He alone delivers.

mots.racine: ض ر ع / خ ف ي

mots.sens: we shall be among the thankful

versets.ar: قُلِ اللَّهُ يُنَجِّيكُم مِّنْهَا وَمِن كُلِّ كَرْبٍ ثُمَّ أَنتُمْ تُشْرِكُونَ
versets.tr: quli llāhu yunajjīkum minhā wa-min kulli karbin thumma antum tushrikūn
versets.trad: Say: “It is God who delivers you from this and from every distress, yet you then associate others with Him!”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī, in litany: The One who alone holds the power to bring you into being is God; the One who makes up for what escapes you is God; the One who decreed your salvation is God; the One who takes you by the hand whenever you stumble is God. And yet, scarcely delivered, they return to association: the forgetting of the blessing is the root of shirk.

mots.def: ashraka: to give partners to God.

versets.ar: قُلْ هُوَ الْقَادِرُ عَلَىٰ أَن يَبْعَثَ عَلَيْكُمْ عَذَابًا مِّن فَوْقِكُمْ أَوْ مِن تَحْتِ أَرْجُلِكُمْ أَوْ يَلْبِسَكُمْ شِيَعًا وَيُذِيقَ بَعْضَكُم بَأْسَ بَعْضٍ ۗ انظُرْ كَيْفَ نُصَرِّفُ الْآيَاتِ لَعَلَّهُمْ يَفْقَهُونَ
versets.tr: qul huwa l-qādiru ʿalā an yabʿatha ʿalaykum ʿadhāban min fawqikum aw min taḥti arjulikum aw yalbisakum shiyaʿan wa-yudhīqa baʿḍakum baʾsa baʿḍin nẓur kayfa nuṣarrifu l-āyāti laʿallahum yafqahūn
versets.trad: Say: “He is able to send upon you a chastisement from above you or from beneath your feet, or to cast you into confusion of sects and make some of you taste the violence of others.” See how We expound the signs, that perhaps they may understand.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī offers here a pearl on concord: no dish is more bitter to man than man — whether in supposed friendship or in declared enmity. Whoever is tried by the hatred of his fellows finds his life here poisoned; whoever is tried by the passionate love of his peers finds his state with the Master troubled; and whoever is preserved from them, that one is the guarded. The discord of “sects” (shiyaʿ) is among the subtlest of chastisements.

mots.racine: ع ذ ب / ف و ق
mots.def: ʿadhāb: chastisement; coming from the sky.

mots.racine: ل ب س / ش ي ع
mots.def: shiyaʿ: factions, parties; labasa: to confound.

mots.racine: ذ و ق / ب أ س
mots.sens: to make some taste the violence of others

versets.ar: وَكَذَّبَ بِهِ قَوْمُكَ وَهُوَ الْحَقُّ ۚ قُل لَّسْتُ عَلَيْكُم بِوَكِيلٍ
versets.tr: wa-kadhdhaba bihi qawmuka wa-huwa l-ḥaqqu qul lastu ʿalaykum bi-wakīl
versets.trad: Your people have denied it, though it is the Truth. Say: “I am not your guardian!”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: say to them: upon me lies only the conveyance of the message; as for realizing the joining through existence and state, that belongs to the prerogatives of Power and the decrees of the eternal Will. The messenger bears the Word; he does not bear the hearts: faith is God's gift, not the preacher's work.

mots.racine: ك ذ ب / ق و م

versets.ar: لِّكُلِّ نَبَإٍ مُّسْتَقَرٌّ ۚ وَسَوْفَ تَعْلَمُونَ
versets.tr: li-kulli nabaʾin mustaqarrun wa-sawfa taʿlamūn
versets.trad: For every tiding there is a fixed time, and you shall come to know.
versets.commentaire: Every tiding (nabaʾ) has its anchoring point (mustaqarr), its appointed hour when it proves true. Qushayrī teaches elsewhere that the servant must surrender to God's time and not his own: what seems deferred is but the awaiting of the right moment, and patience is to believe in the term before seeing it.

versets.ar: وَإِذَا رَأَيْتَ الَّذِينَ يَخُوضُونَ فِي آيَاتِنَا فَأَعْرِضْ عَنْهُمْ حَتَّىٰ يَخُوضُوا فِي حَدِيثٍ غَيْرِهِ ۚ وَإِمَّا يُنسِيَنَّكَ الشَّيْطَانُ فَلَا تَقْعُدْ بَعْدَ الذِّكْرَىٰ مَعَ الْقَوْمِ الظَّالِمِينَ
versets.tr: wa-idhā raʾayta lladhīna yakhūḍūna fī āyātinā fa-aʿriḍ ʿanhum ḥattā yakhūḍū fī ḥadīthin ghayrihi wa-immā yunsiyannaka sh-shayṭānu fa-lā taqʿud baʿda dh-dhikrā maʿa l-qawmi ẓ-ẓālimīn
versets.trad: When you see those who plunge into mockery of Our signs, turn away from them until they plunge into some other talk. And if Satan should make you forget, then do not sit, after the reminder, with the unjust people.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: do not accord yourself to their state, do not answer them by spreading out speech; leave them to their desolation by fair turning-away, and keep far from lending an ear to their ravings. And if inadvertence overtakes you: make up for it by fair remembrance and delicate awakening, and take care not to slip twice into the same fault.

mots.sens: they plunge (in mockery) into Our signs
mots.def: khāḍa: to wade into idle talk.

versets.ar: وَمَا عَلَى الَّذِينَ يَتَّقُونَ مِنْ حِسَابِهِم مِّن شَيْءٍ وَلَٰكِن ذِكْرَىٰ لَعَلَّهُمْ يَتَّقُونَ
versets.tr: wa-mā ʿalā lladhīna yattaqūna min ḥisābihim min shayʾin wa-lākin dhikrā laʿallahum yattaqūn
versets.trad: Those who are godfearing are not accountable for them in any way, but it is a reminder, that they may become godfearing.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: whoever's garment is pure of any crime will, on the day of his resurrection, be kept apart from those pains. The godfearing one is not answerable for the faults of others; yet there remains for him the duty of reminding (dhikrā), not to bear the burden of others, but to open within them the possibility of the saving fear.

versets.ar: وَذَرِ الَّذِينَ اتَّخَذُوا دِينَهُمْ لَعِبًا وَلَهْوًا وَغَرَّتْهُمُ الْحَيَاةُ الدُّنْيَا ۚ وَذَكِّرْ بِهِ أَن تُبْسَلَ نَفْسٌ بِمَا كَسَبَتْ لَيْسَ لَهَا مِن دُونِ اللَّهِ وَلِيٌّ وَلَا شَفِيعٌ وَإِن تَعْدِلْ كُلَّ عَدْلٍ لَّا يُؤْخَذْ مِنْهَا ۗ أُولَٰئِكَ الَّذِينَ أُبْسِلُوا بِمَا كَسَبُوا ۖ لَهُمْ شَرَابٌ مِّنْ حَمِيمٍ وَعَذَابٌ أَلِيمٌ بِمَا كَانُوا يَكْفُرُونَ
versets.tr: wa-dhari lladhīna ttakhadhū dīnahum laʿiban wa-lahwan wa-gharrat'humu l-ḥayātu d-dunyā wa-dhakkir bihi an tubsala nafsun bi-mā kasabat laysa lahā min dūni llāhi waliyyun wa-lā shafīʿun wa-in taʿdil kulla ʿadlin lā yuʾkhadh minhā ulāʾika lladhīna ubsilū bi-mā kasabū lahum sharābun min ḥamīmin wa-ʿadhābun alīmun bi-mā kānū yakfurūn
versets.trad: Leave those who take their religion as play and diversion, and whom the life of this world has deluded. But remind by it, lest a soul be given up to ruin for what it has earned: it shall have, besides God, no protector and no intercessor, and were it to offer every ransom, it would not be accepted. Such are those given up to ruin for what they earned: theirs is a drink of scalding water and a painful chastisement, because they used to disbelieve.
themes: the soul given to its works
versets.commentaire: To make of one's religion play and diversion is to abandon it to the seduction of the world. Qushayrī recalls that nothing — neither ransom nor intercession — redeems the soul given up (mubsala) to its own acts: it stands alone before the fruits of its choice. The Sufi teaching here is blunt: the heart that takes God for diversion condemns itself to the solitude of chastisement.

mots.racine: أ خ ذ / د ي ن
mots.sens: they took their religion as play
that a soul be given up to ruin

mots.racine: ش ر ب / ح م م

versets.ar: قُلْ أَنَدْعُو مِن دُونِ اللَّهِ مَا لَا يَنفَعُنَا وَلَا يَضُرُّنَا وَنُرَدُّ عَلَىٰ أَعْقَابِنَا بَعْدَ إِذْ هَدَانَا اللَّهُ كَالَّذِي اسْتَهْوَتْهُ الشَّيَاطِينُ فِي الْأَرْضِ حَيْرَانَ لَهُ أَصْحَابٌ يَدْعُونَهُ إِلَى الْهُدَى ائْتِنَا ۗ قُلْ إِنَّ هُدَى اللَّهِ هُوَ الْهُدَىٰ ۖ وَأُمِرْنَا لِنُسْلِمَ لِرَبِّ الْعَالَمِينَ
versets.tr: qul a-nadʿū min dūni llāhi mā lā yanfaʿunā wa-lā yaḍurrunā wa-nuraddu ʿalā aʿqābinā baʿda idh hadānā llāhu ka-lladhī stahwat'hu sh-shayāṭīnu fī l-arḍi ḥayrāna lahu aṣḥābun yadʿūnahu ilā l-hudā 'tinā qul inna hudā llāhi huwa l-hudā wa-umirnā li-nuslima li-rabbi l-ʿālamīn
versets.trad: Say: “Shall we call, besides God, upon what can neither profit us nor harm us, and turn back on our heels after God has guided us — like one whom the devils have lured into bewilderment upon the earth, while his companions call him to guidance: Come to us!” Say: “Truly, God's guidance is the guidance, and we have been commanded to submit to the Lord of the worlds;
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: would we prefer error to guidance after the rising of the sun of proof? would we forsake the fairest way after the radiance of the evidence? would we abandon the threshold of the Garden where We have set you down, to demand Hell as a dwelling? That is far from reason, impossible in thought. The image of the strayed one “lured by the devils,” staggering while his friends call him, paints the soul torn between the call from above and the lure from below.

mots.racine: ن ف ع / ض ر ر
mots.sens: what neither profits nor harms us
mots.def: nafaʿa: to profit; ḍarra: to harm.

mots.racine: ر د د / ع ق ب
mots.sens: we turn back on our heels

mots.racine: ه و ي / ش ط ن
mots.sens: whom the devils have lured astray
mots.def: istahwā: to draw into the fall.

versets.ar: وَأَنْ أَقِيمُوا الصَّلَاةَ وَاتَّقُوهُ ۚ وَهُوَ الَّذِي إِلَيْهِ تُحْشَرُونَ
versets.tr: wa-an aqīmū ṣ-ṣalāta wa-ttaqūhu wa-huwa lladhī ilayhi tuḥsharūn
versets.trad: and: Establish the prayer and fear Him; it is He to whom you shall be gathered.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī, on the command of prayer: we were commanded to remain constant at the place of intimate discourse; for when the tongue grows accustomed to confidential whispering with the Sovereign, how should it stoop to converse with the basest? The ṣalāt is the school of presence: whoever grows used to the divine face-to-face turns away from himself.

mots.racine: ق و م / ص ل و
mots.def: aqāma ṣ-ṣalāt: to establish the prayer.

mots.sens: to Him you shall be gathered

versets.ar: وَهُوَ الَّذِي خَلَقَ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضَ بِالْحَقِّ ۖ وَيَوْمَ يَقُولُ كُن فَيَكُونُ ۚ قَوْلُهُ الْحَقُّ ۚ وَلَهُ الْمُلْكُ يَوْمَ يُنفَخُ فِي الصُّورِ ۚ عَالِمُ الْغَيْبِ وَالشَّهَادَةِ ۚ وَهُوَ الْحَكِيمُ الْخَبِيرُ
versets.tr: wa-huwa lladhī khalaqa s-samāwāti wa-l-arḍa bi-l-ḥaqqi wa-yawma yaqūlu kun fa-yakūnu qawluhu l-ḥaqqu wa-lahu l-mulku yawma yunfakhu fī ṣ-ṣūri ʿālimu l-ghaybi wa-sh-shahādati wa-huwa l-ḥakīmu l-khabīr
versets.trad: And it is He who created the heavens and the earth in Truth. And the Day He says: “Be!”, it is. His Word is the Truth. To Him belongs the dominion on the Day the trumpet is blown. Knower of the unseen and the witnessed. And He is the Wise, the All-Aware.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: no object of intention obstructs His power, and His decree never falls short before the handling of any being. The “Be!” (kun) is the pure instant where the Word becomes reality with no interval: no distance between His willing and existence. The Dominion is His on the Day of the trumpet as it is His now — only the unveiling changes.

mots.racine: خ ل ق / ح ق ق
mots.def: bi-l-ḥaqq: by the Real, in truth.

mots.sens: He says Be! and it is

mots.racine: ن ف خ / ص و ر
ع ل م / غ ي ب / ش ه د
mots.sens: Knower of the unseen and the witnessed

versets.ar: وَإِذْ قَالَ إِبْرَاهِيمُ لِأَبِيهِ آزَرَ أَتَتَّخِذُ أَصْنَامًا آلِهَةً ۖ إِنِّي أَرَاكَ وَقَوْمَكَ فِي ضَلَالٍ مُّبِينٍ
versets.tr: wa-idh qāla ibrāhīmu li-abīhi āzara a-tattakhidhu aṣnāman ālihatan innī arāka wa-qawmaka fī ḍalālin mubīn
versets.trad: [Recall the day] when Abraham said to his father Āzar: “Do you take idols as gods? I see you and your people in manifest error.”
versets.commentaire: The opening of the great account of Ibrāhīm. Qushayrī, in a dense stroke: the origin is suspect of denial, the offspring marked with the seal of tawḥīd, and the Real does what He wills. Ibrāhīm, born into a line of idolaters, is the very type of the soul that grace tears from its soil: he breaks first with the nearest, his own father — a sign that truth knows no complaisance.

mots.def: Āzar: the name of Ibrāhīm's father.

mots.racine: ص ن م / أ ل ه
mots.sens: do you take idols as gods

mots.racine: ض ل ل / ب ي ن

versets.ar: وَكَذَٰلِكَ نُرِي إِبْرَاهِيمَ مَلَكُوتَ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضِ وَلِيَكُونَ مِنَ الْمُوقِنِينَ
versets.tr: wa-kadhālika nurī ibrāhīma malakūta s-samāwāti wa-l-arḍi wa-li-yakūna mina l-mūqinīn
versets.trad: Thus did We show Abraham the dominion of the heavens and the earth, that he might be among those of certainty.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: He caressed him with the first solicitude, then unveiled him with the following guidance; He showed him of His proofs of oneness what left, in the horizon of his secret, not an atom of the dust of doubt; and when he was freed from the mists of conjectural reasoning, his secret rose up. The malakūt — the hidden, royal face of creation — is shown to Ibrāhīm that he may pass from reasoned proof to seen certainty (yaqīn).

versets.ar: فَلَمَّا جَنَّ عَلَيْهِ اللَّيْلُ رَأَىٰ كَوْكَبًا ۖ قَالَ هَٰذَا رَبِّي ۖ فَلَمَّا أَفَلَ قَالَ لَا أُحِبُّ الْآفِلِينَ
versets.tr: fa-lammā janna ʿalayhi l-laylu raʾā kawkaban qāla hādhā rabbī fa-lammā afala qāla lā uḥibbu l-āfilīn
versets.trad: When the night enveloped him, he saw a star and said: “This is my Lord!” But when it set, he said: “I do not love those that set.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī unfolds his theory of knowledge through the three heavenly bodies: the veils of the quest had enveloped him, and the dawn of Existence had not yet risen for him; then rose the star of the intellects, and he contemplated the Real in his secret by the light of proof: he said “This is my Lord.” The setting star cannot be the Lord: the soul learns that what absents itself does not deserve love. I do not love those that set is the first law of the path: to love none but the One who never declines.

mots.def: afala: to vanish below the horizon.

mots.racine: ح ب ب / أ ف ل
mots.sens: I do not love those that set

versets.ar: فَلَمَّا رَأَى الْقَمَرَ بَازِغًا قَالَ هَٰذَا رَبِّي ۖ فَلَمَّا أَفَلَ قَالَ لَئِن لَّمْ يَهْدِنِي رَبِّي لَأَكُونَنَّ مِنَ الْقَوْمِ الضَّالِّينَ
versets.tr: fa-lammā raʾā l-qamara bāzighan qāla hādhā rabbī fa-lammā afala qāla la-in lam yahdinī rabbī la-akūnanna mina l-qawmi ḍ-ḍāllīn
versets.trad: When he saw the moon rising, he said: “This is my Lord!” But when it set, he said: “If my Lord does not guide me, I shall surely be among the people who go astray.”
themes: the avowal of the need for guidance
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī continues the ascent: then its brightness grew, and the moon of knowledge rose for him, looking upon him under the condition of explication (bayān): he said “This is my Lord.” But the moon too sets. Ibrāhīm then acknowledges that without the guidance of God, the intellect left to itself goes astray: the avowal of need is the threshold of true knowledge.

mots.racine: ق م ر / ب ز غ

mots.sens: if my Lord does not guide me

mots.def: ḍāll: who has lost his way.

versets.ar: فَلَمَّا رَأَى الشَّمْسَ بَازِغَةً قَالَ هَٰذَا رَبِّي هَٰذَا أَكْبَرُ ۖ فَلَمَّا أَفَلَتْ قَالَ يَا قَوْمِ إِنِّي بَرِيءٌ مِّمَّا تُشْرِكُونَ
versets.tr: fa-lammā raʾā sh-shamsa bāzighatan qāla hādhā rabbī hādhā akbaru fa-lammā afalat qāla yā qawmi innī barīʾun mimmā tushrikūn
versets.trad: When he saw the sun rising, he said: “This is my Lord, this is greater!” But when it set, he said: “O my people! I am quit of what you associate with God.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī crowns the account: then the dawn grew clear and the day blossomed; the suns of gnosis (ʿirfān) rose from the tower of their nobility, and there remained no place for the quest, nor power for conjectural reasoning, nor abode for doubt: he said then “O my people, I am quit of what you associate,” for after direct vision (ʿiyān) there is no more doubt, and after unveiling no more veil. He adds: he contemplated first the traces by God, then things for God and from God, then he saw the others effaced (maḥw) in God.

mots.racine: ش م س / ب ز غ
ب ر أ / ش ر ك

versets.ar: إِنِّي وَجَّهْتُ وَجْهِيَ لِلَّذِي فَطَرَ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضَ حَنِيفًا ۖ وَمَا أَنَا مِنَ الْمُشْرِكِينَ
versets.tr: innī wajjahtu wajhiya li-lladhī faṭara s-samāwāti wa-l-arḍa ḥanīfan wa-mā anā mina l-mushrikīn
versets.trad: I have turned my face, as a pure monotheist, toward Him who created the heavens and the earth, and I am not among those who associate.”
themes: the face turned toward the Creator
versets.commentaire: The summit verse of the unification of the gaze. Qushayrī raises Ibrāhīm's avowal in an ecstatic gradation: I have devoted my orientation to God alone, purified my attachment of all other than God, kept my pact in God for God, and made pure my ecstasy by God: for I am to God, by God — nay, effaced (maḥw) in God, and God is God. The ḥanīf is the one whose face no longer turns away, the soul polarized wholly toward the One.

mots.def: wajjaha: to orient; wajh: face, intention.
ḥanīf: primordial believer, turned toward the One.

versets.ar: وَحَاجَّهُ قَوْمُهُ ۚ قَالَ أَتُحَاجُّونِّي فِي اللَّهِ وَقَدْ هَدَانِ ۚ وَلَا أَخَافُ مَا تُشْرِكُونَ بِهِ إِلَّا أَن يَشَاءَ رَبِّي شَيْئًا ۗ وَسِعَ رَبِّي كُلَّ شَيْءٍ عِلْمًا ۗ أَفَلَا تَتَذَكَّرُونَ
versets.tr: wa-ḥājjahu qawmuhu qāla a-tuḥājjūnnī fī llāhi wa-qad hadāni wa-lā akhāfu mā tushrikūna bihi illā an yashāʾa rabbī shayʾan wasiʿa rabbī kulla shayʾin ʿilman a-fa-lā tatadhakkarūn
versets.trad: His people argued with him. He said: “Do you dispute with me concerning God, when He has guided me? I do not fear what you associate with Him, unless my Lord wills something. My Lord embraces all things in knowledge. Will you not remember?
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī mocks the controversy of the idolaters: he said to them: would you veil the suns by drawing your sleeves over them, or do you mean to trail your hems and lower your curtains over the brightness of day, when His sovereignty has risen and His evidence has followed without cease? Whoever has seen no longer disputes; argument does not reach the one whom guidance has filled.

mots.sens: do you dispute with me about God

mots.racine: و س ع / ع ل م
mots.sens: my Lord embraces all things in knowledge

versets.ar: وَكَيْفَ أَخَافُ مَا أَشْرَكْتُمْ وَلَا تَخَافُونَ أَنَّكُمْ أَشْرَكْتُم بِاللَّهِ مَا لَمْ يُنَزِّلْ بِهِ عَلَيْكُمْ سُلْطَانًا ۚ فَأَيُّ الْفَرِيقَيْنِ أَحَقُّ بِالْأَمْنِ ۖ إِن كُنتُمْ تَعْلَمُونَ
versets.tr: wa-kayfa akhāfu mā ashraktum wa-lā takhāfūna annakum ashraktum bi-llāhi mā lam yunazzil bihi ʿalaykum sulṭānan fa-ayyu l-farīqayni aḥaqqu bi-l-amni in kuntum taʿlamūn
versets.trad: And how should I fear what you associate, when you do not fear that you have associated with God that for which He has sent down upon you no authority? Which of the two parties, then, is more deserving of security, if you but knew?”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī makes Ibrāhīm's assurance speak: what fear would let its shadow fall upon my heart, I who have never brushed against association nor inclined toward denial? You — you have not even smelled the scent of tawḥīd in all your life, nor tasted the savor of faith in your bygone age. The monotheist is in security because he leans upon the Real; the idolater trembles without knowing it, upon a support that is nothing.

mots.racine: خ و ف / ش ر ك
mots.sens: how should I fear what you associate

mots.racine: ن ز ل / س ل ط
mots.sens: that for which He has sent down no authority

mots.racine: ح ق ق / أ م ن

versets.ar: الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَلَمْ يَلْبِسُوا إِيمَانَهُم بِظُلْمٍ أُولَٰئِكَ لَهُمُ الْأَمْنُ وَهُم مُّهْتَدُونَ
versets.tr: alladhīna āmanū wa-lam yalbisū īmānahum bi-ẓulmin ulāʾika lahumu l-amnu wa-hum muhtadūn
versets.trad: Those who believe and have not clothed their faith in injustice — for them is security, and they are rightly guided.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī, on pure faith: these are those who have pointed toward God, then have not returned to any other than God; for whoever says “Allāh,” then returns — in his needs or his desires — to other than God, his adversary in this world and the next will be God.
