And this luminous definition: injustice (ẓulm), in truth, is to put a thing out of its place; and the worst is to believe that in the contingent there is what would come to be without Him — when the Producer is God, the Conductor is God, and there is no god but God: all that is not God falls away.

mots.racine: ل ب س / ظ ل م
mots.sens: they have not clothed their faith in injustice
mots.def: labasa: to clothe, to mix; ẓulm: here, association.

versets.ar: وَتِلْكَ حُجَّتُنَا آتَيْنَاهَا إِبْرَاهِيمَ عَلَىٰ قَوْمِهِ ۚ نَرْفَعُ دَرَجَاتٍ مَّن نَّشَاءُ ۗ إِنَّ رَبَّكَ حَكِيمٌ عَلِيمٌ
versets.tr: wa-tilka ḥujjatunā ātaynāhā ibrāhīma ʿalā qawmihi narfaʿu darajātin man nashāʾu inna rabbaka ḥakīmun ʿalīm
versets.trad: Such is Our argument, which We gave to Abraham against his people. We raise in degrees whom We will. Truly your Lord is Wise, All-Knowing.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī makes of this verse the very outline of the path: it points to his ascent from the contemplation of His signs (āyāt) to the affirmation of His Essence: this is the order of the people of wayfaring toward God. First the realization through the signs, which are His acts — the first degree; then the affirmation of His attributes — the second; then the realization through His existence and His Essence — which is the term of arrival: by His vestiges the servant knows His attributes, and by His attributes he knows His very existence. Ibrāhīm's argument is not dialectical: it is a gift of guidance.

mots.racine: ر ف ع / د ر ج
mots.sens: We raise in degrees whom We will

mots.racine: ح ك م / ع ل م

versets.ar: وَوَهَبْنَا لَهُ إِسْحَاقَ وَيَعْقُوبَ ۚ كُلًّا هَدَيْنَا ۚ وَنُوحًا هَدَيْنَا مِن قَبْلُ ۖ وَمِن ذُرِّيَّتِهِ دَاوُودَ وَسُلَيْمَانَ وَأَيُّوبَ وَيُوسُفَ وَمُوسَىٰ وَهَارُونَ ۚ وَكَذَٰلِكَ نَجْزِي الْمُحْسِنِينَ
versets.tr: wa-wahabnā lahu isḥāqa wa-yaʿqūba kullan hadaynā wa-nūḥan hadaynā min qablu wa-min dhurriyyatihi dāwūda wa-sulaymāna wa-ayyūba wa-yūsufa wa-mūsā wa-hārūna wa-kadhālika najzī l-muḥsinīn
versets.trad: And We bestowed upon him Isaac and Jacob; each We guided. And Noah We guided before, and of his descendants David and Solomon, Job and Joseph, Moses and Aaron — thus do We reward those who do good —
versets.commentaire: The gift of prophetic posterity crowns Ibrāhīm's trial. Qushayrī (on vv.84-88) underscores the immensity of the favor: He recalls the grandeur of His grace upon all these prophets, and shows that without His particular designation, without His election by the supreme honor, they would have had neither merit nor title. Guidance (hidāya) is never earned: it is pure largesse.

mots.def: wahaba: to give as a gift.

mots.racine: ج ز ي / ح س ن
mots.def: muḥsin: who does good with excellence (iḥsān).

versets.ar: وَزَكَرِيَّا وَيَحْيَىٰ وَعِيسَىٰ وَإِلْيَاسَ ۖ كُلٌّ مِّنَ الصَّالِحِينَ
versets.tr: wa-zakariyyā wa-yaḥyā wa-ʿīsā wa-ilyāsa kullun mina ṣ-ṣāliḥīn
versets.trad: and Zachariah and John and Jesus and Elias — each was among the righteous —
versets.commentaire: The litany of the elect continues. Qushayrī insists: their rank as “righteous” (ṣāliḥūn) is no conquered status but a granted qualification. Righteousness (ṣalāḥ) here is not mere moral uprightness, but the perfect adjustment of the being to the divine will — the soul made conformable to its vocation.

versets.ar: وَإِسْمَاعِيلَ وَالْيَسَعَ وَيُونُسَ وَلُوطًا ۚ وَكُلًّا فَضَّلْنَا عَلَى الْعَالَمِينَ
versets.tr: wa-ismāʿīla wa-l-yasaʿa wa-yūnusa wa-lūṭan wa-kullan faḍḍalnā ʿalā l-ʿālamīn
versets.trad: and Ishmael and Elisha and Jonah and Lot. Each We favored above the worlds.
versets.commentaire: Each of these prophets was “favored above the worlds” (faḍḍalnā ʿalā l-ʿālamīn). Qushayrī traces this preference to pure favor: faḍl, the overflowing grace, distinguishes whom God wills, with no prior cause in the creature. Prophetic election is the visible seal of an eternal love.

mots.racine: ف ض ل / ع ل م

versets.ar: وَمِنْ آبَائِهِمْ وَذُرِّيَّاتِهِمْ وَإِخْوَانِهِمْ ۖ وَاجْتَبَيْنَاهُمْ وَهَدَيْنَاهُمْ إِلَىٰ صِرَاطٍ مُّسْتَقِيمٍ
versets.tr: wa-min ābāʾihim wa-dhurriyyātihim wa-ikhwānihim wa-jtabaynāhum wa-hadaynāhum ilā ṣirāṭin mustaqīm
versets.trad: And from among their fathers, their descendants and their brothers — We chose them and guided them to a straight path.
versets.commentaire: The choosing (ijtibāʾ) and the guidance (hidāya) extend to forebears, descendants and brothers. Qushayrī sees in ijtibāʾ — the election that “gathers” the servant for Himself — the summit of grace: God sets the chosen apart from the mass and orients him toward the straight path. Lineage is no hereditary merit, but the unfolding of an electing mercy.

mots.racine: أ ب و / ذ ر ر

mots.def: ijtabā: to choose, to elect for Himself.

mots.racine: ص ر ط / ق و م

versets.ar: ذَٰلِكَ هُدَى اللَّهِ يَهْدِي بِهِ مَن يَشَاءُ مِنْ عِبَادِهِ ۚ وَلَوْ أَشْرَكُوا لَحَبِطَ عَنْهُم مَّا كَانُوا يَعْمَلُونَ
versets.tr: dhālika hudā llāhi yahdī bihi man yashāʾu min ʿibādihi wa-law ashrakū la-ḥabiṭa ʿanhum mā kānū yaʿmalūn
versets.trad: Such is the guidance of God, by which He guides whom He wills of His servants. Had they associated others with Him, all they did would have come to naught.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: had they paid heed to another, or contemplated a thing besides Us, or attributed the least atom of the contingent to a power other than Ours in its appearing, then would have vanished all they had amassed of knowledge and fair doing — for God never forgives association, even though He forgives what is below it, to whom He wills. Shirk is the only act that retroactively annuls the whole edifice of works.

mots.sens: such is the guidance of God

mots.racine: ه د ي / ش ي أ

mots.sens: it would have come to naught for them
mots.def: ḥabiṭa: to become vain, to be annulled.

versets.ar: أُولَٰئِكَ الَّذِينَ آتَيْنَاهُمُ الْكِتَابَ وَالْحُكْمَ وَالنُّبُوَّةَ ۚ فَإِن يَكْفُرْ بِهَا هَٰؤُلَاءِ فَقَدْ وَكَّلْنَا بِهَا قَوْمًا لَّيْسُوا بِهَا بِكَافِرِينَ
versets.tr: ulāʾika lladhīna ātaynāhumu l-kitāba wa-l-ḥukma wa-n-nubuwwata fa-in yakfur bihā hāʾulāʾi fa-qad wakkalnā bihā qawman laysū bihā bi-kāfirīn
versets.trad: Those are they to whom We gave the Book, the wisdom and the prophethood. If these deny them, We have entrusted them to a people who do not deny them.
themes: the trust entrusted to the faithful
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: if your people turn away, O Muḥammad, it is not that We have abandoned all men to denial; many a servant We have purified his heart of all denial, and kneaded his clay with the water of felicity: those do not deviate from tawḥīd for an instant, nor swerve from realization for a breath. The Book, the discernment and the prophethood never remain without bearers.

mots.racine: ك ت ب / ح ك م / ن ب أ
mots.sens: the Book, the wisdom, the prophethood
We entrusted them to a people
mots.def: wakkala: to entrust, to appoint over.

versets.ar: أُولَٰئِكَ الَّذِينَ هَدَى اللَّهُ ۖ فَبِهُدَاهُمُ اقْتَدِهْ ۗ قُل لَّا أَسْأَلُكُمْ عَلَيْهِ أَجْرًا ۖ إِنْ هُوَ إِلَّا ذِكْرَىٰ لِلْعَالَمِينَ
versets.tr: ulāʾika lladhīna hadā llāhu fa-bi-hudāhumu qtadih qul lā asʾalukum ʿalayhi ajran in huwa illā dhikrā li-l-ʿālamīn
versets.trad: Those are they whom God has guided. So follow their guidance. Say: “I ask of you no wage for it; it is but a reminder for the worlds.”
themes: following the guidance of the elect
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: these are those whose secrets God has purified of denial and whose ranks He has raised above all; so follow, O Muḥammad, their guidance, for whoever takes the high road is safe from hardship. The Prophet is bidden to model himself on the cohort of the guided; and the reminder (dhikrā) is offered freely, for truth is not for sale.

mots.def: iqtadā: to take as a model.

mots.racine: ذ ك ر / ع ل م

versets.ar: وَمَا قَدَرُوا اللَّهَ حَقَّ قَدْرِهِ إِذْ قَالُوا مَا أَنزَلَ اللَّهُ عَلَىٰ بَشَرٍ مِّن شَيْءٍ ۗ قُلْ مَنْ أَنزَلَ الْكِتَابَ الَّذِي جَاءَ بِهِ مُوسَىٰ نُورًا وَهُدًى لِّلنَّاسِ ۖ تَجْعَلُونَهُ قَرَاطِيسَ تُبْدُونَهَا وَتُخْفُونَ كَثِيرًا ۖ وَعُلِّمْتُم مَّا لَمْ تَعْلَمُوا أَنتُمْ وَلَا آبَاؤُكُمْ ۖ قُلِ اللَّهُ ۖ ثُمَّ ذَرْهُمْ فِي خَوْضِهِمْ يَلْعَبُونَ
versets.tr: wa-mā qadarū llāha ḥaqqa qadrihi idh qālū mā anzala llāhu ʿalā basharin min shayʾin qul man anzala l-kitāba lladhī jāʾa bihi mūsā nūran wa-hudan li-n-nāsi tajʿalūnahu qarāṭīsa tubdūnahā wa-tukhfūna kathīran wa-ʿullimtum mā lam taʿlamū antum wa-lā ābāʾukum quli llāhu thumma dharhum fī khawḍihim yalʿabūn
versets.trad: They have not measured God with His true measure when they say: “God has sent down nothing upon any mortal!” Say: “Who sent down the Book that Moses brought, a light and a guidance for mankind? You make it into parchments which you display, while you hide much. You were taught what neither you nor your fathers knew.” Say: “God!”, then leave them to play in their idle plunging.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: whoever imagines that the sciences encompass His majesty is mistaken: encompassment (iḥāṭa) does not befit His attribute, as grasping is not permitted of Him, as overlooking is impossible upon His Essence. And on the controversy: proclaim openly the tawḥīd, and let their overbidding in falsehood not appall you, for the embellishments of falsehood have no effect upon the realities.

mots.sens: they did not measure God with His true measure
mots.def: qadara: to estimate; ḥaqqa qadrih: at its true worth.

mots.racine: ن و ر / ه د ي
mots.sens: a light and guidance for mankind

mots.racine: خ و ض / ل ع ب

versets.ar: وَهَٰذَا كِتَابٌ أَنزَلْنَاهُ مُبَارَكٌ مُّصَدِّقُ الَّذِي بَيْنَ يَدَيْهِ وَلِتُنذِرَ أُمَّ الْقُرَىٰ وَمَنْ حَوْلَهَا ۚ وَالَّذِينَ يُؤْمِنُونَ بِالْآخِرَةِ يُؤْمِنُونَ بِهِ ۖ وَهُمْ عَلَىٰ صَلَاتِهِمْ يُحَافِظُونَ
versets.tr: wa-hādhā kitābun anzalnāhu mubārakun muṣaddiqu lladhī bayna yadayhi wa-li-tundhira umma l-qurā wa-man ḥawlahā wa-lladhīna yuʾminūna bi-l-ākhirati yuʾminūna bihi wa-hum ʿalā ṣalātihim yuḥāfiẓūn
versets.trad: And this is a Book We have sent down, blessed, confirming what came before it, that you may warn the Mother of Cities and those around it. Those who believe in the hereafter believe in it, and they are watchful over their prayers.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī makes of the Book the letter of the beloved: the missive of the loved ones is of rare price and lofty trace: it brings consolation amid the assaults of ecstasy; and whoever remains short of union humbles himself before the Envoy. The Qurʾān, the “blessed Book” (mubārak), confirms the earlier revelations and summons, beyond Mecca, all humanity to the vigilance of prayer.

mots.racine: ك ت ب / ب ر ك
أ م م / ق ر ي

versets.ar: وَمَنْ أَظْلَمُ مِمَّنِ افْتَرَىٰ عَلَى اللَّهِ كَذِبًا أَوْ قَالَ أُوحِيَ إِلَيَّ وَلَمْ يُوحَ إِلَيْهِ شَيْءٌ وَمَن قَالَ سَأُنزِلُ مِثْلَ مَا أَنزَلَ اللَّهُ ۗ وَلَوْ تَرَىٰ إِذِ الظَّالِمُونَ فِي غَمَرَاتِ الْمَوْتِ وَالْمَلَائِكَةُ بَاسِطُو أَيْدِيهِمْ أَخْرِجُوا أَنفُسَكُمُ ۖ الْيَوْمَ تُجْزَوْنَ عَذَابَ الْهُونِ بِمَا كُنتُمْ تَقُولُونَ عَلَى اللَّهِ غَيْرَ الْحَقِّ وَكُنتُمْ عَنْ آيَاتِهِ تَسْتَكْبِرُونَ
versets.tr: wa-man aẓlamu mimmani ftarā ʿalā llāhi kadhiban aw qāla ūḥiya ilayya wa-lam yūḥa ilayhi shayʾun wa-man qāla sa-unzilu mithla mā anzala llāhu wa-law tarā idhi ẓ-ẓālimūna fī ghamarāti l-mawti wa-l-malāʾikatu bāsiṭū aydīhim akhrijū anfusakumu l-yawma tujzawna ʿadhāba l-hūni bi-mā kuntum taqūlūna ʿalā llāhi ghayra l-ḥaqqi wa-kuntum ʿan āyātihi tastakbirūn
versets.trad: Who is more unjust than one who forges a lie against God, or says: “It has been revealed to me,” when nothing was revealed to him? Or one who says: “I will send down the like of what God has sent down”? If you could see the unjust in the throes of death, while the angels stretch out their hands: “Yield up your souls! This day you shall be rewarded with the chastisement of humiliation, for what you used to say against God, untruly, and for your scorning His signs in pride.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: those who raise themselves to the rank of the inspired without the least privilege of the divine address being cast to their secrets — of those the Real is quit. And he cites this verse: when tears mingle on the cheeks, one tells apart who weeps from who feigns to weep. The claim to revelation is the supreme imposture; death, by tearing off the mask, reveals the true from the false.

mots.sens: it was revealed to me — with no revelation

mots.racine: غ م ر / م و ت
ع ذ ب / ه و ن

versets.ar: وَلَقَدْ جِئْتُمُونَا فُرَادَىٰ كَمَا خَلَقْنَاكُمْ أَوَّلَ مَرَّةٍ وَتَرَكْتُم مَّا خَوَّلْنَاكُمْ وَرَاءَ ظُهُورِكُمْ ۖ وَمَا نَرَىٰ مَعَكُمْ شُفَعَاءَكُمُ الَّذِينَ زَعَمْتُمْ أَنَّهُمْ فِيكُمْ شُرَكَاءُ ۚ لَقَد تَّقَطَّعَ بَيْنَكُمْ وَضَلَّ عَنكُم مَّا كُنتُمْ تَزْعُمُونَ
versets.tr: wa-la-qad jiʾtumūnā furādā kamā khalaqnākum awwala marratin wa-taraktum mā khawwalnākum warāʾa ẓuhūrikum wa-mā narā maʿakum shufaʿāʾakumu lladhīna zaʿamtum annahum fīkum shurakāʾu la-qad taqaṭṭaʿa baynakum wa-ḍalla ʿankum mā kuntum tazʿumūn
versets.trad: And you have come to Us alone, as We created you the first time, leaving behind your backs what We bestowed upon you. And We do not see with you your intercessors whom you claimed to be associates among you. The bond between you is severed, and what you claimed has forsaken you.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī, with shattering nakedness: you entered the world clad in a single swaddling-cloth, you leave it wrapped in a single shroud — and even that shroud is not yours; you entered only stripped bare, and you leave only solitary. At the threshold of the meeting, no goods, no intercessors, no alliances: every bond is severed, every claim vanishes, and the soul stands alone, as on the first day.

mots.racine: ج ي أ / ف ر د
mots.sens: you have come to Us alone
the bond between you is severed

versets.ar: إِنَّ اللَّهَ فَالِقُ الْحَبِّ وَالنَّوَىٰ ۖ يُخْرِجُ الْحَيَّ مِنَ الْمَيِّتِ وَمُخْرِجُ الْمَيِّتِ مِنَ الْحَيِّ ۚ ذَٰلِكُمُ اللَّهُ ۖ فَأَنَّىٰ تُؤْفَكُونَ
versets.tr: inna llāha fāliqu l-ḥabbi wa-n-nawā yukhriju l-ḥayya mina l-mayyiti wa-mukhriju l-mayyiti mina l-ḥayyi dhālikumu llāhu fa-annā tuʾfakūn
versets.trad: Indeed God is the cleaver of the grain and the date-stone. He brings forth the living from the dead, and brings forth the dead from the living. That is God! How then are you turned away?
themes: the One who cleaves the grain
the living drawn from the dead
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the One who brings into being the essences and the traces, the vestiges and the ruins of the world, subjects nothingness to what He wills of His works and decrees subsistence to what He wills of His creatures: none returns His decree, none denies His right. To cleave the buried grain and draw life from it is the daily sign of the resurrection: the dead becomes living under His hand.

mots.racine: ف ل ق / ح ب ب / ن و ي
mots.sens: cleaver of the grain and the date-stone
mots.def: falaqa: to split; ḥabb: grain; nawā: stone.

mots.racine: خ ر ج / ح ي ي / م و ت
mots.sens: brings the living from the dead

versets.ar: فَالِقُ الْإِصْبَاحِ وَجَعَلَ اللَّيْلَ سَكَنًا وَالشَّمْسَ وَالْقَمَرَ حُسْبَانًا ۚ ذَٰلِكَ تَقْدِيرُ الْعَزِيزِ الْعَلِيمِ
versets.tr: fāliqu l-iṣbāḥi wa-jaʿala l-layla sakanan wa-sh-shamsa wa-l-qamara ḥusbānan dhālika taqdīru l-ʿazīzi l-ʿalīm
versets.trad: He cleaves the sky into dawn; He made the night for repose, and the sun and the moon for reckoning. That is the ordaining of the Mighty, the All-Knowing.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī weaves the cosmic dawn and the inner dawn: just as He cleft the dawn of the universe and the lights shone forth, so He cleft the dawn of the hearts, and by it the secrets were illumined. And just as He made the night a repose where the souls rest from the toil of labor, so He made the night a repose for the loved ones, who rest in it in the sweetness of intimate discourse, when the eyes are stilled from alien gazes.

mots.racine: ف ل ق / ص ب ح

mots.sens: He made the night a repose
the sun and moon for reckoning

versets.ar: وَهُوَ الَّذِي جَعَلَ لَكُمُ النُّجُومَ لِتَهْتَدُوا بِهَا فِي ظُلُمَاتِ الْبَرِّ وَالْبَحْرِ ۗ قَدْ فَصَّلْنَا الْآيَاتِ لِقَوْمٍ يَعْلَمُونَ
versets.tr: wa-huwa lladhī jaʿala lakumu n-nujūma li-tahtadū bihā fī ẓulumāti l-barri wa-l-baḥri qad faṣṣalnā l-āyāti li-qawmin yaʿlamūn
versets.trad: And it is He who has set for you the stars, that you may be guided by them through the darknesses of land and sea. We have detailed the signs for a people who know.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī opens a luminous correspondence: just as the stars of the sky serve as guides in the deserts, so the stars of the hearts serve as guides in the knowledge of the Lord of the lands and the heavens. To the physical stars correspond the inner lights — presentiments, certainties, unveilings — that orient the soul through the night of existence.

mots.sens: He set for you the stars
that you may be guided by them

mots.racine: ظ ل م / ب ر ر / ب ح ر
mots.sens: the darknesses of land and sea

versets.ar: وَهُوَ الَّذِي أَنشَأَكُم مِّن نَّفْسٍ وَاحِدَةٍ فَمُسْتَقَرٌّ وَمُسْتَوْدَعٌ ۗ قَدْ فَصَّلْنَا الْآيَاتِ لِقَوْمٍ يَفْقَهُونَ
versets.tr: wa-huwa lladhī anshaʾakum min nafsin wāḥidatin fa-mustaqarrun wa-mustawdaʿun qad faṣṣalnā l-āyāti li-qawmin yafqahūn
versets.trad: And it is He who brought you forth from a single soul, with a place of settling and a place of deposit. We have detailed the signs for a people who understand.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī transposes the two poles — place of settling (mustaqarr) and of deposit (mustawdaʿ) — from body to spirit: just as souls and bodies have a place of settling and a place of deposit, so the secrets and consciences have one: one has for the dwelling of his heart the homeland of desires and passions; another, the place of asceticism and piety; and yet another has for his dwelling what is beyond every dwelling — beyond the creatures. In annihilation (fanāʾ), the secret melts into the Existence without limits.

mots.racine: ن ش أ / ن ف س
mots.sens: He brought you forth from a single soul
mots.def: anshaʾa: to make grow; nafs wāḥida: Adam.

versets.ar: وَهُوَ الَّذِي أَنزَلَ مِنَ السَّمَاءِ مَاءً فَأَخْرَجْنَا بِهِ نَبَاتَ كُلِّ شَيْءٍ فَأَخْرَجْنَا مِنْهُ خَضِرًا نُّخْرِجُ مِنْهُ حَبًّا مُّتَرَاكِبًا وَمِنَ النَّخْلِ مِن طَلْعِهَا قِنْوَانٌ دَانِيَةٌ وَجَنَّاتٍ مِّنْ أَعْنَابٍ وَالزَّيْتُونَ وَالرُّمَّانَ مُشْتَبِهًا وَغَيْرَ مُتَشَابِهٍ ۗ انظُرُوا إِلَىٰ ثَمَرِهِ إِذَا أَثْمَرَ وَيَنْعِهِ ۚ إِنَّ فِي ذَٰلِكُمْ لَآيَاتٍ لِّقَوْمٍ يُؤْمِنُونَ
versets.tr: wa-huwa lladhī anzala mina s-samāʾi māʾan fa-akhrajnā bihi nabāta kulli shayʾin fa-akhrajnā minhu khaḍiran nukhriju minhu ḥabban mutarākiban wa-mina n-nakhli min ṭalʿihā qinwānun dāniyatun wa-jannātin min aʿnābin wa-z-zaytūna wa-r-rummāna mushtabihan wa-ghayra mutashābihin nẓurū ilā thamarihi idhā athmara wa-yanʿihi inna fī dhālikum la-āyātin li-qawmin yuʾminūn
versets.trad: And it is He who sends down water from the sky, and by it We bring forth the growth of every thing. From it We bring forth verdure, from which We bring forth clustered grain; and from the palm, from its spathe, low-hanging clusters; and gardens of vines, and the olive and the pomegranate, alike and unlike. Look at their fruit when it bears and ripens. Surely in that are signs for a people who believe!
themes: the water that brings forth life
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the parts of the earth resemble one another, the regions of the universe agree, and yet the plants diverge in color and savor, the things differ — and each creature attests, with eloquent tongue and clear utterance, that it does not subsist by itself. One same water, one same soil, and so many unlike fruits: the diversity that springs from unity is the signature of the One.

mots.racine: ن ز ل / م و ه
mots.sens: He sent down water from the sky

mots.racine: ح ب ب / ر ك ب
ق ن و / د ن و

versets.ar: وَجَعَلُوا لِلَّهِ شُرَكَاءَ الْجِنَّ وَخَلَقَهُمْ ۖ وَخَرَقُوا لَهُ بَنِينَ وَبَنَاتٍ بِغَيْرِ عِلْمٍ ۚ سُبْحَانَهُ وَتَعَالَىٰ عَمَّا يَصِفُونَ
versets.tr: wa-jaʿalū li-llāhi shurakāʾa l-jinna wa-khalaqahum wa-kharaqū lahu banīna wa-banātin bi-ghayri ʿilmin subḥānahu wa-taʿālā ʿammā yaṣifūn
versets.trad: They have set up the jinn as associates with God, though it is He who created them; and they have falsely ascribed to Him sons and daughters, without knowledge. Glory be to Him, and exalted is He above what they ascribe!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: their insights were stopped up, so that they were content to worship every diminished thing; and that is a chastisement, hastened from this world, against the masters of heedlessness toward God. To ascribe to God offspring or associates is the verdict of a blinded gaze: transcendence (tanzīh) cleanses the Name of every such defilement.

mots.racine: ش ر ك / ج ن ن

mots.sens: they invented for Him sons and daughters

mots.racine: س ب ح / ع ل و

versets.ar: بَدِيعُ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضِ ۖ أَنَّىٰ يَكُونُ لَهُ وَلَدٌ وَلَمْ تَكُن لَّهُ صَاحِبَةٌ ۖ وَخَلَقَ كُلَّ شَيْءٍ ۖ وَهُوَ بِكُلِّ شَيْءٍ عَلِيمٌ
versets.tr: badīʿu s-samāwāti wa-l-arḍi annā yakūnu lahu waladun wa-lam takun lahu ṣāḥibatun wa-khalaqa kulla shayʾin wa-huwa bi-kulli shayʾin ʿalīm
versets.trad: Originator of the heavens and the earth — how should He have a child, when He has no consort, when He has created all things and knows all things?
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: al-Badīʿ is the One who has no like, or the One who creates without prior model — and both senses befit Him. The One can have no child, for a child implies a partition, and tawḥīd refuses it. The Name Badīʿ bespeaks pure invention, without precedent or associate: what springs from Him imitates nothing and divides Him not.

mots.sens: how should He have a child

versets.ar: ذَٰلِكُمُ اللَّهُ رَبُّكُمْ ۖ لَا إِلَٰهَ إِلَّا هُوَ ۖ خَالِقُ كُلِّ شَيْءٍ فَاعْبُدُوهُ ۚ وَهُوَ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَيْءٍ وَكِيلٌ
versets.tr: dhālikumu llāhu rabbukum lā ilāha illā huwa khāliqu kulli shayʾin fa-ʿbudūhu wa-huwa ʿalā kulli shayʾin wakīl
versets.trad: That is God, your Lord. There is no god but He, the Creator of all things. So worship Him! And He is Guardian over all things.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī distinguishes here two degrees of knowledge: He made Himself known to them by His signs, then by His attributes, then He unveiled them by the realities of His Essence. His word “there is no god but He” is a knowing for the masters and the great; and “Creator of all things” is a knowing for the common and the lesser. The same verse nourishes the beginner through the works and the initiate through the Essence.

mots.sens: there is no god but He

versets.ar: لَّا تُدْرِكُهُ الْأَبْصَارُ وَهُوَ يُدْرِكُ الْأَبْصَارَ ۖ وَهُوَ اللَّطِيفُ الْخَبِيرُ
versets.tr: lā tudrikuhu l-abṣāru wa-huwa yudriku l-abṣāra wa-huwa l-laṭīfu l-khabīr
versets.trad: No vision can grasp Him, but He grasps all vision. He is the Subtle, the All-Aware.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: He has sanctified the Pure Ipseity from all attainment and all grasping: how should perception reach Him, who has neither limit nor term? He is al-Laṭīf, from whom nothing is hidden, al-Khabīr, whose knowledge has encompassed all. The gaze, finite, cannot enclose the Infinite; but the Infinite encloses and penetrates every gaze — a truth that founds the humility of spiritual vision.

mots.racine: د ر ك / ب ص ر
mots.def: adraka: to grasp, to attain; abṣār: visions.

mots.racine: ل ط ف / خ ب ر

versets.ar: قَدْ جَاءَكُم بَصَائِرُ مِن رَّبِّكُمْ ۖ فَمَنْ أَبْصَرَ فَلِنَفْسِهِ ۖ وَمَنْ عَمِيَ فَعَلَيْهَا ۚ وَمَا أَنَا عَلَيْكُم بِحَفِيظٍ
versets.tr: qad jāʾakum baṣāʾiru min rabbikum fa-man abṣara fa-li-nafsihi wa-man ʿamiya fa-ʿalayhā wa-mā anā ʿalaykum bi-ḥafīẓ
versets.trad: [Say, O Muḥammad:] “Clear proofs have come to you from your Lord. Whoever sees, it is for his own good; and whoever is blind, it is to his own loss. And I am not a keeper over you.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī expounds the evidence and cites a bitter verse: He has made the explanation clear, made the proof shine, dispelled the pretexts and lit the path; but — as it has been said — of what use is his pupil to the son of the world, when light and darkness are equal in his sight? The baṣāʾir, inner clarities, are offered to all; each chooses to see or to blind himself — and bears the fruit alone.

mots.racine: ب ص ر / ن ف س
mots.sens: whoever sees, it is for himself
whoever is blind, it is to his loss

versets.ar: وَكَذَٰلِكَ نُصَرِّفُ الْآيَاتِ وَلِيَقُولُوا دَرَسْتَ وَلِنُبَيِّنَهُ لِقَوْمٍ يَعْلَمُونَ
versets.tr: wa-kadhālika nuṣarrifu l-āyāti wa-li-yaqūlū darasta wa-li-nubayyinahu li-qawmin yaʿlamūn
versets.trad: Thus do We diversify the signs, so that they may say: “You have studied,” and that We may make it clear to a people who know.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: He cast the temptation into their hearts, and the states drew back before them: for some a doubt insinuated itself, others a perplexity seized; while realization was the lot of some, and elucidation halted for others. The same sign troubles one and enlightens another: for light is received only according to the heart's disposition.

mots.def: ṣarrafa: to set forth in varied forms.

mots.sens: so that they say: you have learned
and that We may make it clear

versets.ar: اتَّبِعْ مَا أُوحِيَ إِلَيْكَ مِن رَّبِّكَ ۖ لَا إِلَٰهَ إِلَّا هُوَ ۖ وَأَعْرِضْ عَنِ الْمُشْرِكِينَ
versets.tr: ittabiʿ mā ūḥiya ilayka min rabbika lā ilāha illā huwa wa-aʿriḍ ʿani l-mushrikīn
versets.trad: Follow what has been revealed to you from your Lord: there is no god but He. And turn away from those who associate.
versets.commentaire: The command is threefold: to follow the revelation, to profess tawḥīd, to turn away from the idolaters. In the line of Qushayrī, to follow what is revealed (ittibāʿ) is no servile obedience but the alignment of the heart upon the Presence: the received Word becomes the compass of the being, and turning from the associators preserves the purity of orientation.

mots.racine: ت ب ع / و ح ي
mots.sens: follow what is revealed to you
mots.def: ittabaʿa: to follow; awḥā: to reveal.

mots.sens: there is no god but He

mots.racine: ع ر ض / ش ر ك

versets.ar: وَلَوْ شَاءَ اللَّهُ مَا أَشْرَكُوا ۗ وَمَا جَعَلْنَاكَ عَلَيْهِمْ حَفِيظًا ۖ وَمَا أَنتَ عَلَيْهِم بِوَكِيلٍ
versets.tr: wa-law shāʾa llāhu mā ashrakū wa-mā jaʿalnāka ʿalayhim ḥafīẓan wa-mā anta ʿalayhim bi-wakīl
versets.trad: Had God willed, they would not have associated. We have not made you a keeper over them, nor are you a trustee over them.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: strange is the one who acknowledges his own state too short to deserve praise, for falling short of his goal — and yet describes his Worshipped as one whose will might not come to pass in His own kingdom! That men associate other gods belongs, in the last resort, to the divine permission; the Prophet is neither their watchman nor their warrant: faith cannot be forced.

mots.racine: ش ي أ / ش ر ك
mots.sens: had God willed, they would not associate

versets.ar: وَلَا تَسُبُّوا الَّذِينَ يَدْعُونَ مِن دُونِ اللَّهِ فَيَسُبُّوا اللَّهَ عَدْوًا بِغَيْرِ عِلْمٍ ۗ كَذَٰلِكَ زَيَّنَّا لِكُلِّ أُمَّةٍ عَمَلَهُمْ ثُمَّ إِلَىٰ رَبِّهِم مَّرْجِعُهُمْ فَيُنَبِّئُهُم بِمَا كَانُوا يَعْمَلُونَ
versets.tr: wa-lā tasubbū lladhīna yadʿūna min dūni llāhi fa-yasubbū llāha ʿadwan bi-ghayri ʿilmin kadhālika zayyannā li-kulli ummatin ʿamalahum thumma ilā rabbihim marjiʿuhum fa-yunabbiʾuhum bi-mā kānū yaʿmalūn
versets.trad: Do not revile those they call upon besides God, lest they revile God in enmity, without knowledge. Thus have We made fair to every community its own deeds. Then to their Lord is their return, and He will inform them of what they used to do.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī teaches the measure of polemic: address them with the tongue of proof, the holding to arguments and the refuting of doubt; do not speak to them according to the leanings of the soul and of custom, which would drag them to abandon the reverence due to the Name of God. To revile their idols would provoke their blasphemy in return: truth is defended by light, not by invective.

mots.racine: ع د و / ع ل م
ز ي ن / أ م م
mots.sens: We made fair to every community its deed

versets.ar: وَأَقْسَمُوا بِاللَّهِ جَهْدَ أَيْمَانِهِمْ لَئِن جَاءَتْهُمْ آيَةٌ لَّيُؤْمِنُنَّ بِهَا ۚ قُلْ إِنَّمَا الْآيَاتُ عِندَ اللَّهِ ۖ وَمَا يُشْعِرُكُمْ أَنَّهَا إِذَا جَاءَتْ لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ
versets.tr: wa-aqsamū bi-llāhi jahda aymānihim la-in jāʾat'hum āyatun la-yuʾminunna bihā qul innamā l-āyātu ʿinda llāhi wa-mā yushʿirukum annahā idhā jāʾat lā yuʾminūn
versets.trad: They have sworn by God their most solemn oaths that if a sign came to them, they would surely believe in it. Say: “The signs are only with God.” And what makes you realize that even if it came, they would not believe?
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: they promised themselves belief should they see the proof, not knowing that they are under the grip of the decree; and of what use is the clarity of proofs to one whom the antecedents of mercy and the consequences of safekeeping have not aided, according to the requirements of the apportionment? The oath to believe “if” is but an alibi: the sign does not convert the heart that grace has not preceded.

mots.racine: ج ه د / ي م ن

versets.ar: وَنُقَلِّبُ أَفْئِدَتَهُمْ وَأَبْصَارَهُمْ كَمَا لَمْ يُؤْمِنُوا بِهِ أَوَّلَ مَرَّةٍ وَنَذَرُهُمْ فِي طُغْيَانِهِمْ يَعْمَهُونَ
versets.tr: wa-nuqallibu afʾidatahum wa-abṣārahum kamā lam yuʾminū bihi awwala marratin wa-nadharuhum fī ṭughyānihim yaʿmahūn
versets.trad: And We turn away their hearts and their eyes, as they did not believe in it the first time, and We leave them to wander blindly in their excess.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī meditates here the mystery of the Decree (qadar): one of the realities of the turning (taqlīb) is precisely that this affair — manifest as it is — remains obscure to the hearts of those very ones who are counted among the intelligent. Glory to Him who veils this thing despite its evidence! Such is the domination of the Almighty and the decree of the One. That the evident remains hidden from the lucid is the sign of a sovereignty that surpasses reason.

mots.racine: ق ل ب / ف أ د
mots.def: qallaba: to turn over; afʾida: hearts.

mots.ar: كَمَا لَمْ يُؤْمِنُوا بِهِ أَوَّلَ مَرَّةٍ
mots.tr: kamā lam yuʾminū bihi awwala marra
mots.sens: as they did not believe the first time

mots.racine: ط غ ي / ع م ه
mots.def: ṭughyān: rebellion; ʿamiha: to wander blind.

versets.ar: وَلَوْ أَنَّنَا نَزَّلْنَا إِلَيْهِمُ الْمَلَائِكَةَ وَكَلَّمَهُمُ الْمَوْتَىٰ وَحَشَرْنَا عَلَيْهِمْ كُلَّ شَيْءٍ قُبُلًا مَّا كَانُوا لِيُؤْمِنُوا إِلَّا أَن يَشَاءَ اللَّهُ وَلَٰكِنَّ أَكْثَرَهُمْ يَجْهَلُونَ
versets.tr: wa-law annanā nazzalnā ilayhimu l-malāʾikata wa-kallamahumu l-mawtā wa-ḥasharnā ʿalayhim kulla shayʾin qubulan mā kānū li-yuʾminū illā an yashāʾa llāhu wa-lākinna aktharahum yajhalūn
versets.trad: Even had We sent down the angels to them, and the dead had spoken to them, and We had gathered before them all things face to face, they would not have believed unless God willed. But most of them are ignorant.
themes: faith bound to the divine will
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: for though the signs follow one upon another, though the suns of proof rise high, whomever Glory has broken and the apportionment has crushed draws from it only perplexity and straying, and draws for himself only an increase of wretchedness. Angels descended, the dead speaking, marvels gathered: nothing bends the heart that God does not incline. Faith is gift, not conclusion.

mots.racine: ن ز ل / م ل ك
mots.sens: We sent down the angels to them

mots.racine: ك ل م / م و ت
mots.sens: the dead would speak to them

versets.ar: وَكَذَٰلِكَ جَعَلْنَا لِكُلِّ نَبِيٍّ عَدُوًّا شَيَاطِينَ الْإِنسِ وَالْجِنِّ يُوحِي بَعْضُهُمْ إِلَىٰ بَعْضٍ زُخْرُفَ الْقَوْلِ غُرُورًا ۚ وَلَوْ شَاءَ رَبُّكَ مَا فَعَلُوهُ ۖ فَذَرْهُمْ وَمَا يَفْتَرُونَ
versets.tr: wa-kadhālika jaʿalnā li-kulli nabiyyin ʿaduwwan shayāṭīna l-insi wa-l-jinni yūḥī baʿḍuhum ilā baʿḍin zukhrufa l-qawli ghurūran wa-law shāʾa rabbuka mā faʿalūhu fa-dharhum wa-mā yaftarūn
versets.trad: And thus have We made for every prophet an enemy: devils of mankind and the jinn, inspiring one another with the glitter of speech in delusion. Had your Lord willed, they would not have done it. So leave them and what they fabricate.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī, on the price of elevation: the higher the station, the fuller the trials and the stronger the demands; therefore, because the ranks of the prophets are the noblest, the enmity toward them was the harshest and the most grievous. The “glitter of speech” (zukhruf al-qawl) is the devils' weapon: a falsehood that imitates the beauty of truth the better to lead astray.

mots.racine: ش ط ن / أ ن س / ج ن ن
ز خ ر ف / غ ر ر
و ذ ر / ف ر ي
mots.def: dhara: to leave; iftarā: to forge.

versets.ar: وَلِتَصْغَىٰ إِلَيْهِ أَفْئِدَةُ الَّذِينَ لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ بِالْآخِرَةِ وَلِيَرْضَوْهُ وَلِيَقْتَرِفُوا مَا هُم مُّقْتَرِفُونَ
versets.tr: wa-li-taṣghā ilayhi afʾidatu lladhīna lā yuʾminūna bi-l-ākhirati wa-li-yarḍawhu wa-li-yaqtarifū mā hum muqtarifūn
versets.trad: [We did this] so that the hearts of those who do not believe in the hereafter might incline to it, and be content with it, and earn what they are earning.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī, in a single stroke: the hearing of the disbelievers was committed to vanity and their hearts to evil; thus they accepted for themselves the basest share. To incline (ṣaghā) toward the deceitful glitter, to be content with it, to reap its fruit: such is the slope of the heart closed to the hereafter, which chooses the perishable ornament over the abiding truth.

mots.racine: ص غ و / ف أ د
mots.sens: that hearts may incline to it

versets.ar: أَفَغَيْرَ اللَّهِ أَبْتَغِي حَكَمًا وَهُوَ الَّذِي أَنزَلَ إِلَيْكُمُ الْكِتَابَ مُفَصَّلًا ۚ وَالَّذِينَ آتَيْنَاهُمُ الْكِتَابَ يَعْلَمُونَ أَنَّهُ مُنَزَّلٌ مِّن رَّبِّكَ بِالْحَقِّ ۖ فَلَا تَكُونَنَّ مِنَ الْمُمْتَرِينَ
versets.tr: a-fa-ghayra llāhi abtaghī ḥakaman wa-huwa lladhī anzala ilaykumu l-kitāba mufaṣṣalan wa-lladhīna ātaynāhumu l-kitāba yaʿlamūna annahu munazzalun min rabbika bi-l-ḥaqqi fa-lā takūnanna mina l-mumtarīn
versets.trad: Shall I seek a judge other than God, when it is He who has sent down to you the Book, fully detailed? Those to whom We gave the Book know that it is sent down from your Lord in truth. So be not among the doubters!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: say to them: do you suppose that, after the radiance of the evidence and the clarity of the proof, I would abandon certainty for conjecture, forsake the Real to cling to a caprice? That is impossible in thought. When the Book has come down “fully detailed” (mufaṣṣal), to seek another arbiter would be to forsake the light for the shadow of doubt.

mots.racine: ب غ ي / ح ك م
mots.sens: shall I seek a judge other than God

versets.ar: وَتَمَّتْ كَلِمَتُ رَبِّكَ صِدْقًا وَعَدْلًا ۚ لَّا مُبَدِّلَ لِكَلِمَاتِهِ ۚ وَهُوَ السَّمِيعُ الْعَلِيمُ
versets.tr: wa-tammat kalimatu rabbika ṣidqan wa-ʿadlan lā mubaddila li-kalimātihi wa-huwa s-samīʿu l-ʿalīm
versets.trad: The Word of your Lord is fulfilled in truth and justice. There is no changing His words. He is the All-Hearing, the All-Knowing.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: His Essence is sanctified from all mutation, His attributes purified from all change; fulfillment (tamām) excludes deficiency, and all deficiency has its source in the contingent — but how should deficiency reach Him whose attribute is Priority? The Word of God, fulfilled in truth (ṣidq) and justice (ʿadl), admits no retouching: it is perfect because eternal.

mots.racine: ت م م / ك ل م
mots.sens: the Word of your Lord is fulfilled
mots.def: tamma: to be complete; kalima: word, decree.

mots.racine: ص د ق / ع د ل

versets.ar: وَإِن تُطِعْ أَكْثَرَ مَن فِي الْأَرْضِ يُضِلُّوكَ عَن سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ ۚ إِن يَتَّبِعُونَ إِلَّا الظَّنَّ وَإِنْ هُمْ إِلَّا يَخْرُصُونَ
versets.tr: wa-in tuṭiʿ akthara man fī l-arḍi yuḍillūka ʿan sabīli llāhi in yattabiʿūna illā ẓ-ẓanna wa-in hum illā yakhruṣūn
versets.trad: If you obey most of those on the earth, they will lead you astray from the path of God. They follow only conjecture, and they only guess.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the people of God are few in number, even if they are countless in weight and worth; as for the adversaries, they are the multitude. If you regard them, O Muḥammad, they will seduce you; if you keep their company, they will turn you from the Real and overturn you. Number is no argument: truth is not counted, it is weighed.

mots.sens: most of those on the earth

mots.racine: ض ل ل / س ب ل
mots.sens: they lead you astray from the path of God

mots.racine: ت ب ع / ظ ن ن

versets.ar: إِنَّ رَبَّكَ هُوَ أَعْلَمُ مَن يَضِلُّ عَن سَبِيلِهِ ۖ وَهُوَ أَعْلَمُ بِالْمُهْتَدِينَ
versets.tr: inna rabbaka huwa aʿlamu man yaḍillu ʿan sabīlihi wa-huwa aʿlamu bi-l-muhtadīn
versets.trad: Truly your Lord knows best who strays from His path, and He knows best the rightly guided.
themes: the knowledge of the strayed and the guided
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the sciences of the creatures fall too short to attain His unseen, save in the measure He informs them of it; and the One to whom nothing is hidden is the One, glory be to Him. God alone knows who strays and who is guided, for He alone sees the hidden root of hearts: guidance and straying are not judged from without.

mots.racine: ع ل م / ض ل ل

versets.ar: فَكُلُوا مِمَّا ذُكِرَ اسْمُ اللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ إِن كُنتُم بِآيَاتِهِ مُؤْمِنِينَ
versets.tr: fa-kulū mimmā dhukira smu llāhi ʿalayhi in kuntum bi-āyātihi muʾminīn
versets.trad: So eat of that over which the name of God has been invoked, if you are believers in His signs.
themes: eating in the Name of God
versets.commentaire: In the literal sense, the verse concerns the slaughtered beast. But Qushayrī reads in it a sign: an indication against eating in heedlessness; for whoever eats in the forgetfulness of God, as long as that food remains in him, his thoughts are but the suspicions of the soul or the whisperings of the devil. To invoke the Name over what one takes is to sanctify the most everyday act by the presence of the heart.

mots.racine: ذ ك ر / س م و
mots.sens: over which the Name of God was pronounced

versets.ar: وَمَا لَكُمْ أَلَّا تَأْكُلُوا مِمَّا ذُكِرَ اسْمُ اللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ وَقَدْ فَصَّلَ لَكُم مَّا حَرَّمَ عَلَيْكُمْ إِلَّا مَا اضْطُرِرْتُمْ إِلَيْهِ ۗ وَإِنَّ كَثِيرًا لَّيُضِلُّونَ بِأَهْوَائِهِم بِغَيْرِ عِلْمٍ ۗ إِنَّ رَبَّكَ هُوَ أَعْلَمُ بِالْمُعْتَدِينَ
versets.tr: wa-mā lakum allā taʾkulū mimmā dhukira smu llāhi ʿalayhi wa-qad faṣṣala lakum mā ḥarrama ʿalaykum illā mā ḍṭurirtum ilayhi wa-inna kathīran la-yuḍillūna bi-ahwāʾihim bi-ghayri ʿilmin inna rabbaka huwa aʿlamu bi-l-muʿtadīn
versets.trad: And what is the matter with you that you do not eat of that over which the name of God has been invoked, when He has made clear to you what He has forbidden you, except what you are compelled to? Many indeed lead others astray by their passions, without knowledge. Truly your Lord knows best the transgressors.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī, extending the inner sense: what harm would there be for you in forsaking heedlessness? what would it cost you to remain in remembrance (dhikr)? The difference between the intimacy of remembrance and the desolation of forgetting is now manifest to you in the present instant — so know too the verdict of reward and chastisement in the hereafter. The detail of the licit and the illicit forms the soul to vigilance.

mots.sens: what is the matter with you that you do not eat

mots.racine: ف ص ل / ح ر م

mots.sens: except what you are compelled to
mots.def: uḍṭurra: to be forced by necessity.

versets.ar: وَذَرُوا ظَاهِرَ الْإِثْمِ وَبَاطِنَهُ ۚ إِنَّ الَّذِينَ يَكْسِبُونَ الْإِثْمَ سَيُجْزَوْنَ بِمَا كَانُوا يَقْتَرِفُونَ
versets.tr: wa-dharū ẓāhira l-ithmi wa-bāṭinahu inna lladhīna yaksibūna l-ithma sa-yujzawna bi-mā kānū yaqtarifūn
versets.trad: Forsake sin, outward and inward. Those who earn sin shall be requited for what they used to commit.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī unfolds a vertiginous ladder of “inward sin”: outward sin is what others have knowledge of; inward sin is a secret between you and God, which no creature could surprise. It is also said: inward sin, on the tongue of the people of knowledge, is to close one's eyes upon that wherein your ego has its share; and on the tongue of the people of love, it is to fail ceaselessly the demands of love — for this love is built upon reproach and constraint. And he cites: when I say “what fault have I committed?”, she answers: your very life is a fault to which no fault compares.

mots.racine: و ذ ر / ظ ه ر / أ ث م

versets.ar: وَلَا تَأْكُلُوا مِمَّا لَمْ يُذْكَرِ اسْمُ اللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ وَإِنَّهُ لَفِسْقٌ ۗ وَإِنَّ الشَّيَاطِينَ لَيُوحُونَ إِلَىٰ أَوْلِيَائِهِمْ لِيُجَادِلُوكُمْ ۖ وَإِنْ أَطَعْتُمُوهُمْ إِنَّكُمْ لَمُشْرِكُونَ
versets.tr: wa-lā taʾkulū mimmā lam yudhkari smu llāhi ʿalayhi wa-innahu la-fisqun wa-inna sh-shayāṭīna la-yūḥūna ilā awliyāʾihim li-yujādilūkum wa-in aṭaʿtumūhum innakum la-mushrikūn
versets.trad: And do not eat of that over which the name of God has not been invoked; that is indeed a perversion. The devils inspire their adherents to dispute with you; and if you obey them, you are indeed associators.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: this shows that whoever guards himself from such a thing sees his thoughts unified for God, and the whisperings of the devil cut off from him; for the root of all hardness is the following of passions, and whoever grows accustomed to following them, let him bid farewell to the purity of the heart. To eat without the Name is to open the door to satanic whispering; to obey the agents of falsehood is to slip into shirk.

mots.ar: مَا لَمْ يُذْكَرِ اسْمُ اللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ
mots.tr: mā lam yudhkari smu llāhi ʿalayh
mots.sens: over which the Name of God was not pronounced

mots.racine: ش ط ن / و ح ي / و ل ي

versets.ar: أَوَمَن كَانَ مَيْتًا فَأَحْيَيْنَاهُ وَجَعَلْنَا لَهُ نُورًا يَمْشِي بِهِ فِي النَّاسِ كَمَن مَّثَلُهُ فِي الظُّلُمَاتِ لَيْسَ بِخَارِجٍ مِّنْهَا ۚ كَذَٰلِكَ زُيِّنَ لِلْكَافِرِينَ مَا كَانُوا يَعْمَلُونَ
versets.tr: a-wa-man kāna maytan fa-aḥyaynāhu wa-jaʿalnā lahu nūran yamshī bihi fī n-nāsi ka-man mathaluhu fī ẓ-ẓulumāti laysa bi-khārijin minhā kadhālika zuyyina li-l-kāfirīna mā kānū yaʿmalūn
versets.trad: Is one who was dead, and We gave him life and a light by which he walks among men, like one who is in the darknesses, from which he cannot emerge? Thus has been made fair to the disbelievers what they used to do.
versets.commentaire: A sumptuous verse on the life of the heart. Qushayrī: faith, among these people, is the life of the heart by God. The people of heedlessness, when they reach remembrance (dhikr), become living after having been dead; and the masters of remembrance, if forgetting overtakes them, die after having lived. The one who is in the lights of nearness, beneath the radiance of gnosis and in the spirit of insight, can never be the equal of one who languishes captive of the darknesses.

mots.racine: م و ت / ح ي ي
mots.sens: he was dead, We gave him life
mots.def: aḥyā: to give life, to revive.

mots.racine: ن و ر / م ش ي
mots.sens: a light by which he walks

mots.racine: م ث ل / ظ ل م

versets.ar: وَكَذَٰلِكَ جَعَلْنَا فِي كُلِّ قَرْيَةٍ أَكَابِرَ مُجْرِمِيهَا لِيَمْكُرُوا فِيهَا ۖ وَمَا يَمْكُرُونَ إِلَّا بِأَنفُسِهِمْ وَمَا يَشْعُرُونَ
versets.tr: wa-kadhālika jaʿalnā fī kulli qaryatin akābira mujrimīhā li-yamkurū fīhā wa-mā yamkurūna illā bi-anfusihim wa-mā yashʿurūn
versets.trad: And thus have We placed in every city its great criminals to scheme therein; but they scheme only against themselves, without realizing.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: We confounded for them the realities of tawḥīd, and their conjectures led them to believe they possessed some parcel of effacement and affirmation; thus they plunged in, thinking to scheme, while in truth they are the deceived — and they shall know, when no knowledge avails them. To believe one is conducting the ruse when one is conducted: such is the illusion of the “great” of this world.

mots.racine: ك ب ر / ج ر م
م ك ر / ن ف س

versets.ar: وَإِذَا جَاءَتْهُمْ آيَةٌ قَالُوا لَن نُّؤْمِنَ حَتَّىٰ نُؤْتَىٰ مِثْلَ مَا أُوتِيَ رُسُلُ اللَّهِ ۘ اللَّهُ أَعْلَمُ حَيْثُ يَجْعَلُ رِسَالَتَهُ ۗ سَيُصِيبُ الَّذِينَ أَجْرَمُوا صَغَارٌ عِندَ اللَّهِ وَعَذَابٌ شَدِيدٌ بِمَا كَانُوا يَمْكُرُونَ
versets.tr: wa-idhā jāʾat'hum āyatun qālū lan nuʾmina ḥattā nuʾtā mithla mā ūtiya rusulu llāhi llāhu aʿlamu ḥaythu yajʿalu risālatahu sa-yuṣību lladhīna ajramū ṣaghārun ʿinda llāhi wa-ʿadhābun shadīdun bi-mā kānū yamkurūn
versets.trad: And when a sign comes to them, they say: “We will not believe until we are given the like of what God's messengers were given.” God knows best where to place His message. Humiliation from God and a severe chastisement shall strike the criminals for their scheming.
themes: God knows where to place His message
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: after the removal of every pretext, the exposition of the proof and the dispelling of doubt, to take refuge in demanding a surplus of insight is the confession of bad conduct; it is, on their part, a wish to equal him who came by legitimate title, while coming by some suggestion of the soul. To want prophethood for oneself is the ego's pretension; but the risāla, the message, God alone knows to which heart to entrust it.

mots.racine: أ م ن / أ ت ي
mots.sens: we will not believe until we are given

mots.racine: ع ل م / ر س ل
mots.sens: God knows where to place His message

versets.ar: فَمَن يُرِدِ اللَّهُ أَن يَهْدِيَهُ يَشْرَحْ صَدْرَهُ لِلْإِسْلَامِ ۖ وَمَن يُرِدْ أَن يُضِلَّهُ يَجْعَلْ صَدْرَهُ ضَيِّقًا حَرَجًا كَأَنَّمَا يَصَّعَّدُ فِي السَّمَاءِ ۚ كَذَٰلِكَ يَجْعَلُ اللَّهُ الرِّجْسَ عَلَى الَّذِينَ لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ
versets.tr: fa-man yuridi llāhu an yahdiyahu yashraḥ ṣadrahu li-l-islāmi wa-man yurid an yuḍillahu yajʿal ṣadrahu ḍayyiqan ḥarajan ka-annamā yaṣṣaʿʿadu fī s-samāʾi kadhālika yajʿalu llāhu r-rijsa ʿalā lladhīna lā yuʾminūn
versets.trad: Whomever God wishes to guide, He expands his breast to submission (al-islām). And whomever He wishes to lead astray, He makes his breast tight and constricted, as if he were climbing into the sky. Thus does God lay defilement upon those who do not believe.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī unfolds here his famous hierarchy of the lights: a light at the beginning, which is the light of the intellect; a light in the intermediate degrees, which is the light of knowledge; a light at the term, which is the light of gnosis (ʿirfān). The man of intellect lives with the proof, the man of knowledge with the explication, the man of gnosis in the regime of direct vision. And he describes the extinction of the contemplator: as one who fixes the disk of the sun sees the light of his sight absorbed into the solar ray, so the lights of insight are extinguished in the realities of contemplation; then the servant subsists by Existence without vision, then is extinguished entirely, and the Oneness remains in perpetuity. The breast God “expands” (sharḥ al-ṣadr) is dilated by light; the one He “tightens” suffocates like one who exhausts himself climbing to the sky.

mots.racine: ش ر ح / ص د ر
mots.sens: He expands his breast to islām
mots.def: sharaḥa: to open, to widen; ṣadr: breast.

mots.racine: ض ي ق / ح ر ج

mots.sens: as if climbing into the sky

versets.ar: وَهَٰذَا صِرَاطُ رَبِّكَ مُسْتَقِيمًا ۗ قَدْ فَصَّلْنَا الْآيَاتِ لِقَوْمٍ يَذَّكَّرُونَ
versets.tr: wa-hādhā ṣirāṭu rabbika mustaqīman qad faṣṣalnā l-āyāti li-qawmin yadhdhakkarūn
versets.trad: And this is the path of your Lord, straight. We have detailed the signs for a people who remember.
themes: the straight path of the Lord
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī gives here his deepest definition of the ṣirāṭ mustaqīm: the straight path is the establishment of servanthood (ʿubūdiyya) in the realization of Lordship: a discernment (farq) supported by union (jamʿ), and a union bound to the Law; the affirmation of gnosis to the utmost of capacity, the distancing from transgressions to the height of effort, and the realization that the Conductor is One, without associate. Then: the abandonment of every support — to lean neither on one's movements nor one's stillnesses, but to await what the Decree will open; for if the man of rectitude deviates an instant and turns right or left, he falls a fall from which he does not rise.

mots.racine: ص ر ط / ق و م
mots.sens: the path of your Lord, straight

mots.def: faṣṣala: to set forth in detail.

versets.ar: لَهُمْ دَارُ السَّلَامِ عِندَ رَبِّهِمْ ۖ وَهُوَ وَلِيُّهُم بِمَا كَانُوا يَعْمَلُونَ
versets.tr: lahum dāru s-salāmi ʿinda rabbihim wa-huwa waliyyuhum bi-mā kānū yaʿmalūn
versets.trad: For them is the Abode of Peace with their Lord, and He is their Protector by reason of what they used to do.
versets.commentaire: A summit verse of Qushayrī's commentary. The Abode of Peace (dār al-salām) is the abode of safety; and whoever is captive of some coveting or some creature finds no safety — only the one who has freed himself from the bondage of beings finds it: the verse indicates that these people are in the Garden without being prisoners of the Garden, freed from the servitude of all created things. And on “He is their Friend” (walī), he launches into a litany: He is their friend here and in the hereafter, their friend whose story has seized their hearts so as to leave no room for any other; their friend who has ravished them from every part and portion, raised a veil between them and every near one, every intimate, freeing them from every described, sought or beloved object; their friend, the Host of their secrets, the contemplation where their gazes are gathered, the Presence where their spirits pasture; their friend besides whom they have no other, whom alone they contemplate, whom alone they find — neither in their beginning do they aim at another, nor in their term do they find another.

mots.racine: د و ر / س ل م

versets.ar: وَيَوْمَ يَحْشُرُهُمْ جَمِيعًا يَا مَعْشَرَ الْجِنِّ قَدِ اسْتَكْثَرْتُم مِّنَ الْإِنسِ ۖ وَقَالَ أَوْلِيَاؤُهُم مِّنَ الْإِنسِ رَبَّنَا اسْتَمْتَعَ بَعْضُنَا بِبَعْضٍ وَبَلَغْنَا أَجَلَنَا الَّذِي أَجَّلْتَ لَنَا ۚ قَالَ النَّارُ مَثْوَاكُمْ خَالِدِينَ فِيهَا إِلَّا مَا شَاءَ اللَّهُ ۗ إِنَّ رَبَّكَ حَكِيمٌ عَلِيمٌ
versets.tr: wa-yawma yaḥshuruhum jamīʿan yā maʿshara l-jinni qadi stakthartum mina l-insi wa-qāla awliyāʾuhum mina l-insi rabbanā stamtaʿa baʿḍunā bi-baʿḍin wa-balaghnā ajalanā lladhī ajjalta lanā qāla n-nāru mathwākum khālidīna fīhā illā mā shāʾa llāhu inna rabbaka ḥakīmun ʿalīm
versets.trad: And on the Day He gathers them all: “O assembly of jinn! You took many of mankind!” And their adherents among mankind will say: “Our Lord! We profited from one another, and we have reached our term which You appointed for us.” He will say: “The Fire is your dwelling, abiding therein forever, except as God wills.” Truly your Lord is Wise, All-Knowing.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: they excuse themselves, but none hears them; they argue, but to no profit. Once, had they offered less than that, it would have been accepted of them; but the eternal apportionment preceded, and wretchedness was their due. The “reached” term rings here as an end without recourse: the hour of repentance was in the delay, not at the moment of the verdict.

mots.racine: ع ش ر / ج ن ن
ن و ر / ث و ي

versets.ar: وَكَذَٰلِكَ نُوَلِّي بَعْضَ الظَّالِمِينَ بَعْضًا بِمَا كَانُوا يَكْسِبُونَ
versets.tr: wa-kadhālika nuwallī baʿḍa ẓ-ẓālimīna baʿḍan bi-mā kānū yaksibūn
versets.trad: And thus do We give some of the unjust authority over others, for what they used to earn.
themes: the unjust delivered to one another
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: We gather the like together: the friends (of God) are gathered, enjoying one another; the enemies are gathered, fleeing one another. Affinity gathers: the unjust find themselves under the sway of the unjust, a chastisement that springs from their own nature — each reaps his likes.

mots.racine: و ل ي / ظ ل م
mots.sens: We set the unjust over one another
mots.def: wallā: to set in authority over.

mots.sens: for what they used to earn

versets.ar: يَا مَعْشَرَ الْجِنِّ وَالْإِنسِ أَلَمْ يَأْتِكُمْ رُسُلٌ مِّنكُمْ يَقُصُّونَ عَلَيْكُمْ آيَاتِي وَيُنذِرُونَكُمْ لِقَاءَ يَوْمِكُمْ هَٰذَا ۚ قَالُوا شَهِدْنَا عَلَىٰ أَنفُسِنَا ۖ وَغَرَّتْهُمُ الْحَيَاةُ الدُّنْيَا وَشَهِدُوا عَلَىٰ أَنفُسِهِمْ أَنَّهُمْ كَانُوا كَافِرِينَ
versets.tr: yā maʿshara l-jinni wa-l-insi a-lam yaʾtikum rusulun minkum yaquṣṣūna ʿalaykum āyātī wa-yundhirūnakum liqāʾa yawmikum hādhā qālū shahidnā ʿalā anfusinā wa-gharrat'humu l-ḥayātu d-dunyā wa-shahidū ʿalā anfusihim annahum kānū kāfirīn
versets.trad: “O assembly of jinn and mankind! Did not messengers come to you from among you, recounting to you My signs and warning you of the meeting of this your Day?” They will say: “We bear witness against ourselves.” The life of this world deceived them, and they will bear witness against themselves that they were disbelievers.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: He makes known to them that He removed from them every pretext by the holding to proof; but He decreed for them wretchedness in pre-eternity, and then veiled from them the high road. Summoned on the last Day, the strayed can only testify against themselves: the deception of the world (ghurūr al-dunyā) has blinded them, and the avowal comes too late.

mots.racine: ر س ل / ق ص ص
mots.def: qaṣṣa: to set forth, to recount.

mots.racine: ش ه د / ن ف س

mots.sens: the life of this world deceived them

versets.ar: ذَٰلِكَ أَن لَّمْ يَكُن رَّبُّكَ مُهْلِكَ الْقُرَىٰ بِظُلْمٍ وَأَهْلُهَا غَافِلُونَ
versets.tr: dhālika an lam yakun rabbuka muhlika l-qurā bi-ẓulmin wa-ahluhā ghāfilūn
versets.trad: That is because your Lord would not destroy cities unjustly while their people were heedless.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: how should the imagination of injustice apply to His description, when the sovereignty is His sovereignty and the creation His creation? how should it be shameful of Him to dispose of a being as He wills, when the servant is His servant and the judgment His judgment? God never annihilates without prior warning: His justice sends first the light, then only the sanction.

mots.racine: ه ل ك / ق ر ي / ظ ل م

versets.ar: وَلِكُلٍّ دَرَجَاتٌ مِّمَّا عَمِلُوا ۚ وَمَا رَبُّكَ بِغَافِلٍ عَمَّا يَعْمَلُونَ
versets.tr: wa-li-kullin darajātun mimmā ʿamilū wa-mā rabbuka bi-ghāfilin ʿammā yaʿmalūn
versets.trad: And for all there are degrees according to what they did. And your Lord is not heedless of what they do.
versets.commentaire: With perfect concision, Qushayrī: the good-doer is, in the spirit of reward, immersed in delight; the sinner is, in the lamentation of chastisement, overwhelmed with pain. The “degrees” (darajāt) are no external ranking: they are the inmost measure of each soul, the very fruit of what it has built.

mots.sens: not heedless of what they do

versets.ar: وَرَبُّكَ الْغَنِيُّ ذُو الرَّحْمَةِ ۚ إِن يَشَأْ يُذْهِبْكُمْ وَيَسْتَخْلِفْ مِن بَعْدِكُم مَّا يَشَاءُ كَمَا أَنشَأَكُم مِّن ذُرِّيَّةِ قَوْمٍ آخَرِينَ
versets.tr: wa-rabbuka l-ghaniyyu dhū r-raḥmati in yashaʾ yudhhibkum wa-yastakhlif min baʿdikum mā yashāʾu kamā anshaʾakum min dhurriyyati qawmin ākharīn
versets.trad: And your Lord is the Self-Sufficient, the Master of Mercy. If He wills, He can remove you and bring after you whom He wills as successors, as He brought you forth from the offspring of another people.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī holds the two Names together: “the Self-Sufficient” (al-Ghaniyy) points to His majestic unveiling, “Master of Mercy” to His gentleness; by His majesty He unveils them and confounds them, by His largesse He caresses them and revives them. And this pearl on ecstatic audition: to hear His Self-Sufficiency imposes effacement (maḥw); to hear His Mercy imposes the return to self (ṣaḥw): between subsistence and annihilation, between honor and engulfment, the heart oscillates.

mots.racine: غ ن ي / ر ح م
mots.def: ghaniyy: who needs nothing; raḥma: mercy.
adhhaba: to take away, to remove.

versets.ar: إِنَّ مَا تُوعَدُونَ لَآتٍ ۖ وَمَا أَنتُم بِمُعْجِزِينَ
versets.tr: inna mā tūʿadūna la-ātin wa-mā antum bi-muʿjizīn
versets.trad: What you are promised is surely coming, and you cannot thwart it.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the indication of this verse is to the shortening of hope (qiṣar al-amal); and whoever shortens his hope beautifies his work, for all that is to come has its term near. To know the promise inescapable and the term imminent is to live in vigilance: the soul that knows itself mortal acts with rightness.

mots.racine: و ع د / أ ت ي
mots.sens: what you are promised is coming

mots.def: aʿjaza: to render powerless, to foil.

versets.ar: قُلْ يَا قَوْمِ اعْمَلُوا عَلَىٰ مَكَانَتِكُمْ إِنِّي عَامِلٌ ۖ فَسَوْفَ تَعْلَمُونَ مَن تَكُونُ لَهُ عَاقِبَةُ الدَّارِ ۗ إِنَّهُ لَا يُفْلِحُ الظَّالِمُونَ
versets.tr: qul yā qawmi ʿmalū ʿalā makānatikum innī ʿāmilun fa-sawfa taʿlamūna man takūnu lahu ʿāqibatu d-dāri innahu lā yufliḥu ẓ-ẓālimūn
versets.trad: Say: “O my people! Act according to your ability; I too am acting. You will come to know to whom belongs the final abode. Truly the unjust shall not prosper.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: this is the height of dissuasion, for it is a threat, even if cast in the form of a command. “Act in your way, I act in mine” is no concession but a summons: time itself will decide, and the outcome of the abode (ʿāqibat al-dār) will reveal who was in the truth.

mots.racine: ع م ل / ك و ن
ع ق ب / د و ر
mots.def: ʿāqiba: end; dār: the final abode.

mots.racine: ف ل ح / ظ ل م

versets.ar: وَجَعَلُوا لِلَّهِ مِمَّا ذَرَأَ مِنَ الْحَرْثِ وَالْأَنْعَامِ نَصِيبًا فَقَالُوا هَٰذَا لِلَّهِ بِزَعْمِهِمْ وَهَٰذَا لِشُرَكَائِنَا ۖ فَمَا كَانَ لِشُرَكَائِهِمْ فَلَا يَصِلُ إِلَى اللَّهِ ۖ وَمَا كَانَ لِلَّهِ فَهُوَ يَصِلُ إِلَىٰ شُرَكَائِهِمْ ۗ سَاءَ مَا يَحْكُمُونَ
versets.tr: wa-jaʿalū li-llāhi mimmā dharaʾa mina l-ḥarthi wa-l-anʿāmi naṣīban fa-qālū hādhā li-llāhi bi-zaʿmihim wa-hādhā li-shurakāʾinā fa-mā kāna li-shurakāʾihim fa-lā yaṣilu ilā llāhi wa-mā kāna li-llāhi fa-huwa yaṣilu ilā shurakāʾihim sāʾa mā yaḥkumūn
versets.trad: They assign to God a share of the crops and cattle that He Himself has produced in abundance, and they say: “This is for God” — so they claim — “and this is for our associates.” But what is for their associates does not reach God, while what is for God does reach their associates. How evil is their judgment!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: having built the foundation of their affair upon the demand of passion, their branches accorded with their roots. The partition they invent — a share for God, a share for their idols, and the fraud that always tilts toward the idols — betrays the corruption of their principle: a worship founded on desire begets iniquitous rules.

mots.racine: ذ ر أ / ح ر ث / ن ع م
mots.sens: of what He produced in crops and cattle

mots.racine: س و أ / ح ك م
mots.def: sāʾa: to be detestable; ḥakama: to judge.

versets.ar: وَكَذَٰلِكَ زَيَّنَ لِكَثِيرٍ مِّنَ الْمُشْرِكِينَ قَتْلَ أَوْلَادِهِمْ شُرَكَاؤُهُمْ لِيُرْدُوهُمْ وَلِيَلْبِسُوا عَلَيْهِمْ دِينَهُمْ ۖ وَلَوْ شَاءَ اللَّهُ مَا فَعَلُوهُ ۖ فَذَرْهُمْ وَمَا يَفْتَرُونَ
versets.tr: wa-kadhālika zayyana li-kathīrin mina l-mushrikīna qatla awlādihim shurakāʾuhum li-yurdūhum wa-li-yalbisū ʿalayhim dīnahum wa-law shāʾa llāhu mā faʿalūhu fa-dharhum wa-mā yaftarūn
versets.trad: And in the same way, their associates have made fair to many of the associators the killing of their children, to ruin them and to obscure their religion. Had God willed, they would not have done it. So leave them and what they fabricate.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: their devils whispered to them falsehood, and their souls received it, for the like give one another aid; the soul calls only to the stranger (to God), for it is pretentious and imagines itself to be something — and the root of all association is this pretension (daʿwā). The ego that thinks itself autonomous is the first of associates: to kill one's own children for fear of want is the extreme fruit of this self-idolatry.

mots.racine: ز ي ن / ق ت ل
mots.sens: made fair the killing of their children

mots.racine: ل ب س / د ي ن

versets.ar: وَقَالُوا هَٰذِهِ أَنْعَامٌ وَحَرْثٌ حِجْرٌ لَّا يَطْعَمُهَا إِلَّا مَن نَّشَاءُ بِزَعْمِهِمْ وَأَنْعَامٌ حُرِّمَتْ ظُهُورُهَا وَأَنْعَامٌ لَّا يَذْكُرُونَ اسْمَ اللَّهِ عَلَيْهَا افْتِرَاءً عَلَيْهِ ۚ سَيَجْزِيهِم بِمَا كَانُوا يَفْتَرُونَ
versets.tr: wa-qālū hādhihi anʿāmun wa-ḥarthun ḥijrun lā yaṭʿamuhā illā man nashāʾu bi-zaʿmihim wa-anʿāmun ḥurrimat ẓuhūruhā wa-anʿāmun lā yadhkurūna sma llāhi ʿalayhā ftirāʾan ʿalayhi sa-yajzīhim bi-mā kānū yaftarūn
versets.trad: And they say: “These cattle and crops are forbidden; none may eat them but whom we wish” — so they claim — “and these are cattle whose backs are forbidden,” and cattle over which they do not pronounce the name of God, inventing against Him. He will requite them for what they invented.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: He reports things they have innovated as they wished, rules they have instituted according to their custom, then ascribed to the Real with no proof, legislated with no warrant from a messenger. And he warns: whoever imitates them in adding or subtracting something from the Law of the Muslims, sanctioning thereby their vanity, is enrolled in their cohort of excess.

mots.racine: ن ع م / ح ر ث / ح ج ر
ح ر م / ظ ه ر

versets.ar: وَقَالُوا مَا فِي بُطُونِ هَٰذِهِ الْأَنْعَامِ خَالِصَةٌ لِّذُكُورِنَا وَمُحَرَّمٌ عَلَىٰ أَزْوَاجِنَا ۖ وَإِن يَكُن مَّيْتَةً فَهُمْ فِيهِ شُرَكَاءُ ۚ سَيَجْزِيهِمْ وَصْفَهُمْ ۚ إِنَّهُ حَكِيمٌ عَلِيمٌ
versets.tr: wa-qālū mā fī buṭūni hādhihi l-anʿāmi khāliṣatun li-dhukūrinā wa-muḥarramun ʿalā azwājinā wa-in yakun maytatan fa-hum fīhi shurakāʾu sa-yajzīhim waṣfahum innahu ḥakīmun ʿalīm
versets.trad: And they say: “What is in the wombs of these cattle is reserved for our males and forbidden to our wives.” But if it be stillborn, they all share in it. He will requite them for their describing. He is Wise, All-Knowing.
versets.commentaire: Extending the previous verse, Qushayrī ranks these decrees among the sanctioned inventions: what proceeds from the custom of men, raised against the revelation, cannot pass for worship. God, Wise (ḥakīm) and All-Knowing (ʿalīm), requites these fictions according to their just measure: the law forged by desire turns back upon its authors.

mots.racine: ب ط ن / ن ع م
mots.sens: what is in the wombs of these cattle

mots.racine: خ ل ص / ذ ك ر
ج ز ي / و ص ف
mots.sens: He will requite them for their describing

versets.ar: قَدْ خَسِرَ الَّذِينَ قَتَلُوا أَوْلَادَهُمْ سَفَهًا بِغَيْرِ عِلْمٍ وَحَرَّمُوا مَا رَزَقَهُمُ اللَّهُ افْتِرَاءً عَلَى اللَّهِ ۚ قَدْ ضَلُّوا وَمَا كَانُوا مُهْتَدِينَ
versets.tr: qad khasira lladhīna qatalū awlādahum safahan bi-ghayri ʿilmin wa-ḥarramū mā razaqahumu llāhu ftirāʾan ʿalā llāhi qad ḍallū wa-mā kānū muhtadīn
versets.trad: Lost indeed are those who killed their children in folly, without knowledge, and forbade what God provided them, inventing against God. They have gone astray and were not guided.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the way of trust in God (thiqa) was corrupted in them, and the fear of want drove them to kill their children; therefore the men of realization said: among the signs of certainty and its realities is to have a numerous family upon the carpet of trusting reliance (tawakkul). To kill out of fear of poverty is to deny that God provides: infanticide is the reverse of tawakkul.

mots.racine: ق ت ل / س ف ه
mots.sens: they killed their children in folly

mots.racine: ح ر م / ر ز ق

versets.ar: وَهُوَ الَّذِي أَنشَأَ جَنَّاتٍ مَّعْرُوشَاتٍ وَغَيْرَ مَعْرُوشَاتٍ وَالنَّخْلَ وَالزَّرْعَ مُخْتَلِفًا أُكُلُهُ وَالزَّيْتُونَ وَالرُّمَّانَ مُتَشَابِهًا وَغَيْرَ مُتَشَابِهٍ ۚ كُلُوا مِن ثَمَرِهِ إِذَا أَثْمَرَ وَآتُوا حَقَّهُ يَوْمَ حَصَادِهِ ۖ وَلَا تُسْرِفُوا ۚ إِنَّهُ لَا يُحِبُّ الْمُسْرِفِينَ
versets.tr: wa-huwa lladhī anshaʾa jannātin maʿrūshātin wa-ghayra maʿrūshātin wa-n-nakhla wa-z-zarʿa mukhtalifan ukuluhu wa-z-zaytūna wa-r-rummāna mutashābihan wa-ghayra mutashābihin kulū min thamarihi idhā athmara wa-ātū ḥaqqahu yawma ḥaṣādihi wa-lā tusrifū innahu lā yuḥibbu l-musrifīn
versets.trad: And it is He who brought forth gardens, trellised and untrellised, and the palm and the crops of diverse produce, and the olive and the pomegranate, alike and unlike. Eat of their fruit when they bear, and give its due on the day of harvest. And do not be wasteful; truly He does not love the wasteful.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī weaves the outer and inner gardens: just as He brought forth outwardly gardens and orchards, so He brought forth in the secret depths gardens and orchards; and the delight of the hearts is more complete than the gardens of the apparent: the flowers of the hearts are dazzling, the suns of the secrets resplendent, the rivers of the knowledges overflowing. And on wastefulness (isrāf): it is prodigality whatever you spend for the share of your ego, were it a grain of sesame; and what you spend in His way is not prodigality, were it to exceed thousands.

mots.racine: ج ن ن / ع ر ش
خ ل ف / أ ك ل
ح ق ق / ح ص د
mots.sens: give its due on the day of harvest

versets.ar: وَمِنَ الْأَنْعَامِ حَمُولَةً وَفَرْشًا ۚ كُلُوا مِمَّا رَزَقَكُمُ اللَّهُ وَلَا تَتَّبِعُوا خُطُوَاتِ الشَّيْطَانِ ۚ إِنَّهُ لَكُمْ عَدُوٌّ مُّبِينٌ
versets.tr: wa-mina l-anʿāmi ḥamūlatan wa-farshan kulū mimmā razaqakumu llāhu wa-lā tattabiʿū khuṭuwāti sh-shayṭāni innahu lakum ʿaduwwun mubīn
versets.trad: And of the cattle, some are for burden and some for slaughter. Eat of what God has provided you, and do not follow the footsteps of Satan; he is for you a manifest enemy.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: sustenance (rizq) is not limited to foods; it extends to all from which profit is drawn. And it divides into sustenance of the apparent and sustenance of the secrets: the first is the enjoyment of blessings, the second the contemplation of the Generosity — nay, extinction (khumūd) in the Priority. The heart has its sustenance, which is realization through gnosis; the spirit has its own, which is love; the secret has its own, which is vision (shuhūd).

mots.racine: ح م ل / ف ر ش
mots.def: ḥamūla: beasts of burden; farsh: small cattle.

mots.racine: أ ك ل / ر ز ق
mots.sens: eat of what God provided you

mots.racine: خ ط و / ش ط ن

versets.ar: ثَمَانِيَةَ أَزْوَاجٍ ۖ مِّنَ الضَّأْنِ اثْنَيْنِ وَمِنَ الْمَعْزِ اثْنَيْنِ ۗ قُلْ آلذَّكَرَيْنِ حَرَّمَ أَمِ الْأُنثَيَيْنِ أَمَّا اشْتَمَلَتْ عَلَيْهِ أَرْحَامُ الْأُنثَيَيْنِ ۖ نَبِّئُونِي بِعِلْمٍ إِن كُنتُمْ صَادِقِينَ
versets.tr: thamāniyata azwājin mina ḍ-ḍaʾni thnayni wa-mina l-maʿzi thnayni qul ā-dh-dhakarayni ḥarrama ami l-unthayayni ammā shtamalat ʿalayhi arḥāmu l-unthayayni nabbiʾūnī bi-ʿilmin in kuntum ṣādiqīn
versets.trad: Eight pairs: of the sheep two, and of the goats two. Say: “Is it the two males He has forbidden, or the two females, or what the wombs of the two females contain? Inform me with knowledge, if you are truthful.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī draws a lesson of conduct from the sheep: the allusion, in mentioning the sheep, is that the servant trains himself to the constancy of stillness and the beauty of character; for the ewe is docile to whoever rules over it — neither by its cry does it wound, nor otherwise. Such is the way of one who treads this carpet. The gentleness of the beast becomes a parable of the soul surrendered, peaceful beneath the hand of its Master.

mots.racine: ث م ن / ز و ج
ض أ ن / م ع ز
ن ب أ / ع ل م

versets.ar: وَمِنَ الْإِبِلِ اثْنَيْنِ وَمِنَ الْبَقَرِ اثْنَيْنِ ۗ قُلْ آلذَّكَرَيْنِ حَرَّمَ أَمِ الْأُنثَيَيْنِ أَمَّا اشْتَمَلَتْ عَلَيْهِ أَرْحَامُ الْأُنثَيَيْنِ ۖ أَمْ كُنتُمْ شُهَدَاءَ إِذْ وَصَّاكُمُ اللَّهُ بِهَٰذَا ۚ فَمَنْ أَظْلَمُ مِمَّنِ افْتَرَىٰ عَلَى اللَّهِ كَذِبًا لِّيُضِلَّ النَّاسَ بِغَيْرِ عِلْمٍ ۗ إِنَّ اللَّهَ لَا يَهْدِي الْقَوْمَ الظَّالِمِينَ
versets.tr: wa-mina l-ibili thnayni wa-mina l-baqari thnayni qul ā-dh-dhakarayni ḥarrama ami l-unthayayni ammā shtamalat ʿalayhi arḥāmu l-unthayayni am kuntum shuhadāʾa idh waṣṣākumu llāhu bi-hādhā fa-man aẓlamu mimmani ftarā ʿalā llāhi kadhiban li-yuḍilla n-nāsa bi-ghayri ʿilmin inna llāha lā yahdī l-qawma ẓ-ẓālimīn
versets.trad: And of the camels two, and of the cattle two. Say: “Is it the two males He has forbidden, or the two females, or what the wombs of the two females contain? Or were you witnesses when God enjoined this upon you?” Who then is more unjust than one who forges a lie against God to lead men astray without knowledge? Truly God does not guide the unjust people.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī reads in the camel other virtues: among the signs in the camelids: its docility to whoever pulls its bridle, its kneeling where it is made to kneel without dispute or choice; its acceptance of the load, its patience in thirst, its endurance that consumes it in the march. These beasts school one in surrender; whereas to forge prohibitions upon God to lead men astray is the injustice that excludes from guidance.

mots.racine: أ ب ل / ب ق ر
ض ل ل / ع ل م
mots.sens: to lead men astray without knowledge

versets.ar: قُل لَّا أَجِدُ فِي مَا أُوحِيَ إِلَيَّ مُحَرَّمًا عَلَىٰ طَاعِمٍ يَطْعَمُهُ إِلَّا أَن يَكُونَ مَيْتَةً أَوْ دَمًا مَّسْفُوحًا أَوْ لَحْمَ خِنزِيرٍ فَإِنَّهُ رِجْسٌ أَوْ فِسْقًا أُهِلَّ لِغَيْرِ اللَّهِ بِهِ ۚ فَمَنِ اضْطُرَّ غَيْرَ بَاغٍ وَلَا عَادٍ فَإِنَّ رَبَّكَ غَفُورٌ رَّحِيمٌ
versets.tr: qul lā ajidu fī mā ūḥiya ilayya muḥarraman ʿalā ṭāʿimin yaṭʿamuhu illā an yakūna maytatan aw daman masfūḥan aw laḥma khinzīrin fa-innahu rijsun aw fisqan uhilla li-ghayri llāhi bihi fa-mani ḍṭurra ghayra bāghin wa-lā ʿādin fa-inna rabbaka ghafūrun raḥīm
versets.trad: Say: “I do not find, in what is revealed to me, anything forbidden to one who would eat it, except carrion, or blood poured forth, or the flesh of swine — for that is a defilement — or a profane offering dedicated to other than God. But whoever is compelled, without rebellion or transgression — your Lord is forgiving, compassionate.”
themes: the lifting of the prohibition by necessity
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: He has shown that the Legislator is God, that the One who forbids to the creatures is God, and that what comes from other than God is null and void with God. He has shown too that, when constraint (iḍṭirār) arrives, the verdict of free choice is effaced. Real necessity suspends the prohibition: mercy (ghafūr, raḥīm) accompanies the rigor of the law.

mots.racine: ح ر م / ط ع م
mots.sens: forbidden to one who would eat

mots.racine: م و ت / د م و / س ف ح
ض ر ر / ب غ ي
mots.def: uḍṭurra: to be forced; bāghin: transgressor.

versets.ar: وَعَلَى الَّذِينَ هَادُوا حَرَّمْنَا كُلَّ ذِي ظُفُرٍ ۖ وَمِنَ الْبَقَرِ وَالْغَنَمِ حَرَّمْنَا عَلَيْهِمْ شُحُومَهُمَا إِلَّا مَا حَمَلَتْ ظُهُورُهُمَا أَوِ الْحَوَايَا أَوْ مَا اخْتَلَطَ بِعَظْمٍ ۚ ذَٰلِكَ جَزَيْنَاهُم بِبَغْيِهِمْ ۖ وَإِنَّا لَصَادِقُونَ
versets.tr: wa-ʿalā lladhīna hādū ḥarramnā kulla dhī ẓufurin wa-mina l-baqari wa-l-ghanami ḥarramnā ʿalayhim shuḥūmahumā illā mā ḥamalat ẓuhūruhumā awi l-ḥawāyā aw mā khtalaṭa bi-ʿaẓmin dhālika jazaynāhum bi-baghyihim wa-innā la-ṣādiqūn
versets.trad: And to those who practiced Judaism We forbade every animal with claws; and of the cattle and the sheep We forbade them their fat, except what their backs bear, or the entrails, or what is mixed with bone. Thus did We requite them for their rebellion; and We are truthful.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: He shows that what was forbidden to them they let go to waste: for, not chastising them on the spot, they did not perceive His immense ruse in what they had innovated of their own accord; they neglected it, did not observe it, and thus deserved the great weight of the burden and the painful separation. The prohibitions imposed “for their rebellion” (baghy) are chastisement and pedagogy at once.

mots.racine: ح ر م / ش ح م
ج ز ي / ب غ ي
mots.sens: We requited them for their rebellion

versets.ar: فَإِن كَذَّبُوكَ فَقُل رَّبُّكُمْ ذُو رَحْمَةٍ وَاسِعَةٍ وَلَا يُرَدُّ بَأْسُهُ عَنِ الْقَوْمِ الْمُجْرِمِينَ
versets.tr: fa-in kadhdhabūka fa-qul rabbukum dhū raḥmatin wāsiʿatin wa-lā yuraddu baʾsuhu ʿani l-qawmi l-mujrimīn
versets.trad: If they deny you, then say: “Your Lord is the Possessor of vast mercy, but His might shall not be turned away from the criminal people.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the allusion is to the distinction of the friends by mercy, and of the enemies by rejection and curse. The human form gathers them all, but the eternal apportionment separates them. The “vast” mercy (wāsiʿa) embraces, but does not suspend the justice: the same human face, opposite destinies, according to the secret of election.

mots.racine: ر ح م / و س ع
ر د د / ب أ س
mots.sens: His might is not turned away

versets.ar: سَيَقُولُ الَّذِينَ أَشْرَكُوا لَوْ شَاءَ اللَّهُ مَا أَشْرَكْنَا وَلَا آبَاؤُنَا وَلَا حَرَّمْنَا مِن شَيْءٍ ۚ كَذَٰلِكَ كَذَّبَ الَّذِينَ مِن قَبْلِهِمْ حَتَّىٰ ذَاقُوا بَأْسَنَا ۗ قُلْ هَلْ عِندَكُم مِّنْ عِلْمٍ فَتُخْرِجُوهُ لَنَا ۖ إِن تَتَّبِعُونَ إِلَّا الظَّنَّ وَإِنْ أَنتُمْ إِلَّا تَخْرُصُونَ
versets.tr: sa-yaqūlu lladhīna ashrakū law shāʾa llāhu mā ashraknā wa-lā ābāʾunā wa-lā ḥarramnā min shayʾin kadhālika kadhdhaba lladhīna min qablihim ḥattā dhāqū baʾsanā qul hal ʿindakum min ʿilmin fa-tukhrijūhu lanā in tattabiʿūna illā ẓ-ẓanna wa-in antum illā takhruṣūn
versets.trad: Those who associated will say: “Had God willed, neither we nor our fathers would have associated, nor would we have forbidden anything.” Thus did those before them deny, until they tasted Our might. Say: “Do you have any knowledge to bring forth for us? You follow only conjecture, and you only guess.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: their saying was a lie, for it did not issue from a sincere faith; therefore they were blamed for their ignorance, even though their statement was — at bottom — true (the decree does exist). A subtle distinction: to invoke predestination to excuse shirk is not to speak the truth but to use it as an alibi of the soul — conjecture (ẓann) disguised as theology.

mots.racine: ش ي أ / ش ر ك
mots.sens: had God willed, we would not have associated

mots.racine: ذ و ق / ب أ س
ت ب ع / ظ ن ن

versets.ar: قُلْ فَلِلَّهِ الْحُجَّةُ الْبَالِغَةُ ۖ فَلَوْ شَاءَ لَهَدَاكُمْ أَجْمَعِينَ
versets.tr: qul fa-li-llāhi l-ḥujjatu l-bālighatu fa-law shāʾa la-hadākum ajmaʿīn
versets.trad: Say: “To God belongs the decisive argument. Had He willed, He would have guided you all.”
themes: the will that could have guided all
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī, in a single stroke: He proclaims that His will never falls short of an object willed, and that no contestation rises against Him. The ḥujja bāligha — the proof that reaches its goal — belongs to God alone: had He willed universal guidance, it would have been; that not all are guided belongs to the mystery of His will, not to any incapacity.

mots.racine: ح ج ج / ب ل غ
ش ي أ / ه د ي
mots.sens: had He willed, He would have guided you

versets.ar: قُلْ هَلُمَّ شُهَدَاءَكُمُ الَّذِينَ يَشْهَدُونَ أَنَّ اللَّهَ حَرَّمَ هَٰذَا ۖ فَإِن شَهِدُوا فَلَا تَشْهَدْ مَعَهُمْ ۚ وَلَا تَتَّبِعْ أَهْوَاءَ الَّذِينَ كَذَّبُوا بِآيَاتِنَا وَالَّذِينَ لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ بِالْآخِرَةِ وَهُم بِرَبِّهِمْ يَعْدِلُونَ
versets.tr: qul halumma shuhadāʾakumu lladhīna yashhadūna anna llāha ḥarrama hādhā fa-in shahidū fa-lā tashhad maʿahum wa-lā tattabiʿ ahwāʾa lladhīna kadhdhabū bi-āyātinā wa-lladhīna lā yuʾminūna bi-l-ākhirati wa-hum bi-rabbihim yaʿdilūn
versets.trad: Say: “Bring forth your witnesses who testify that God forbade this!” If they testify, do not testify with them. And do not follow the desires of those who deny Our signs, and those who do not believe in the hereafter while they set up equals to their Lord.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: it indicates that whoever comes forth devoid of a proof that articulates itself and a demonstration that lights itself up is not accepted in what he advances. To demand “witnesses” one knows to be unfindable is to unmask the emptiness of their law: truth is established upon evidence, and false testimony is not espoused.

mots.sens: they set up equals to their Lord
mots.def: ʿadala bi-: to set up an equal.

versets.ar: قُلْ تَعَالَوْا أَتْلُ مَا حَرَّمَ رَبُّكُمْ عَلَيْكُمْ ۖ أَلَّا تُشْرِكُوا بِهِ شَيْئًا ۖ وَبِالْوَالِدَيْنِ إِحْسَانًا ۖ وَلَا تَقْتُلُوا أَوْلَادَكُم مِّنْ إِمْلَاقٍ ۖ نَّحْنُ نَرْزُقُكُمْ وَإِيَّاهُمْ ۖ وَلَا تَقْرَبُوا الْفَوَاحِشَ مَا ظَهَرَ مِنْهَا وَمَا بَطَنَ ۖ وَلَا تَقْتُلُوا النَّفْسَ الَّتِي حَرَّمَ اللَّهُ إِلَّا بِالْحَقِّ ۚ ذَٰلِكُمْ وَصَّاكُم بِهِ لَعَلَّكُمْ تَعْقِلُونَ
versets.tr: qul taʿālaw atlu mā ḥarrama rabbukum ʿalaykum allā tushrikū bihi shayʾan wa-bi-l-wālidayni iḥsānan wa-lā taqtulū awlādakum min imlāqin naḥnu narzuqukum wa-iyyāhum wa-lā taqrabū l-fawāḥisha mā ẓahara minhā wa-mā baṭana wa-lā taqtulū n-nafsa llatī ḥarrama llāhu illā bi-l-ḥaqqi dhālikum waṣṣākum bihi laʿallakum taʿqilūn
versets.trad: Say: “Come, I will recite what your Lord has forbidden you: that you associate nothing with Him; and toward parents, kindness; and do not kill your children out of poverty — We provide for you and for them; and do not approach indecencies, whether outward or inward; and do not kill the soul that God has made sacred, except by right.” This He has enjoined upon you, that you may understand.
versets.commentaire: First panel of the ten injunctions. Qushayrī: the first of these qualities is association, for it is the head of the prohibitions, the one with which no obedience is accepted; and it divides into manifest association — the worship of idols — and hidden association: to regard the creatures with the eye that ascribes to them the right to veneration. Subtle shirk — to revere the created as if it deserved worship — is the snare of refined souls.

mots.sens: that you associate nothing with Him

mots.racine: و ل د / ح س ن

mots.ar: الْفَوَاحِشَ مَا ظَهَرَ مِنْهَا وَمَا بَطَنَ
mots.tr: al-fawāḥisha mā ẓahara minhā wa-mā baṭana
mots.racine: ف ح ش / ظ ه ر / ب ط ن

versets.ar: وَلَا تَقْرَبُوا مَالَ الْيَتِيمِ إِلَّا بِالَّتِي هِيَ أَحْسَنُ حَتَّىٰ يَبْلُغَ أَشُدَّهُ ۖ وَأَوْفُوا الْكَيْلَ وَالْمِيزَانَ بِالْقِسْطِ ۖ لَا نُكَلِّفُ نَفْسًا إِلَّا وُسْعَهَا ۖ وَإِذَا قُلْتُمْ فَاعْدِلُوا وَلَوْ كَانَ ذَا قُرْبَىٰ ۖ وَبِعَهْدِ اللَّهِ أَوْفُوا ۚ ذَٰلِكُمْ وَصَّاكُم بِهِ لَعَلَّكُمْ تَذَكَّرُونَ
versets.tr: wa-lā taqrabū māla l-yatīmi illā bi-llatī hiya aḥsanu ḥattā yablugha ashuddahu wa-awfū l-kayla wa-l-mīzāna bi-l-qisṭi lā nukallifu nafsan illā wusʿahā wa-idhā qultum fa-ʿdilū wa-law kāna dhā qurbā wa-bi-ʿahdi llāhi awfū dhālikum waṣṣākum bihi laʿallakum tadhakkarūn
versets.trad: And do not approach the wealth of the orphan except in the best way, until he reaches maturity. And give full measure and weight in justice. We charge no soul beyond its capacity. And when you speak, be just, even if it be a near relative. And fulfill the covenant of God. This He has enjoined upon you, that you may remember.
versets.commentaire: Second panel of the decalogue. Qushayrī sums up the whole: respect for the orphan's wealth and the gaze cast upon him with the eye of honor; then equity in transactions and the prevention of every encumbrance; then truthfulness in speech and justice in act; then the following of the path according to what the gleams of proof indicate. The “covenant of God” (ʿahd Allāh) crowns these commandments: fidelity to the primordial pledge.

mots.racine: ي ت م / ح س ن
mots.sens: the orphan's wealth, in the best way

mots.racine: و ف ي / ك ي ل / و ز ن / ق س ط
