mots.sens: give full measure and weight in equity

mots.racine: ق و ل / ع د ل
ع ه د / و ف ي

versets.ar: وَأَنَّ هَٰذَا صِرَاطِي مُسْتَقِيمًا فَاتَّبِعُوهُ ۖ وَلَا تَتَّبِعُوا السُّبُلَ فَتَفَرَّقَ بِكُمْ عَن سَبِيلِهِ ۚ ذَٰلِكُمْ وَصَّاكُم بِهِ لَعَلَّكُمْ تَتَّقُونَ
versets.tr: wa-anna hādhā ṣirāṭī mustaqīman fa-ttabiʿūhu wa-lā tattabiʿū s-subula fa-tafarraqa bikum ʿan sabīlihi dhālikum waṣṣākum bihi laʿallakum tattaqūn
versets.trad: And: This is My straight path, so follow it; and do not follow other ways, lest they scatter you from His way. This He has enjoined upon you, that you may be godfearing.
versets.commentaire: The straight path (ṣirāṭ mustaqīm) is one, the byways of straying are countless. In the spirit of Qushayrī, the “other ways” (subul) are the manifold leanings of the soul that fragment the orientation: one single path gathers the heart toward the One, while dispersion (tafarruq) tears it from its polarity. Reverential fear (taqwā) is the fruit of this unification.

mots.racine: ص ر ط / ق و م
ت ب ع / س ب ل

versets.ar: ثُمَّ آتَيْنَا مُوسَى الْكِتَابَ تَمَامًا عَلَى الَّذِي أَحْسَنَ وَتَفْصِيلًا لِّكُلِّ شَيْءٍ وَهُدًى وَرَحْمَةً لَّعَلَّهُم بِلِقَاءِ رَبِّهِمْ يُؤْمِنُونَ
versets.tr: thumma ātaynā mūsā l-kitāba tamāman ʿalā lladhī aḥsana wa-tafṣīlan li-kulli shayʾin wa-hudan wa-raḥmatan laʿallahum bi-liqāʾi rabbihim yuʾminūn
versets.trad: Then We gave Moses the Book, complete for the one who does good, and a detailing of all things, a guidance and a mercy, that they might believe in the meeting with their Lord.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: He lightens for them the hardship of bearing the obligations by recalling that those who preceded them were, in weakness and impotence, like us; then they were patient and triumphed, they made themselves pure and were saved. The Book of Moses, “a detailing of all things” (tafṣīl li-kulli shayʾ), prepares for faith in the meeting (liqāʾ) with the Lord — the term of all guidance.

mots.racine: ت م م / ح س ن
mots.sens: complete for the one who does good
mots.def: tamām: completion; aḥsana: to do good.

mots.racine: ل ق ي / ر ب ب

versets.ar: وَهَٰذَا كِتَابٌ أَنزَلْنَاهُ مُبَارَكٌ فَاتَّبِعُوهُ وَاتَّقُوا لَعَلَّكُمْ تُرْحَمُونَ
versets.tr: wa-hādhā kitābun anzalnāhu mubārakun fa-ttabiʿūhu wa-ttaqū laʿallakum turḥamūn
versets.trad: And this is a blessed Book We have sent down, so follow it and be godfearing, that you may receive mercy.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī gives here a pearl on the spiritual audition of the Qurʾān: the descent of the Book upon them is the fulfillment of the gift; and when the servant remains short of hearing the direct Address, he consoles himself by the reading of the Book. Whoever does not find in the recitation of the Qurʾān the fullness of life and intimacy, it is because he reads by formal imitation, not by true realization. The Qurʾān is the letter of the Beloved: one tastes its savor only from a present heart.

mots.racine: ك ت ب / ب ر ك

versets.ar: أَن تَقُولُوا إِنَّمَا أُنزِلَ الْكِتَابُ عَلَىٰ طَائِفَتَيْنِ مِن قَبْلِنَا وَإِن كُنَّا عَن دِرَاسَتِهِمْ لَغَافِلِينَ
versets.tr: an taqūlū innamā unzila l-kitābu ʿalā ṭāʾifatayni min qablinā wa-in kunnā ʿan dirāsatihim la-ghāfilīn
versets.trad: Lest you should say: “The Book was sent down only upon two communities before us, and we were unaware of their study.”
versets.commentaire: The Book is sent down to remove every alibi. Qushayrī (on vv.156-157): He has set aside every pretext and made every joining appear, so that there remains for you no excuse, no recourse in the folds of the alibi. None will be able to say that the revelation was reserved to other peoples: the Word is now accessible, and ignorance is no longer a defense.

mots.racine: د ر س / غ ف ل

versets.ar: أَوْ تَقُولُوا لَوْ أَنَّا أُنزِلَ عَلَيْنَا الْكِتَابُ لَكُنَّا أَهْدَىٰ مِنْهُمْ ۚ فَقَدْ جَاءَكُم بَيِّنَةٌ مِّن رَّبِّكُمْ وَهُدًى وَرَحْمَةٌ ۚ فَمَنْ أَظْلَمُ مِمَّن كَذَّبَ بِآيَاتِ اللَّهِ وَصَدَفَ عَنْهَا ۗ سَنَجْزِي الَّذِينَ يَصْدِفُونَ عَنْ آيَاتِنَا سُوءَ الْعَذَابِ بِمَا كَانُوا يَصْدِفُونَ
versets.tr: aw taqūlū law annā unzila ʿalaynā l-kitābu la-kunnā ahdā minhum fa-qad jāʾakum bayyinatun min rabbikum wa-hudan wa-raḥmatun fa-man aẓlamu mimman kadhdhaba bi-āyāti llāhi wa-ṣadafa ʿanhā sa-najzī lladhīna yaṣdifūna ʿan āyātinā sūʾa l-ʿadhābi bi-mā kānū yaṣdifūn
versets.trad: Or lest you should say: “Had the Book been sent down upon us, we would have been better guided than they.” Now there has come to you a clear proof from your Lord, a guidance and a mercy. Who then is more unjust than one who denies the signs of God and turns away from them? We shall requite those who turn away from Our signs with an evil chastisement, for their turning away.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the sanction of every crime is deferred; but the sanction of denial is hastened: it is their maintenance in the prison of doubt, where their heart settles upon nothing. The denial of the signs (āyāt) bears within itself its first chastisement: the disquiet of the heart that finds no resting place, wandering from doubt to doubt.

mots.sens: we would be better guided than they

mots.racine: ب ي ن / ه د ي / ر ح م
mots.sens: a proof, a guidance and a mercy

mots.racine: ك ذ ب / ص د ف
mots.sens: he denied and turned away from them

versets.ar: هَلْ يَنظُرُونَ إِلَّا أَن تَأْتِيَهُمُ الْمَلَائِكَةُ أَوْ يَأْتِيَ رَبُّكَ أَوْ يَأْتِيَ بَعْضُ آيَاتِ رَبِّكَ ۗ يَوْمَ يَأْتِي بَعْضُ آيَاتِ رَبِّكَ لَا يَنفَعُ نَفْسًا إِيمَانُهَا لَمْ تَكُنْ آمَنَتْ مِن قَبْلُ أَوْ كَسَبَتْ فِي إِيمَانِهَا خَيْرًا ۗ قُلِ انتَظِرُوا إِنَّا مُنتَظِرُونَ
versets.tr: hal yanẓurūna illā an taʾtiyahumu l-malāʾikatu aw yaʾtiya rabbuka aw yaʾtiya baʿḍu āyāti rabbika yawma yaʾtī baʿḍu āyāti rabbika lā yanfaʿu nafsan īmānuhā lam takun āmanat min qablu aw kasabat fī īmānihā khayran quli ntaẓirū innā muntaẓirūn
versets.trad: Do they await only that the angels come to them, or that your Lord come, or that some sign of your Lord come? On the day some sign of your Lord comes, faith will not profit a soul that had not believed before, or earned good in its faith. Say: “Wait! We too are waiting.”
themes: the vain awaiting of the sign
the faith that comes too late
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: after removing from them every pretext, they demanded what was not theirs, and deluded themselves by the length of their impunity; but when He decides the sanction of a servant, none opposes His decree, none contradicts His governance. The ultimate “sign,” when it appears, will close the time of choice: faith wrenched out by the constraint of unveiling is worth nothing more.

mots.racine: أ ت ي / م ل ك
mots.sens: that the angels come to them

mots.racine: ن ف ع / أ م ن
mots.sens: faith will not profit a soul
mots.def: faith come too late is worthless.

versets.ar: إِنَّ الَّذِينَ فَرَّقُوا دِينَهُمْ وَكَانُوا شِيَعًا لَّسْتَ مِنْهُمْ فِي شَيْءٍ ۚ إِنَّمَا أَمْرُهُمْ إِلَى اللَّهِ ثُمَّ يُنَبِّئُهُم بِمَا كَانُوا يَفْعَلُونَ
versets.tr: inna lladhīna farraqū dīnahum wa-kānū shiyaʿan lasta minhum fī shayʾin innamā amruhum ilā llāhi thumma yunabbiʾuhum bi-mā kānū yafʿalūn
versets.trad: As for those who have divided their religion and become sects, you have nothing to do with them. Their affair rests with God; then He will inform them of what they used to do.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: they agreed by their bodies and divided by their hearts: gathered openly, separated — in reality — secret against secret. And on “you have nothing to do with them”: We will not gather you with them: your camp is that of the realities, theirs that of falsehood — and there is no gathering of opposites. To fragment religion (tafrīq al-dīn) is to dissolve the unity of the path.

mots.racine: ف ر ق / د ي ن

mots.sens: you are in no way of them

versets.ar: مَن جَاءَ بِالْحَسَنَةِ فَلَهُ عَشْرُ أَمْثَالِهَا ۖ وَمَن جَاءَ بِالسَّيِّئَةِ فَلَا يُجْزَىٰ إِلَّا مِثْلَهَا وَهُمْ لَا يُظْلَمُونَ
versets.tr: man jāʾa bi-l-ḥasanati fa-lahu ʿashru amthālihā wa-man jāʾa bi-s-sayyiʾati fa-lā yujzā illā mithlahā wa-hum lā yuẓlamūn
versets.trad: Whoever brings a good deed shall have ten times its like; and whoever brings an evil deed shall be requited only with its like; and they shall not be wronged.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī unfolds a ladder of iḥsān: these are the good deeds of the apparent; as for the good deeds of the hearts, to the One they are worth a hundred, up to multiples without number. And this pearl on the gratuity of grace: the good deed proceeds from His favor and is obtained by His gentleness: He makes it come to be, then accepts and praises it, then rewards and gives — the toil is from you, His act; the reward is for you, His grace. And again: to the ascetics and worshippers, a reward measured and counted; to the people of ecstasy, a Meeting neither cut off nor refused.

mots.racine: ح س ن / ع ش ر / م ث ل
mots.sens: a good deed: ten times its like

mots.racine: س و أ / م ث ل
mots.sens: an evil deed: only its like

versets.ar: قُلْ إِنَّنِي هَدَانِي رَبِّي إِلَىٰ صِرَاطٍ مُّسْتَقِيمٍ دِينًا قِيَمًا مِّلَّةَ إِبْرَاهِيمَ حَنِيفًا ۚ وَمَا كَانَ مِنَ الْمُشْرِكِينَ
versets.tr: qul innanī hadānī rabbī ilā ṣirāṭin mustaqīmin dīnan qiyaman millata ibrāhīma ḥanīfan wa-mā kāna mina l-mushrikīn
versets.trad: Say: “Truly my Lord has guided me to a straight path, an upright religion, the creed of Abraham, a pure monotheist; and he was not among the associators.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: He oriented him to the true road; and orientation to it is achieved only by the obstruction of all ways toward another than Him. Whoever finds a way to a created being wanders in the homelands of illusion, for the others have no parcel of creative power; whoever takes a way to a creature, builds upon them a hope or sets upon them a reliance, has harbored a delusion and swallowed a leading-astray. And he defines: the straight path is to see none but Him establish even an atom or an instant; the ḥanīf is the one who inclines toward the Real, swerves from the false, turns from the contrary of Reality.

mots.racine: ه د ي / ص ر ط
mots.sens: my Lord guided me to a straight path

mots.racine: د ي ن / ق و م
م ل ل / ح ن ف
mots.sens: the creed of Abraham, a pure monotheist
mots.def: milla: way, community; ḥanīf: primordial monotheist.

versets.ar: قُلْ إِنَّ صَلَاتِي وَنُسُكِي وَمَحْيَايَ وَمَمَاتِي لِلَّهِ رَبِّ الْعَالَمِينَ
versets.tr: qul inna ṣalātī wa-nusukī wa-maḥyāya wa-mamātī li-llāhi rabbi l-ʿālamīn
versets.trad: Say: “Truly my prayer, my devotion, my life and my death are for God, the Lord of the worlds;
versets.commentaire: The verse of total surrender. Qushayrī: the one unveiled to the realities of tawḥīd contemplates that the One who maintains him, who acts in him, who holds him and transports him from attribute to attribute, who passes over him the waves of becoming — is One, without an associate to share Him, Glorious without a peer to rival Him. To offer prayer, worship, life and death to God is to have no part reserved for oneself any longer.

mots.racine: ص ل و / ن س ك
ح ي ي / م و ت
ر ب ب / ع ل م
mots.sens: for God, Lord of the worlds

versets.ar: لَا شَرِيكَ لَهُ ۖ وَبِذَٰلِكَ أُمِرْتُ وَأَنَا أَوَّلُ الْمُسْلِمِينَ
versets.tr: lā sharīka lahu wa-bi-dhālika umirtu wa-anā awwalu l-muslimīn
versets.trad: He has no associate. With that I am commanded, and I am the first of those who submit.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: whoever knows that he is by God knows that he is for God; and when he knows that he is for God, there remains in him no part for any other than God: he is then surrendered to the decree of God, not opposing His ruling, not contradicting His choice, not turning from embracing His command. To profess “no associate” is not merely a doctrine: it is the total dispossession of self into the hands of the One.

mots.sens: the first of those who submit

versets.ar: قُلْ أَغَيْرَ اللَّهِ أَبْغِي رَبًّا وَهُوَ رَبُّ كُلِّ شَيْءٍ ۚ وَلَا تَكْسِبُ كُلُّ نَفْسٍ إِلَّا عَلَيْهَا ۚ وَلَا تَزِرُ وَازِرَةٌ وِزْرَ أُخْرَىٰ ۚ ثُمَّ إِلَىٰ رَبِّكُم مَّرْجِعُكُمْ فَيُنَبِّئُكُم بِمَا كُنتُمْ فِيهِ تَخْتَلِفُونَ
versets.tr: qul a-ghayra llāhi abghī rabban wa-huwa rabbu kulli shayʾin wa-lā taksibu kullu nafsin illā ʿalayhā wa-lā taziru wāziratun wizra ukhrā thumma ilā rabbikum marjiʿukum fa-yunabbiʾukum bi-mā kuntum fīhi takhtalifūn
versets.trad: Say: “Shall I seek a Lord other than God, when He is the Lord of all things? No soul earns except against itself, and no bearer bears the burden of another. Then to your Lord is your return, and He will inform you of that wherein you differed.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī raises the avowal to the vertigo of unified vision: how should I prefer to Him a substitute, when I find outside His decree no recourse? how should I speak of an other, a contrary, an associate? if I look to the right, I contemplate only His kingdom; if I look to the left, I see only His kingdom — nay: if I look to the right, I contemplate His felicity (yumn), and to the left, I find His ease (yusr) coming toward me. And no soul bears the burden of another: each stands alone before the return.

mots.racine: ب غ ي / ر ب ب
mots.sens: shall I seek a Lord other than God

mots.ar: وَلَا تَكْسِبُ كُلُّ نَفْسٍ إِلَّا عَلَيْهَا
mots.tr: wa-lā taksibu kullu nafsin illā ʿalayhā
mots.racine: ك س ب / ن ف س
mots.sens: no soul earns except against itself
mots.def: kasaba: to earn, to be responsible.

mots.sens: no bearer bears the burden of another
mots.def: wazara: to carry a load; wizr: burden.

versets.ar: وَهُوَ الَّذِي جَعَلَكُمْ خَلَائِفَ الْأَرْضِ وَرَفَعَ بَعْضَكُمْ فَوْقَ بَعْضٍ دَرَجَاتٍ لِّيَبْلُوَكُمْ فِي مَا آتَاكُمْ ۗ إِنَّ رَبَّكَ سَرِيعُ الْعِقَابِ وَإِنَّهُ لَغَفُورٌ رَّحِيمٌ
versets.tr: wa-huwa lladhī jaʿalakum khalāʾifa l-arḍi wa-rafaʿa baʿḍakum fawqa baʿḍin darajātin li-yabluwakum fī mā ātākum inna rabbaka sarīʿu l-ʿiqābi wa-innahu la-ghafūrun raḥīm
versets.trad: And it is He who made you successors on the earth, and raised some of you above others in degrees, that He might test you in what He has given you. Truly your Lord is swift in chastisement, and truly He is forgiving, compassionate.
versets.commentaire: Seal of the sūra. Qushayrī: He has committed the turning-back (tawba) to you, and restricted to you the verdict of your time: it is you who are intended today, to the exclusion of any other. Then He made you of diverse conditions, created you unequal: one is served, cherished, eased, and many people toil for him; another is tried, weighed down with fatigue, his head set spinning. The trial has come to test you in what He has given you: His reckoning overtakes you, His decree precedes you. And the sūra is sealed, as it had opened, upon praise — here the balance of the two divine hands: swift in chastisement, and yet forgiving and compassionate.

mots.racine: خ ل ف / أ ر ض
ر ف ع / د ر ج
mots.sens: He raised some above others in degrees

mots.racine: ب ل و / أ ت ي
mots.sens: that He might test you in what He gave you
mots.def: balā: to put to the test.

mots.racine: س ر ع / ع ق ب / غ ف ر / ر ح م
